summary: Design MORE factions / zones / areas / NPC clusters for Kamikakushi beyond the 3 starters — primarily the upper influence tiers (region/domain/Edo) + cross-cutting networks — each itself incremental. Parallel facets → curate/synthesize → adversarial verify.

expanded_world.expanded_world_summary:
CURATED EXPANDED WORLD — beyond the 3 starters (Estate ladder / Asagiri reputation-web / Sawatari-juku origin support track). The four facet drafts deliberately overlapped (the cross-cutting facet re-covered the porter guild, broker/finance, racket, pilgrimage, and artisan lineage that the T3/T4/T5 facets also covered). I dedupe by treating SIX of these as CROSS-CUTTING NETWORKS that the player picks up minimal at a low tier and re-meets, larger, at every higher tier — and treating the rest as TIER-LOCAL clusters that bloom at one tier. This collapses ~37 raw facet entries into 18 curated nodes (6 cross-cutting networks + 12 tier-local clusters), losing nothing of value while removing duplication.

THE GOVERNING SHAPE. Everything obeys three locked rules. (1) FRACTAL INCREMENTALITY: every node arrives as ONE contact / ONE place / ONE verb, then teaches its people and systems as story beats — the whole-game UI-reveal motion repeated in miniature. (2) THREE DISTINCT SHAPES preserved from the starters so the world never reads as 'one Influence bar painted 18 colours': route/producer networks (Porter Guild, charcoal, Kuzuhara works), per-node patron WEBS (Neighbouring Valleys, Merchant-Finance, Artisan Lineage, Nihonbashi wholesale), discrete MILESTONE tracks (Pilgrimage, Scholars, Marriage/Adoption, sekisho pass-tiers, the Edo conduits), and ONE evidence/petition META-track (the Racket — friction, not a producer). (3) SPINE vs SIDE is rigorously audited so a spine-only player has a thin-but-complete path to T5 and ALL depth is genuinely optional.

THE SPINE THROUGH-LINE (what a minimal player touches, never more): T3 = a tradeable surplus + a thin logistics+pass backbone to move it + a broker to convert it + the Kuzuhara embankment as the headline distinguished-service beat. T4 = a castle-town townhouse beachhead + officialdom that RECORDS achievements + debt restructured into creditworthiness + the meibutsu graded toward Osaka. T5 = the lord's sankin-kotai outfitting + the domain Edo yashiki correspondence + the meibutsu reaching Edo markets + the touring-inspector set-piece + a mediated merit commendation flowing DOWN through the lord. Everything else — courting valleys, the pilgrimage circuit, the matagi, the bandit investigation, the artisan masters, the scholars, the marriage brokerage, the entertainment district's deeper layers, the full racket prosecution — is an optional accelerant/colour tuned inside the locked ~halves-time-to-tier budget.

THE ONE LOAD-BEARING ANTAGONIST WEB. The Rice-Quota & Smuggling Racket is the single grounded antagonist spanning all tiers, already half-built in canon (Magobei skim → Yagoemon's doctored ledgers → Tedai Kuroiwa → Guildmaster Zenroku's contraband, enforced by Hanzaki), extended UP to a domain daikan/magistrate (T4) and an untouchable Edo factor (T5). It is structurally woven into the spine (its quota machine is the same rotten root under the estate's debt, the village's grief, Kuzuhara's drowning, and Tahei's written-off caravan) yet defeated by EVIDENCE and economic daylight, never required combat — and its side-mysteries never hard-block the climb. While active it is the chief DIEGETIC DRAG on the economy, so dismantling reachable rungs feeds the spine by REMOVING friction (cheaper trade, safer roads, cleaner finance) as much as by adding Influence. Justice stays deliberately PARTIAL: the apex magistrate/Edo factor largely escapes, honouring the ceiling.

RIVAL HOUSE TOMITA is the single inter-house STATUS rival woven T3→T5 (one entry, not three): contests valleys (T3), broker/guild terms and samurai-society standing (T4), and stages its own display at the touring-inspector set-piece (T5). Always out-maneuvered commercially/socially, never killed; its edge is capital+connections+ruthlessness, all matchable by work (binds the talent-foil rule).

DEDUPE/MERGE DECISIONS (load-bearing): (a) Porter Guild, Merchant-Finance, Racket, Pilgrimage, Artisan Lineage, Scholars/Medicine are ONE cross-cutting entry each, NOT separate T3/T4/T5 copies — the T3 'broker network', T4 Marutaya+Kuramae-quarter+wholesale-row, and T5 Osaka-Edo-finance-chain+Nihonbashi+banzuke all fold into the appropriate network's higher rungs. (b) The Sekisho/Tegata pass system and the Bandit/Ronin menace MERGE into the Porter Guild as its friction layer — they only exist to make logistics felt, and the bandit menace IS the racket's enforcer arm. (c) Two duplicate 'Onui' NPCs are split-named: the Hibara weaver stays Onui; the merchant heir is renamed Osato. (d) The 'regional shrine circuit' and 'pilgrimage order' are one Pilgrimage network. (e) The Townhouse and Entertainment-District (T4) merge into one hub; Samurai-Society and Marriage/Adoption-Brokerage merge into one T4 faction; Nihonbashi-wholesale and the banzuke-fame milieu merge into one T5 node (commercial + reputational faces of the one meibutsu outcome). GROUNDED/NO-MAGIC is held everywhere: every belief/omen resolves to a human/natural cause, the ≤1 residual-ambiguity cap stays at the Asagiri boundary-stone, and recovery aids/porter's-knot/dream confer ZERO mechanical edge (access/efficiency only).

factions_zones.name: The Kaido Porters' & Transport Guild (logistics network — with sekisho pass-tiers and route-risk friction)
factions_zones.tier: cross-tier: identity-seed T1 (porter's-knot, ZERO bonus); opens proper T3 via the origin (Denbei/Kanta); deepens T3 region → T4 Osaka rice shipping → T5 sankin-kotai freight to Edo
factions_zones.theme: The roads as the circulatory system of the rise — honest muscle, manifests, knots, safe-passage. The protagonist's OWN porter past, turned from the wound (the caravan that 'killed' him) into the supply chain that carries the house upward. Absorbs the sekisho/tegata pass system (the friction that makes access feel earned) and the bandit/ronin route-risk (which IS the racket's enforcer arm). GROUNDED: attested toiya porters/horses, sukego corvee, kaido freight, sekisho barriers + tegata permits, contraband on tax-rice routes; the 'one-eyed mountain god of the pass' = Hanzaki + lethal fog-blind terrain; the caravan 'curse' = the neglected embankment failing; no road-kami ever mechanically acts.
factions_zones.first_encounter: MINIMAL: a single broken porter's-knot beat at the Stables/Woodlot (T1) — 'huh, you've hauled before' (the locked zero-bonus identity clue). The network proper opens with ONE contact: friend Kanta runs the estate's FIRST consignment off the books as a favour — one route, one porter, one verb (dispatch a load). No guild UI yet, just a friend with a carrying-pole.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Kanta's favour run succeeds → the 'routes' panel appears with exactly ONE route (Asagiri→Sawatari-juku) and its risk/throughput/cost; Denbei gives a legitimate manifest. → First time goods or the protagonist try to pass the home sekisho they are turned back for lack of a tegata; the only verb is 'obtain a pass' (from estate standing) — a single-use pass opens one onward stage. → Contracting a SECOND porter reveals porter-roster management (load vs speed vs sukego availability); the first raid loss on the pass reveals the route-RISK layer (insurance/escort/reroute/toll) with Hanzaki's name as rumour. → Repeated lawful passage upgrades single-use→standing tegata (the protagonist), then cargo tegata (consignments unthrottled), then guarantor status (the barrier waves the house's mon through — a recognition beat). → Guild standing unlocks horse-strings, a formal guild contract (volume discount), and the sukego obligation as a lever (~2 porters+2 horses/100 koku — petition for relief, or help ORGANISE the district sukego, a recognition beat). → T4: a regional sub-master grants a freight contract to the castle-town and onward to Osaka boats; recruited Origin guild-mates become idle producer-carriers. → T5: goods ride the lord's sankin-kotai convoy to Edo. Each leg is a story beat (a flooded ford repaired, a pass earned, a relay allied).

key_npcs.name: Kanta
key_npcs.role: Existing origin NPC, wider role: first porter contact and human face of the guild; childhood-friend warmth as the on-ramp; recruitable lead carrier.

key_npcs.name: Master Denbei
key_npcs.role: Existing origin NPC, wider role: legitimate-manifests + route-knowledge ally; taught the porter's-knot — ZERO mechanical bonus, access only.

key_npcs.name: Guildmaster Zenroku
key_npcs.role: Existing origin antagonist, wider role: the guild's compromised apex; wrote Tahei off to hide contraband; reachable witness/culprit tying the road into the Racket (defeated by leverage/evidence).

key_npcs.name: Barrier-keeper Sobei
key_npcs.role: New: keeper of the home sekisho; turns you back, then over time waves the house's mon through — a living progress bar of recognized standing.

key_npcs.name: Sukego-tedai Mohei
key_npcs.role: New: minor official allocating assisting-village corvee porters; friction NPC to negotiate/petition, eventually help coordinate (recognition beat).

key_npcs.name: Hanzaki
key_npcs.role: Existing racket enforcer, shared with the Racket node: the 'one-eyed mountain god' making the pass route risky — survived by labour-built endurance, never out-fought.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A logistics layer over the producer economy: pack-routes are recurring auto-transports converting estate/origin surplus into delivered goods + a 'recognized delivery' Influence beat; each new leg adds a route producer with cost/throughput scaling; hired porters + Origin recruits are assignable idle helpers; pass-tiers (single→standing→cargo→guarantor tegata) unthrottle movement step by step; route-risk (insurance/escort/reroute/toll) and seasonal closures (snowed-in passes) give the damper texture; a late co-financed caravan line is a holdings node.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Trade ties (lever 7) + road works (lever 3): moving recognized surplus is the conversion step itself; a fulfilled acknowledged consignment, a formal guild contract, a guarantor-pass, helping organise the sukego, and the capstone meibutsu-reaches-Edo-via-sankin-kotai are explicit recognition beats. It is the practical bottleneck on T4 (no Osaka shipping without a logistics spine).
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE-ADJACENT in its THIN form (you cannot export surplus without some logistics + at least the protagonist's/cargo pass), but ALL DEPTH is optional accelerant (full roster mgmt, caravan line, guild co-direction, the bandit investigation, Origin guild-mate recruits). The pass-tiers are friction the spine naturally retires; the contested-checkpoint and racket-enforcer threads are optional and never block passage (always pay/wait/reroute). The Origin-emotional layer stays optional; the bare route is available via standing alone.

factions_zones.name: The Rice & Silver Network (merchant-finance: village broker → regional kuramoto → castle-town finance house → Osaka kakeya & Edo fudasashi)
factions_zones.kind: cross-cutting network (per-node patron web + finance milestone shape)
factions_zones.tier: cross-tier: seed T2 (Asagiri rice-broker) + T3 (Okimi's trading family); regional broker/warehouse T3; domain-finance house + rice-coupon settlement T4; Osaka kura-yashiki→Edo fudasashi chain T5
factions_zones.theme: Money is the lever that turns the strangling inherited debt into creditworthiness, and the runway that makes each tier AFFORDABLE. The legitimate counter-weight to the Racket: the same rice-and-silver economy run honestly (proper merchant finance, sealed contracts, rice-bills) instead of through quota-skimming. GROUNDED: attested nakagai middlemen, kuramoto warehouse masters, kakeya/goyo-shonin/fudasashi finance, rice-coupon (kome-kitte) settlement, kurayashiki, goyokin, the Osaka/Dojima exchange; the saturation-crash keeps it from being free money; the only 'mystery' is which silver is clean (honest network vs the Racket's dirty money), resolving to documents and people.
factions_zones.first_encounter: MINIMAL: ONE contact — sell the estate's first surplus lot at the village rice-broker / dry-goods shop (the existing per-shop patron meter). One node, one verb (sell). At T3 a travelling middleman (nakagai Heikichi) offers a lowball regional price for the first exportable surplus — one buyer, one sale, no paper yet.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Village broker patron standing → forward-contracts (lock a sell price, soften the saturation crash). → T3: the first lowball regional sale reveals a 'broker standing/price' meter and the regional market-saturation/price-crash. → standing unlocks a regional WAREHOUSE agent (Kuraya Jinbei) who STORES surplus for a fee and pays fairer prices → unlocks RICE-BILLS (kome-kitte: hold/trade/borrow against — credit appears) → diversifying goods (rice+cloth+charcoal+mountain goods) hedges the crash. → T4: a castle-town finance house (Marutaya Sobei) RESTRUCTURES the grandfather-era usurious loan into proper merchant/domain finance (prove honest books → present recorded yields as collateral → negotiate → service the new loan); once solvent the relationship inverts and Marutaya courts the house, opening the GOYOKIN lever and working capital. → T5: the Osaka kura-yashiki returns a rice-bill against shipped surplus; the kakeya auctions/advances; Edo fudasashi (Kuramae) discount the house's bills and bankroll its capital footprint on its own paper — a 'house credit' standing emerges. Each tier is ONE new financial verb (sell → forward-contract → store → paper → credit → restructure → goyokin → bill-discount).

key_npcs.name: Asagiri rice-broker / dry-goods merchant
key_npcs.role: Existing village per-shop node, wider role: first finance contact; forward-contracts and the on-ramp to credit.

key_npcs.name: Okimi
key_npcs.role: Existing origin NPC, wider role: Tahei's sister married into a trading family; routes goods, opens post-town/Osaka-facing finance — the §1.5.4 multiplier made concrete. (Shared with Wider Post-Town.)

key_npcs.name: Clerk Tanomo
key_npcs.role: Existing estate accountant, wider role: the in-house thread; works the ledgers WITH the player to convert liability into solvency and manage the rice-bill/debt-restructuring interface.

key_npcs.name: Nakagai Heikichi
key_npcs.role: New (T3): first regional broker & lowball buyer; on-ramp to the paper-economy; won over by reliable diversified volume.

key_npcs.name: Warehouse-agent Kuraya Jinbei
key_npcs.role: New (T3): regional kuramoto-style warehouse master; gates storage, fair pricing, rice-bills, credit; his sealed contract is a major recognition beat and the bridge toward Osaka.

key_npcs.name: Marutaya Sobei
key_npcs.role: New (T4): master of the domain-finance house; restructures the strangling debt, opens goyokin and working capital; merchant power that out-ranks samurai in fact but never in form.

key_npcs.name: Osato (Marutaya heir)
key_npcs.role: New (T4): Marutaya's sharp daughter who runs the back office; pragmatic ally valuing competence over birth, and a possible marriage-alliance candidate. (Renamed from the facet's duplicate 'Onui' to avoid collision with the Hibara weaver.)

key_npcs.name: Fudasashi Daikokuya Jube
key_npcs.role: New (T5): Edo rice-broker of Kuramae who discounts the house's bills; the first to treat the Kurosawa name as collateral worth having.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Finance sub-loops over the coin/koku economy: forward-contracts; regional market-saturation/price-crash; warehouse storage-for-fee; rice-bills (tradeable paper); credit/borrow-against-paper; debt restructuring (usury→merchant finance); goyokin (spend coin/koku for a standing bump); diversified-goods hedging; a creditworthiness meter that lowers borrowing cost and funds higher-tier projects (yashiki repair, sankin-kotai outfitting). Per-merchant patron standing mirrors the village shop meters.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: The primary CONVERSION engine: surplus only becomes Influence when the broker/market RECOGNIZES it (a recorded sale, a sealed contract, a held rice-bill, restructured debt = creditworthy; goyokin = formal standing; Osaka/Edo extending terms on the name = capital recognition). Hits levers 2/4/7 and underpins the T4 creditworthiness gate and the upper-tier affordability.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE in its THIN form (some broker is unavoidable to convert surplus and reach T4; the inherited debt must be serviced/restructured for T4 solvency — achievable minimally via the estate's own grind + Tanomo). DEPTH (paper, full credit, diversification, goyokin, the Osaka/Edo fudasashi grind) is optional accelerant. Which broker and how aggressively to diversify stay player-chosen.

factions_zones.name: The Rice-Quota & Smuggling Racket (the grounded antagonist web — an evidence/petition meta-track)
factions_zones.kind: cross-cutting network (antagonist meta-track shape) + linked NPC-cluster
factions_zones.tier: cross-tier: roots T2 (Magobei; Yagoemon's ledgers) → T3 (Kuroiwa's cover-up; Zenroku's contraband; Hanzaki at the pass) → T4 (the daikan who profits; the osso petition) → T5 (an Edo factor laundering the silver, untouchable)
factions_zones.theme: The grounded human antagonist of the whole saga — not a monster but a QUOTA MACHINE: officials and middlemen skimming tax-rice and moving smuggled silver, dressing negligence as fate. The same machine nearly sold Tama, drowned Kuzuhara, and wrote off Tahei's caravan. The 'one rotten root' under the spine, the village grief, the origin, and the house's backstory. GROUNDED keystone of NO-MAGIC: every folk-belief resolves one-to-one — kappa ford = undertow + weighted-net sinking-spot; tanuki stealing rice = Magobei's skim; cursed village = diverted relief-rice + neglected embankment; kamikakushi itself = people who fled debt/violence or died on a lethal road. Debunked with dawning DREAD, never a Scooby-Doo unmasking; the apex magistrate and Edo factor escape — partial justice held.
factions_zones.first_encounter: MINIMAL: ONE thread — Foreman Magobei, 'the tanuki of the rumours board' (T2), skimming village rice. You meet the racket as a single petty-theft mystery, not a conspiracy. One contact, one suspicious detail (a doctored measuring-box).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Expose Magobei's skim → a strand leads to Yagoemon's doctored ledgers (the reachable, frightened middleman). → T3: the strand climbs to Tedai Kuroiwa (the patient, dangerous architect) and Zenroku's short-weighted/contraband caravans; the smugglers' 'kappa' sinking-spot at the ford yields recoverable evidence/drowned-witness clues; Hanzaki appears as the pass enforcer (the talent-foil — trained not gifted; the famine-band under Gonpachi can be FED/resettled as a mercy quest, not killed). → T4: the trail reaches the daikan who profits; an osso-style petition is mounted on documented proof (priest's register + Naozane's testimony + a porter-witness + the rigged kyo-masu); the racket-evidence track physically routes through the castle-town warehouse weighing-yard where the rigged masu is found. → T5: the silver's Edo laundering factor is GLIMPSED but UNTOUCHABLE. Each rung is discovered open-endedly through labour/travel/rising trust (a suggestion + a story, never A→B→C). The racket THREADS into the other networks as friction (a manifest scrap from the Porter Guild, a temple register entry from the Pilgrimage order, a doctored box from the Finance network's books, a porter-witness from the origin).

key_npcs.name: Foreman Magobei
key_npcs.role: Existing: entry rung — petty skimming foreman, the 'tanuki'; reachable, human.

key_npcs.name: Headman Yagoemon
key_npcs.role: Existing: doctored-ledger middleman (double duty — village quest-giver AND humanly-reachable culprit; never a monster).

key_npcs.name: Tedai Kuroiwa
key_npcs.role: Existing: primary human antagonist of the regional/domain tiers — architect of the cover-up; lives at the T4 daikan's office as its polite, dangerous tedai; opposes the rise because a documented house asks where the rice went. Defeated by evidence routed ABOVE him.

key_npcs.name: Hanzaki
key_npcs.role: Existing: scarred ronin enforcer; the talent-gone-rotten mirror; the rare dangerous combat beat, survived by labour-built conditioning. Shared with the Porter Guild route-risk.

key_npcs.name: Clerk Naozane
key_npcs.role: Existing: guilt-sick junior clerk — the crack in the cover-up; supplies documentary proof; also issues/forges the pass paperwork (a documentary crack at the sekisho).

key_npcs.name: Daikan Iemasa
key_npcs.role: New (T4): the deputy magistrate — overworked, status-conscious, not personally corrupt but incurious; signs what Kuroiwa hands him; embodies how negligence dressed as competence shields the guilty above (he largely escapes — honoured ceiling).

key_npcs.name: The Edo laundering factor
key_npcs.role: New (T5): glimpsed-but-untouchable apex who launders the silver; the wall the evidence reaches and cannot cross.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Not a producer — an EVIDENCE/petition meta-track woven through the others: clues collected via ordinary work, travel, and trust across tiers; exposing a rung DENTS the racket's drag on the economy (it skims/saturates/blocks passage against you) and yields a recoverable, scripted Influence beat (a won petition = distinguished service). The rare Hanzaki fight uses the idle-combat suite gated behind conditioning. A mercy-vs-force choice on the famine-band.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Distinguished service (lever 5): a resolved case/won petition is a headline Influence beat and helps bridge toward T4's service gate. CRITICAL inversion: while active the racket is the chief diegetic DRAG (extractive quotas, market manipulation, blocked roads) — dismantling reachable rungs unlocks cheaper trade, safer roads, cleaner finance, so it feeds the spine by REMOVING friction as much as by adding Influence.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: STRUCTURALLY woven into the spine (the rotten root the story turns on) BUT its side-mysteries (lost-child, full caravan truth, the prosecution arc) are OPTIONAL and never hard-gate the climb. Tier progress is ALWAYS achievable by economic means alone; pursuing the racket is a parallel justice track that enriches and unblocks. A player can rise to creditworthiness and Edo renown without ever taking down Kuroiwa (it costs only justice and some Influence).

factions_zones.name: The Pilgrimage Order (Asagiri shrine → regional temple circuit → domain head-temple → an Edo dedication)
factions_zones.tier: cross-tier: seed T2 (Priest Ryoa's shrine); regional circuit T3; domain head-temple patronage T4; a celebrated Edo offering T5
factions_zones.theme: Soft power and legitimacy through visible piety and patronage — an authentic Edo route to standing. Folk-religion TEXTURE (Shinto shrine vs Buddhist temple, Bon, jizo, soul-calling) as believed-atmosphere, never mechanized miracle. The region-scale cousin of Asagiri's single shrine, and the home of the cross-valley priest-register evidence net that feeds the Racket. GROUNDED is load-bearing: no rite ever mechanically 'works' — the soul-calling brings comfort not a soul; a 'miracle cure' resolves to Ranpo's medicine or coincidence; an omen resolves to weather/human action. Respectful Shinto/Buddhist distinction per the locked folklore decision. This network is the natural home of the ONE capped residual-ambiguity beat — kept at the Asagiri boundary-stone/jizo per canon.
factions_zones.first_encounter: MINIMAL: the Asagiri shrine opens (T2) with ONE act — leave a single offering / hear the soul-calling rite from Priest Ryoa. One contact, one node, one verb. (His register of the vanished is also a lost-child clue — double duty.)
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Tend/repair the local shrine → small village goodwill + the temple register opens as a lore node. → sponsor a modest local rite/festival (ties to Brewer Tokuemon's festival hub) — the estate's mon appears as a patron. → T3: Ryoa asks the estate to send an offering to a neighbouring valley's neglected shrine for the regional Bon (one verb); a 'regional piety/goodwill' soft meter and a second shrine node appear; patronising rites & repairing a shrine path unlocks the next shrine and a soft reputation perk (smoother dealings with that valley's headman — never stats); the pilgrimage ROUTE appears as a thin line linking shrines (foot-traffic the waystations/goods benefit from); the cross-valley priest-register EVIDENCE net becomes an optional investigation layer for the racket/lost-child. → T4: patronage of the domain head-temple buys legitimacy and a written vouch that lifts the house upward (lever 6). → T5: a celebrated dedication/offering at an Edo temple lifts the house's face at the capital (indirect, mediated). Each shrine added is one small story beat about that valley's grief.

key_npcs.name: Priest Ryoa
key_npcs.role: Existing Asagiri shrine-keeper, wider role: first contact; runs the soul-calling rite and the register of the vanished (hard evidence in the lost-child thread); introduces the regional clergy and curates the cross-valley register net.

key_npcs.name: Peddler/pilgrim Sokichi
key_npcs.role: Existing village peddler, wider role: the canon 'bridge' beyond the village; first walks the player toward the regional circuit and carries word of the house's piety down the road; doubles as a road-scale information broker (gossip and paid manifests, not prophecy).

key_npcs.name: Abbot Tetsugen
key_npcs.role: New (T3/T4): head of the small pilgrimage-route temple / domain head-temple; clergy gatekeeper whose endorsement lends the house a pious name (soft Influence) and a written vouch.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A patronage sub-loop: spend koku/coin/goods on rites, repairs, offerings → recognized piety → Influence + reputation + occasional lay-supporter ally + festival labour. Pilgrimage stops are discrete milestone nodes (chunky one-time unlocks, each a sensory story beat). Pilgrim foot-traffic lightly boosts waystation trade. The cross-valley register is an evidence network for the optional mysteries. Soft power — never combat or stats.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Patronage & alliance (lever 6) in its pious form: visible devotion + a head-temple's vouch are recognized standing that greases headman/broker/official dealings; feeds Influence as RECOGNIZED standing and supplies upward-vouching patrons for the T4→T5 climb.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SIDE / fully optional. A secular player skips it entirely and loses only soft-power and lore. CONFIRMED: no rite and no shrine node ever gates tier progression (binds the locked folklore rule).

factions_zones.name: The Artisan Masters' Lineage (village artisans → regional master & sumiyaki → castle-town guild charter → the meibutsu celebrated in Edo)
factions_zones.kind: cross-cutting network (per-node patron web → craft lineage shape)
factions_zones.tier: cross-tier: seed T2 (Gonta/Risuke/Onatsu/Tokuemon); regional master + sumiyaki sub-economy T3; chartered guilds (za/kabu-nakama) + wholesale grading T4; meibutsu fame T5
factions_zones.theme: Mastery passed hand to hand — the grind made social. The protagonist organizes (and the artisans perfect) a signature regional PRODUCT (the meibutsu) that becomes the house's claim to fame, climbing from village tools to a domain meibutsu celebrated in the capital. Absorbs the charcoal-burners' sumiyaki sub-economy as a T3 supply line and the castle-town wholesale-row grading as its T4 reveal. GROUNDED: real Edo by-employments and craft economics (sericulture, brewing, weaving, lacquer, papermaking, sumiyaki + firewood-floating); za/kabu-nakama are attested chartered guilds; no magic forging — 'masterwork' is skill+time+materials, a 'blessed brew'/'lucky cloth' is marketing/belief resolving to quality; the 'fox-fire' is a hidden kiln; burners handled with dignity (organised fairly or exploitatively at the player's choice — the extract-vs-uplift tension).
factions_zones.first_encounter: MINIMAL: ONE artisan, ONE recipe — Smith Gonta gates basic metalcraft (a single tool repaired/forged), valuing shown effort over flash. One contact, one verb (craft).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Gonta patron/apprentice standing unlocks the first craft chain (tools→spearheads). → the artisans' guild meter opens Risuke (building), Onatsu (cloth), the dyer, and — at T3 — the scattered CHARCOAL-BURNERS' grounds (sumiyaki): buy a lone burner Tasuke's first poor load → investigate the 'fox-fire ridge' rumour (debunks to his hidden kiln, builds trust) → provision the burners (by-employment lever) → unlock firewood RIVER-FLOATING (a throughput line for the Porter Guild & broker). → T3: a regional MASTER takes the first proto-industry commission (sericulture/silk, sake, or textile organised as a village by-employment the estate takes a cut of). → T4: a master-tier craft quarter gates master tools/components and master commissions; the GUILD CHARTER lever (petition the meibutsu under a kabu-nakama charter — quality assurance + protected branding); product GRADING via the wholesale row (silk/sake/textile each its own buyer) and proto-branding; an optional guild SEAT/kabu. → T5: the meibutsu is celebrated in Edo's markets (the spine's headline T5 outcome). Apprenticeship side-quests deepen specific lineages, each open-ended.

key_npcs.name: Smith Gonta
key_npcs.role: Existing: first artisan contact; gates metalcraft; effort-over-flash ethos.

key_npcs.name: Carpenter Risuke
key_npcs.role: Existing: gates building/construction (rebuild & expand the estate; later the Kuzuhara river-works lead). Shared with Kuzuhara.

key_npcs.name: Weaver Onatsu
key_npcs.role: Existing: cloth/textile; ties into Lady Chiyo's household economy and origin routes; lead meibutsu candidate.

key_npcs.name: Brewer Tokuemon
key_npcs.role: Existing: sake; proto-industry candidate and festival/social hub (cross-links to the Pilgrimage festivals).

key_npcs.name: Charcoal-burner Tasuke
key_npcs.role: New (T3): first contact to the sumiyaki community; the 'fox-fire' debunk in person; suspicious of lowland houses until the estate provisions fairly.

key_npcs.name: Guild-elder Kanshiro
key_npcs.role: New (T4): head of the relevant kabu-nakama; proud protectionist gatekeeper of quality and charters — won over by craft standards, not coin.

key_npcs.name: Master-dyer Oko / Silk-tonya Iseya Han'emon
key_npcs.role: New (T4): master finisher + meticulous wholesale grader who raise Asagiri textile to graded, brandable quality and gate its standing contract.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Craft chains over the crafting/component economy: recipes and component tiers gated by per-artisan patron standing (mirrors village shop meters); a proto-industry sub-loop (organize a by-employment → produce a refined good → take a cut) scaling into the meibutsu; the sumiyaki spend→produce→sell loop + firewood-floating throughput; a master craft TIER (master tools/components feeding grounded combat-gear and proto-industry); guild regard meters; the GUILD CHARTER + optional kabu seat; product grading + multi-line portfolio + proto-branding. High standing softens the saturation crash on the signature good. Carpenter-built construction physically unlocks estate/region nodes.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Cash crops & proto-industry (lever 2): building toward a famous domain product / meibutsu is one of the most direct Influence engines and the literal substance celebrated at Edo; a charter is formal quality recognition; a guild seat is standing the officialdom/samurai tracks register; charcoal aggregation seeds the meibutsu story. Cross-links to river/road works (lever 3) via Risuke.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: OPTIONAL in depth, but its OUTPUT (a tradeable surplus / signature product) is the natural fuel for the T3+ economy and the meibutsu that crowns the endgame. A player can climb on raw rice alone (slower, no meibutsu option); apprenticeship quests, charcoal, and the kabu seat are pure side content. Never a hard gate.

factions_zones.name: The Scholars' & Physicians' Network (Ranpo & Obaa Sato → domain academy/han-school → Edo learning)
factions_zones.tier: cross-tier: seed T1 (Ranpo) + T2 (Obaa Sato); domain academy/han-school + town physician T3–T4; Edo learning / a recognized treatise T5
factions_zones.theme: Knowledge as the quiet third kind of power, beside muscle (Porter Guild) and money (Finance). Practical learning — medicine, agronomy, accounting, surveying, water-engineering — that materially improves yields, heals the workforce, and earns intellectual legitimacy. The rational counter-voice to folklore: it NAMES symptoms not visions, embankment failures not curses. Absorbs the T4 domain-academy (hankoo) etiquette/letters function as its formal-learning rung. GROUNDED ANTI-magic anchor: Ranpo names trauma not spirits; agronomy/engineering replace omen-reading; a 'cursed harvest' resolves to blight/weather and is fixed by technique; folk-medicine is respected herb-craft + placebo-comfort, never mechanized miracle; the recovery aid and porter's-knot confer ZERO combat/stat gift — only access/efficiency (binds no-hidden-edge).
factions_zones.first_encounter: MINIMAL: ONE contact, ONE service — Ranpo dresses the healing scalp wound at the open (T1), naming symptoms not visions, gating healing/medicine, flatly disbelieving the kami story. One node, one verb (be treated / later, gather a remedy).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Ranpo gates the healing/medicine sub-system; Obaa Sato teaches foraging/herb-craft. → practical knowledge applied: agronomy improves paddy yield, surveying enables the shinden reclamation, water-engineering plans the embankment (cross-links to the Kuzuhara river-works and the backstory). → T3/T4: a domain academy / han-school + a town physician open as nodes — formal learning confers legitimacy, better technique, status-appropriate etiquette/letters that smooth every other castle-town track, and a scholar who vouches the house upward. → T5: a recognized treatise on the house's agronomy/water-works, or a rare scholarly appointment, lifts the house's name at the capital (indirect, mediated). Each step is a fresh minimal arrival (one teacher, one technique).

key_npcs.name: Ranpo
key_npcs.role: Existing estate physician: first contact; gates healing/medicine; the rational, kami-disbelieving voice; grounds the mundane amnesia.

key_npcs.name: Obaa Sato
key_npcs.role: Existing village herbalist: folk-medicine and the sympathetic folklore-keeper who KNOWS the legend is coping; bridges folk-lore and practical herb-craft.

key_npcs.name: Dowager Toku
key_npcs.role: Existing estate backstory keeper, wider role: her memory of grandfather Sadamune's failed flood-venture is the knowledge that motivates getting the water-engineering RIGHT this time (ties scholarship to Kuzuhara). Shared with Kuzuhara.

key_npcs.name: Academy-head Rai Genpachi
key_npcs.role: New (T3/T4): hankoo principal — a stern Confucian scholar who grades effort/character over birth (the domain-scale restatement of Jubei's creed); gatekeeper of formal learning and samurai-society respectability.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A research/technique sub-loop: invest time/coin into learning (agronomy, surveying, medicine, accounting, etiquette/letters) → permanent technique upgrades that improve producer yields, heal/condition the workforce faster (recovery aid, NOT a combat bonus), and unlock the engineering for river/road works. Knowledge nodes are discrete milestones (chunky, like origin ties).
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Cross-links to river & road works (lever 3 — the surveying/engineering that secures the embankment and earns official credit, also resolving the backstory) and distinguished service (lever 5 — a treatise or famine-relief technique recognized by the domain). Feeds Influence by making every OTHER lever more efficient and buying intellectual legitimacy for the T4→T5 climb.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SIDE / optional accelerant. A player can climb without ever investing in learning (slower, less efficient, more downtime). The only near-spine touchpoint: sound engineering makes the Kuzuhara river-works beats safer/richer — but those remain achievable minimally. Never blocks the spine.

factions_zones.name: Rival House Tomita (the inter-house status rival)
factions_zones.tier: cross-tier: contests valleys/brokers/routes T3; broker terms + samurai-society standing + the daikan's favour T4; stages its own display at the inspector set-piece T5
factions_zones.theme: The single inter-house STATUS rival woven across the whole world: a competing goshi/merchant house that prospered exactly as the Kurosawa fell — by capital + connections + ruthlessness, never innate gift (the talent-foil rule). Contests every prize the Kurosawa reach for. A rival to OUT-MANEUVER commercially and socially, never to kill. GROUNDED: pure status/economic competition between two real-type houses; no magic, no innate-talent gap (Tomita's edge is matchable by work); out-maneuvered, never beaten by a power fantasy; partial outcomes honoured.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE collision, not a faction-board: the estate's first attempt to court a neighbouring valley or sign a broker contract is quietly underbid by 'House Tomita' — a name on a competing manifest. The only initial handle is the contested deal itself (win or lose it commercially).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: First underbid → Tomita is a single rival name. → repeated contests reveal a 'house standing vs Tomita' relative meter (valleys leaning, routes contested, broker terms compared). → an emissary beat introduces Tomita's heir/agent in person (negotiation, not duel). → the rivalry deepens into discrete contests (a courted valley, broker exclusivity, a road-works credit, the daikan's notice, a samurai-society introduction) each won by out-PERFORMING — out-supplying, out-pricing, out-arbitrating, building goodwill they neglect. → T5 capstone: Tomita stages its own display to win the touring inspector's favour over the Kurosawa — decided by substance vs show. → detente, forced alliance, or clean commercial defeat. A squeezed valley flipping to the fairer Kurosawa shows ruthlessness has a cost.

key_npcs.name: Tomita Sozaemon / Kageyuki (head & heir)
key_npcs.role: New: head/heir of the rival house — shrewd, established, dismissive of the upstart Kurosawa; every advantage shown as bought/lucked/ruthless, never innately superior (binds the talent-foil rule).

key_npcs.name: Tomita's agent Yasubei
key_npcs.role: New: the rival's regional fixer who underbids deals, courts the same valleys, leans on brokers — the legible day-to-day face you out-maneuver.

key_npcs.name: Kurosawa Naoyuki
key_npcs.role: Existing estate heir, contrast role: now an ALLY against Tomita — the internal talent-foil-turned-brother whose converted-not-innate growth mirrors the thesis; reports from Edo what the protagonist's work bought the house. Shared with Sankin-kotai.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A relative 'house standing vs rival' meter; contested-deal mechanics across valleys/brokers/routes/officials (zero-sum prizes won by performance, not violence); a detente/alliance/defeat resolution that carries upward. Competitive pressure that gives the whole economy stakes and direction.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Every contest won is a recognized gain at the rival's expense → Influence; the daikan/inspector noticing the Kurosawa edging out an established house is a bridge beat toward 'be reckoned with by those who rule.'
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: MIXED, leaning SIDE. The rivalry COLOURS the spine but no single contest is mandatory — you can climb by growing your own pie rather than beating theirs every time. CONFIRMED: losing a Tomita contest never hard-blocks the spine, only slows/reshapes it. The deepest rivalry arc is optional.

factions_zones.name: The Neighbouring Valleys (allied & rival villages of the district)
factions_zones.theme: The estate stops being 'the big house in one valley' and becomes a presence across a CLUSTER of valleys — each a small Asagiri-cousin (a headman, a shrine, a craft speciality, its own grievances) to court, supply, arbitrate, or out-compete. Asagiri itself is the model; this is that model, fractally, at district scale. GROUNDED pure socio-economics: headmen, surplus, debts, brokered consignments, festival diplomacy; folklore-light (each valley carries its OWN small kamikakushi-flavoured rumour that resolves to a mundane cause and may feed the priest-register evidence net); no magic — the contest with rival valleys is commercial and reputational only.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE node only: a single down-valley hamlet, Hibara, sends its headman's son to beg the now-anchored estate to broker its surplus (one verb: agree to take Hibara's first consignment). You never 'open the whole district' — you meet one valley.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Hibara contact → after one fulfilled consignment, Hibara's per-valley goodwill meter appears and its tiny shop-row opens (1 craft speciality: ramie/hemp cloth). → a goodwill threshold reveals a SECOND valley (Toge-mura, a mountain-mouth village whose speciality is timber/charcoal access — a bridge to the charcoal-burners & matagi). → each valley unlocks the SAME shape Asagiri taught (headman regard + per-shop standing + per-family help + a craft node) but slimmer, so it is familiar not overwhelming. → a 'district standing' roll-up bar fades in only after 2+ valleys are courted, then a 'valley alliance' beat (a shared festival, a joint relief-rice pool) converts the cluster into one bloc the estate speaks for. Rival valleys (leaning to Tomita) appear as contested meters you flip by out-supplying/out-arbitrating, never by force.

key_npcs.name: Headman Kyubei of Hibara
key_npcs.role: New: first neighbouring-valley contact & gatekeeper; cautious, proud, owes a rice debt he fears the estate will weaponise (echoes Yagoemon's shape, not a copy) — a warm quest-giver who tests whether the rising house extracts or uplifts.

key_npcs.name: Onui
key_npcs.role: New: Hibara's ramie-weaver and district gossip-spine (the 'Sayo of the wider valleys'); tutorializes neighbouring-valley reputation and carries news of which valleys lean Tomita. (Distinct from Marutaya's heir Osato.)

key_npcs.name: Headman Tarozaemon of Toge-mura
key_npcs.role: New: mountain-mouth headman; the human gateway to Toge-mura's charcoal grounds and the matagi compact; gruff, transactional, respects delivered results.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A district-wide reputation WEB (the Asagiri web multiplied across valleys), each valley a craft speciality the estate aggregates, routes, and takes a cut of; a 'district standing' roll-up; a valley-alliance bloc lever. Feeds the broker network (aggregated surplus) and the Porter Guild (more routes). Carries the locked estate-vs-village tension upward: high-rank duties may demand extracting quota from a courted valley (Influence up, goodwill down) with an always-available 'soften it quietly' option.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Each courted/allied valley is a recognized expansion of reach → region-band Influence on each alliance/contract sealed (recognized by the district's headmen and, later, the daikan noticing a house that speaks for a bloc). Being the broker-anchor for a cluster is a direct trade-ties + patronage lever.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SIDE / optional accelerant. Courting valleys speeds Influence and unlocks craft/trade depth but is never required: a spine-only player reaches T4 through the estate's own surplus + the broker runway. Confirmed non-blocking. (Tuned inside the ~halves-time multiplier.)

factions_zones.name: Kuzuhara — the re-foundable upstream hamlet & embankment river-works
factions_zones.theme: The geographic and emotional KNOT of T3: the drowned upstream hamlet (the disaster that nearly killed Tahei and is one root of the racket) becomes a re-foundable resettlement node AND the site of the embankment (seki) river-works that 'secures the disaster so it cannot recur.' The locked faction-3 fusion: Origin-thread payoff fused with a spine Influence project (access-only, grind-built). GROUNDED no-magic: the disaster was a neglected embankment diverted to protect tax fields (locked canon); the rebuild is corvee + engineering + koku; 'naming the drowned' is grief-work and a temple register, not a rite that 'works'; the 'cursed valley' belief resolves to the documented negligence.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE site, ONE verb: with memory returned + conditioning + standing to range, the protagonist reaches the broken embankment and can do exactly one thing — clear/survey the ruined seki. No town, no producers yet; just a scarred site and a single labour verb.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Survey the broken seki → reveals the river-works PROJECT bar (a multi-stage embankment rebuild — the canonical Tier-3 flood-control lever) AND the resettlement-site stub. → first works stage stabilises the channel and unlocks the FIRST resettlement plot (one returning family / one Origin recruit as an idle producer). → each works stage names more of the drowned (a temple-register/grief beat) and adds a resettlement producer slot. → completing the embankment 'secures' the valley (a major recognition beat) and converts Kuzuhara into a small standing producer-base + the backstory's resolution (the cover-up evidence surfaces here). Strictly fractal: a ruin → a wall → a plot → a hamlet, never handed whole.

key_npcs.name: Oharu / the real Tama
key_npcs.role: Existing, wider role: the living lost child connected to Kuzuhara's resettlement and the rice-debt cover-up that drowned it; her choice whether to be part of the re-founding is the costly-and-incomplete emotional payoff.

key_npcs.name: Carpenter Risuke
key_npcs.role: Existing village builder, wider role: the river-works/construction lead for the embankment stages — gates the building system at regional scale. (Shared with the Artisan Lineage.)

key_npcs.name: Dowager Toku
key_npcs.role: Existing estate backstory keeper, wider role: her knowledge of grandfather Sadamune's failed flood-venture makes Kuzuhara the house ATONING for its own root-sin. (Shared with the Scholars network.)

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A multi-stage river-works construction project (the Tier-3 flood-control lever), a new resettlement producer-base (idle producers, a fresh small economy), and the backstory-resolution evidence layer. A genuine new canvas node, not a menu.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: The embankment is a flagship distinguished-service / river-works conversion lever — securing it is RECOGNIZED by the district/daikan as a large region-band Influence award, and is the cleanest bridge beat toward T4 (flood-control service is exactly the T4 entry gate). The resettlement adds standing producers.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE (one of the two clearly-spine T3 nodes, with the broker runway). A headline T3 beat per §1.6 and a natural T4 gateway. The Origin-emotional resolution layered on it stays optional in TONE; the works themselves are core. The memory-gate is access, not a side-quest wall.

factions_zones.name: The Wider Post-Town Region (Sawatari-juku's outer commercial world & the kaido waystation network)
factions_zones.theme: The Origin faction's home town opened in T3 as Tahei's personal reunion; this is its OUTWARD commercial face as a regional node — the honjin/waki-honjin, the toiya transport office, its market, and the chain of smaller waystations up and down the kaido. Where the estate becomes a recognized trading house with a presence on the road. GROUNDED: attested Edo institutions (toiya/tonya shipping agents, honjin/waki-honjin official inns, sukego-supported porter-and-horse allocation, status-marked lodging); no magic — the 'information broker' is gossip and paid manifests, not prophecy.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE verb at ONE node: once the dream-gate + travel-standing open Sawatari-juku for the Origin reunion, a single additional door appears — the toiya master will register the estate's mon for a standing porter-and-horse allocation IF the estate posts a guarantor. That is the only regional handle at first.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Toiya registration → the waystation MAP appears as a thin line (Sawatari-juku only) with a freight-throughput meter. → first fulfilled freight run reveals the NEXT waystation up the kaido (a half-day stage) and its tiny market. → toiya reputation unlocks the waki-honjin (lodge there with status — a recognition beat) and a regional information-broker service (paid rumours surfacing district opportunities & Tomita's moves). → late: a branch consignment office the estate keeps at Sawatari-juku (a holdings node); the honjin becomes reachable only for ceremonial recognition, never daily use. Each stage is one new dot on the line.

key_npcs.name: Toiya-master Chobei
key_npcs.role: New: runs the post-town transport office; primary regional-trade gatekeeper — registers the allocation, gates the waystation network and freight throughput; loyalty bought by reliable volume.

key_npcs.name: Sister Okimi
key_npcs.role: Existing origin NPC, wider role: married into a trading family — the estate's natural in at the post-town's commerce; her family's manifests legitimise consignments. Access-only bridge, zero mechanical gift. (Shared with the Finance network.)

key_npcs.name: Waki-honjin keeper Itoya Han'emon
key_npcs.role: New: keeper of the sub-honjin; a status-gatekeeper whose lodging-the-house-with-honour is a small Influence recognition beat.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: The freight-throughput / waystation logistics layer (the physical road over which the Porter Guild operates), a paid regional information-broker service, a branch consignment office (first holdings node), and status-lodging recognition beats. The commercial supply-spine the broker network and neighbouring valleys both feed into.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: A registered mon on the toiya's books, a standing porter allocation, and lodging at the waki-honjin are RECOGNITIONS of regional standing → region-band Influence; the road presence is the literal canvas the T3 Influence number sits on (levers 3 & 7).
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: MIXED. The post-town REUNION is Origin-side (optional); but the toiya/waystation TRADE layer is a primary runway to surplus-export and thus part of the practical spine to T4 — designed so the Origin-emotional content stays optional while the commercial road stays available to a spine-only player (registration needs standing, not the reunion).

factions_zones.name: The Matagi Hunters of the High Range
factions_zones.theme: A proud, semi-closed band of mountain hunters (matagi) of the high cedars and snow-range — masters of terrain that kills outsiders. The gateway to safe high-mountain expeditions, rare mountain goods, and a guarded compact; the grounded reality behind the high-woods 'yamanba/tengu/mountain-god' rumours. NOT a combat-power source for the protagonist (no-hidden-edge holds). GROUNDED matagi culture (terrain mastery, bear-fat medicine, game, charcoal links, a respectful mountain-courtesy) handled with dignity, never 'wild people to tame'; their skill is lifelong labour, not innate gift; all high-woods 'yokai' resolve to mundane causes.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE contact, ONE verb: the matagi don't recruit — a lone hunter, Shozo, found near Toge-mura, will only let the estate buy ONE bear-fat salve and warns him off the high range. The single verb is a respectful trade; trust is the whole gate.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: First respectful trade → a thin 'matagi regard' meter (earned by fair dealing, gifts of rice/iron, and NOT trespassing). → regard threshold: a hunter GUIDES one high-range expedition (safe passage through terrain that otherwise kills — the matagi as living expedition insurance). → deeper regard unlocks a hunting/forage compact (rare goods for the broker: hides, bear-fat medicine, game), then a SHARED-RANGE agreement (the estate respects their grounds; they secure a high route for the Porter Guild against the bandit menace). → late: a respectful mountain-courtesy beat (folklore-light) and recruited hunter-scouts who make pass expeditions survivable. Their knowledge debunks the 'mountain god' rumours one expedition at a time.

key_npcs.name: Matagi Shozo
key_npcs.role: New: first contact; the wary trade-and-warning gatekeeper who slowly becomes the high-range ally if treated with respect, not as a resource to strip.

key_npcs.name: Matagi headman Kuranosuke
key_npcs.role: New: leader of the band; gates the hunting/forage compact and the shared-range agreement; a second dignified effort-over-talent model (skilled by a lifetime of mountain work, never magic).

key_npcs.name: Genza
key_npcs.role: Existing region NPC, wider role: the fugitive ex-foot-soldier 'tengu' of the high cedars becomes the bridge between the estate and the matagi — a wary teacher, witness, and the human debunk of the high-woods rumour.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A 'matagi regard' trust track; expedition-guide/safe-passage (high-range exploration & logistics safety); a rare mountain-goods supply line for the broker; a shared-range agreement securing high routes against bandits; recruited hunter-scouts. Crucially UTILITY (access, safety, goods, scouts) — NOT a personal-combat upgrade.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Their goods feed the broker (trade ties); secured high routes protect throughput (road works/security); a formal compact with a respected band is a recognition beat (patronage/alliance). Indirect but real Influence, all access/utility, zero hidden-edge.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SIDE / optional. Gates the deepest wilderness exploration and the pass route's safety, but those are optional frontiers; the spine to T4 runs through trade/works. Confirmed non-blocking. Pairs with the bandit menace as the wilderness counter-weight.

factions_zones.name: The Daikan's Office (代官所) — domain officialdom & the racket's nerve-centre
factions_zones.theme: Being reckoned with — and watched — by the men who actually administer the land. The polite, ledgered face of power, with rot underneath: the same quota machine that menaced the valley, now seen from the top of the funnel. Bureaucracy as both ladder and trap; where most T4 Influence is minted. GROUNDED administrative realism: daikan/tedai/kanjo-kata/machi-bugyo are attested; the racket is mundane fraud (rigged kyo-masu, doctored tally-sticks, short-weighted shipments); NO folklore here — the castle-town is deliberately the rational, ledgered counter-world to the valley's superstition, and that contrast is the point.
factions_zones.first_encounter: A single summons: after the T4 entry-gate service is rendered, a low tedai delivers a sealed note ordering the Kurosawa's representative to present himself — one node (the receiving anteroom), one verb (present credentials / answer questions).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Arrive minimal: kept waiting, dealt with by junior clerks (one contact: the courteous, careful Tedai Kuroiwa). → repeated correct conduct + service record earns an audience with the daikan (Iemasa) and the 'standing with officialdom' meter. → the rice/finance sub-office (kanjo-kata) opens: the house's reclaimed yields and contracts are FORMALLY RECORDED (the koku→Influence conversion at domain scale). → the magistrate's apparatus (machi-bugyo-sho) opens: the petition/case hall where formal disputes and the osso petition are heard. → deep/optional/late: the audit/evidence sub-thread — cross-referencing recorded yields against quota ledgers exposes the skim; the racket-evidence track unlocks (routes through the warehouse weighing-yard where the rigged masu is found). Each step is one new door inside the compound, announced as a story beat.

key_npcs.name: Tedai Kuroiwa
key_npcs.role: Existing: senior agent; first & main contact; outwardly gracious facilitator, secretly the local architect of the skim/cover-up; defeated by evidence routed ABOVE him, never the sword. (Shared with the Racket node.)

key_npcs.name: Daikan Iemasa
key_npcs.role: New: the deputy magistrate — overworked, status-conscious, not personally corrupt but incurious; the apex to impress for officialdom standing; embodies how negligence shields the guilty above (largely escapes — honoured ceiling). (Shared with the Racket node.)

key_npcs.name: Clerk Naozane
key_npcs.role: Existing: guilt-sick kanjo-kata clerk who transcribed the lies; the crack in the cover-up and reachable conscience who supplies documentary proof. (Shared with the Racket and Porter Guild paperwork.)

key_npcs.name: The Touring Inspector (Metsuke / junkenshi)
key_npcs.role: Existing apex authority, surfaced here as the over-the-head petition's ultimate audience — reachable only at the very top of T4/into T5; only partial justice ever won. (Headlines its own T5 set-piece.)

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Formal RECORDING of achievements (the explicit koku→Influence conversion at domain scale); the petition/case system (win formal disputes; the osso petition as the high-risk capstone); a 'standing with officialdom' meter gating which offices you may approach; the optional racket-EVIDENCE track. Bureaucratic friction is a soft cost (wrong etiquette/low standing slows recording).
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: The authority that RECOGNISES T4 achievements — where most T4 Influence is minted (service acknowledged, contracts sealed, a won case). The racket sub-thread ties the estate's backstory, the lost-child mystery, and the origin caravan into the 'one rotten root'; a partial win lifts Influence while the magistrate above largely escapes.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE-CRITICAL for the formal-recognition mechanic (you cannot bank T4 Influence without engaging officialdom) — but the RACKET-EVIDENCE prosecution sub-thread is OPTIONAL and never blocks the spine (a player may reach Osaka renown without ever taking down Kuroiwa).

factions_zones.name: The Kurosawa Castle-Town Townhouse & the Entertainment/Social District
factions_zones.theme: The estate's beachhead in the seat of power AND the grease of the castle-town machine. The townhouse is the literal upgradeable home-base through which all T4 introductions route; the teahouse/ryotei quarter beside it is where deals no office will host are struck and standing is performed. Together: the connective tissue that makes every other T4 faction REACHABLE. (Merged: the two facets' separate 'townhouse' and 'social district' entries are one hub.) GROUNDED: retainers/rising houses maintaining a castle-town residence, and teahouse quarters as the real venue for dealmaking, are well attested; period-accurate restraint (geisha as performing artists — music/dance/conversation, NOT prostitution; no explicit content); the ONE permitted T4 folklore wink lives here as a single light 'castle-town ghost story' rumour — fully optional, instantly mundane, never mechanical.
factions_zones.first_encounter: One verb: secure a modest rented townhouse (the T4 entry reward) — a single bare room with a gate, mirroring the T1 kura cold-open (the fractal motion restated). The town-agent then points you to ONE reputable teahouse where you may host one modest gathering or be brought as a guest.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Townhouse: bare room → install a caretaker/houseman (opens the 'receive introductions' verb, the social hub) → upgrade rooms (receiving room = entertaining; strongroom = hold coin/credit for finance; staff quarters = house seconded allies) → a town agent transacts while you're at the estate (idle/auto T4 dealings) + a stable → late: own (not rent) and upgrade to befit standing (a visible Influence trophy). Social district: rapport with the proprietress unlocks the INTRODUCTIONS system (spend coin + standing to be presented to a specific official/merchant/retainer you couldn't otherwise reach — gating who across ALL other T4 factions you can even meet) → a ryotei + more venues raise the tier of guest you can host (entertaining officials = Influence) → a grounded information-broker feed (who is in debt, who feuds, who can be allied — fuels the Tomita rivalry & racket-evidence) → optional patron-of-the-arts angle. Each is one venue/room/contact at a time.

key_npcs.name: Town-agent Heikuro
key_npcs.role: New: the townhouse caretaker/agent; runs introductions, holds the social calendar, transacts on the house's behalf when the protagonist is away (the idle/automation hook). A dryly competent fixer.

key_npcs.name: Proprietress Okatsu
key_npcs.role: New: mistress of the leading teahouse; the district's discreet super-broker who trades in introductions and confidences; rapport with her opens doors to every other faction.

key_npcs.name: Tokujiro (seconded)
key_npcs.role: Existing green estate recruit, wider role: sent to staff the townhouse — re-homing the 'you now teach others from zero' layer at T4 (the climber now mentoring a junior).

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A HOME-BASE hub gating access to the rest of T4 (introductions received here); upgradeable rooms each unlocking a system (entertaining, finance-holding, ally-housing); a town agent enabling idle/automated T4 transactions; the INTRODUCTIONS system (the master gate for WHO you can meet); ENTERTAINING (host gatherings to mint Influence with officials/allies); a grounded information-broker feed; a visible standing trophy (rent→own→befit-standing).
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: The townhouse's grade IS a legible Influence display; several Influence-minting acts (entertaining officials, hosting alliance negotiations, contract signings) physically happen here; introductions are the enabling layer for nearly every other lever (you must be presented before you can render service, restructure debt at the top, or negotiate an alliance).
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE-ENABLING infrastructure (the T4 hub; introductions gate access) but flexible — the minimal townhouse already unlocks the tier and there is always at least one affordable introduction route, so it never hard-blocks; luxe upgrades, the ryotei tier, and the patronage/information depth are optional richness.

factions_zones.name: Domain Samurai Society & the Marriage/Adoption Alliance Brokerage
factions_zones.theme: Acceptance by one's peers — the hardest currency for a taken-in stray turned right-hand. Etiquette and alliance as the gates of true samurai standing, where birth still whispers even as merit shouts. Home of the lord's lifted-standing mechanism (load-bearing for the T5 mediated-recognition endgame) and the marriage/adoption alliance lever (the most authentic, slowest top-rung leap). (Merged: the two facets' 'samurai society' and 'marriage/adoption brokerage' entries; the academy/hankoo learning function lives in the Scholars network.) GROUNDED: hankoo academies, the retainer corps, and strategic marriage / merchant-to-samurai yoshi adoption via goyokin are attested (if rare) mobility levers; strictly social — no folklore, no magic, no romance-fantasy, period restraint; Tomita's edge is explicitly made (trained/lucky/ruthless), per the talent-foil rule.
factions_zones.first_encounter: One invitation: the protagonist, as the Kurosawa's representative, is grudgingly admitted to a single domain function (a poetry/tea gathering or a lecture). One node, one verb — present yourself correctly and not embarrass the house.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Tolerated at one gathering, snubbed by most, with a single cordial contact. → correct conduct + the house's rising fortunes raise a 'samurai-society standing' meter (status-appropriate dialogue smooths every other T4 track; formal learning is gained via the Scholars' han-school). → individual retainers warm one at a time (a peer WEB, not a ladder); a few become allies, one or two stay hostile. → the Tomita rivalry sharpens here (contested introductions, undercut contracts, social slights to out-maneuver). → deep/late/optional: a go-between (nakodo) sounds out the house and the MARRIAGE/ADOPTION ALLIANCE matures — concrete options appear (a domain-retainer line, the merchant Marutaya via Osato, a rival-neutralising tie), each a multi-beat negotiation (standing checks, gifts, goyokin, patron endorsements), capped by an optional symbolic adoption into a better-placed name (a late standing leap). The protagonist's service is converted into the LORD'S lifted standing — the mechanism by which the house (not the man) gains face. You learn the cast gradually, by who deigns to speak to you next; every proposal is declinable.

key_npcs.name: Retainer Tadahiro
key_npcs.role: New: a mid-ranking, fair-minded domain retainer — the protagonist's first genuine peer-ally; a sponsor who vouches him upward (patronage lever).

key_npcs.name: Lord Kurosawa Munenori (in town)
key_npcs.role: Existing head of the house, surfaced at the castle-town: the protagonist's labour materially lifts HIS standing among peers and officials — the mediated-recognition ceiling made structural. Shared with Sankin-kotai.

key_npcs.name: Go-between Omiya-no-Sahei
key_npcs.role: New: a professional marriage/adoption broker (nakodo) plugged into every faction; surfaces alliance options and runs negotiations; trades in standing and discretion, not romance.

key_npcs.name: Lady Kurosawa Chiyo (escalated)
key_npcs.role: Existing, wider role: her domestic-economy and hospitality mastery makes her the house's alliance-strategist at T4 — she vets candidates and hosts negotiations, extending her T1 role outward.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A 'samurai-society standing' meter gating status-appropriate access and smoothing all T4 dealings; a peer-relationship WEB; the Tomita rivalry as contested standing; the PATRONAGE lever (sponsors who vouch upward); and an ALLIANCE-NEGOTIATION sub-loop (surface candidates → standing/gift/goyokin checks → multi-beat negotiation → a sanctioned tie/adoption granting a discrete standing leap + a faction synergy: a retainer tie smooths society, a merchant tie deepens finance, a strategic tie neutralises Tomita). Distinct chunky milestones, closer to the origin track's shape than a meter.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Hosts the patronage/alliance lever (6) and is where the protagonist's service is converted into the LORD'S lifted standing — the load-bearing mechanism of the T5 mediated-recognition endgame. Out-maneuvering Tomita is direct contested Influence; a sanctioned alliance is among the largest discrete Influence steps at T4/into T5.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: MIXED. A baseline of samurai-society standing is SPINE-relevant (status-gating smooths required officialdom/finance dealings and is the path to lifting the lord's face for T5). DEPTH — the Tomita escalations and the WHOLE marriage/adoption brokerage — is FULLY OPTIONAL: the house is completable without any alliance (it costs only the fastest, most 'authentic Edo' rise). Confirmed non-blocking; every proposal declinable.

factions_zones.name: The Domain's Edo Yashiki & the rusui correspondence conduit
factions_zones.theme: The arm's-length embassy: the house's only fixed footprint in the capital is a corner of someone else's compound, run by people the protagonist will only ever know through letters. The primary T5 conduit — and the structural enforcer of the ceiling (ALL bakufu-adjacent contact routes through the rusui, never directly). GROUNDED: kanjo-bugyo/machi-bugyo offices and their clerk/yoriki layers, and domain Edo yashiki run by rusui-yaku, are attested; commoners and provincial retainers reached them only through intermediaries; strictly honours the ceiling — NO direct contact, no hatamoto status, no shogunal audience; no folklore.
factions_zones.first_encounter: A single sealed letter arrives at the estate from the domain's Edo residence — the rusui-yaku acknowledging receipt of the first crate of meibutsu the lord carried up on sankin-kotai. One contact, one inbound verb (read the dispatch).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Read the first dispatch (the yashiki exists, has a name, has one official: rusui Konoe). → earn a reply channel — SEND a manifest/letter (the 'correspondence' panel, a slow async loop measured in road-days). → Konoe names the kami/naka/shimo-yashiki (upper/middle/lower residence) — the compound becomes a small map of rooms you petition through. → meet the working staff as they become relevant (the kura-bugyo who logs incoming goods; the accounts clerk drowning in upkeep). → late: the yashiki becomes a project — the protagonist's surplus quietly underwrites its repair, lifting the lord's prestige among neighbouring domains' residences. Bakufu-adjacent officialdom (permits, sumptuary clearances) is reachable ONLY through Konoe — the wall introduced as a wall; one small filtered favour, then a documented merit notation that enters an official register (the house's name in bakufu ink) and is delivered DOWN through Konoe and the lord, never handed to the protagonist.

key_npcs.name: Rusui-yaku Konoe Settsu-no-suke
key_npcs.role: New: the domain's resident Edo liaison (rusui); the protagonist's SOLE pen-pal proxy in the capital and the single recurring through-line face of T5; warmed gradually as a small relationship arc; the indispensable filter through whom every official contact passes.

key_npcs.name: Kura-bugyo Masaki
key_npcs.role: New: warehouse officer of the Edo residence; logs each incoming crate of meibutsu and reports its reception — the diegetic source of 'your goods arrived and were noted' Influence events.

key_npcs.name: Kanjo-kata clerk Oka Shinnojo
key_npcs.role: New: a mid-level bakufu finance-office clerk the rusui deals with; processes permits and notations; the highest official the protagonist's influence will ever indirectly move.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: The async CORRESPONDENCE loop (send manifests/petitions/goods up, get acknowledgements/instructions/Influence back on a road-day delay); a residence-upkeep sink the house can elect to underwrite; a PERMITS & PETITIONS layer (transit licences, sumptuary compliance, formal acknowledgements) reachable only through the rusui. A remote 'desk' in Edo without ever being there.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: The yashiki is the ledger where capital-side recognition is RECORDED (each acknowledged crate, each repair underwritten, each successful filtered petition posts an Influence event); official notations are the heaviest, rarest Influence events — bakufu ink is the apex acknowledgement, and it arrives mediated, unlocking the final Influence band.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE — the primary T5 conduit and how the tier is reached at all; the permit(s) gating the capstone and the climactic petition for formal merit acknowledgement are core. Sub-projects (funding the repair, the deepest creditworthiness) are optional accelerants. The protagonist's DISTANCE is non-negotiable.

factions_zones.name: The Lord's Sankin-kotai Attendance (the procession & the lord-as-proxy)
factions_zones.theme: The protagonist's hands reach Edo only inside the LORD's body of work: he equips, provisions, and burnishes the lord's mandatory biennial attendance, and the credit accrues to the lord — and thence to the house. The cleanest expression of mediated Influence. GROUNDED: sankin-kotai is the historically exact mechanism by which a rural house touched the capital; the protagonist never joins the audience — he provisions the man who does; folklore-free, pure institutional logistics.
factions_zones.first_encounter: Late T4, Chief Steward Genemon hands the protagonist a share of preparing the lord's biennial journey up the kaido — one verb: outfit one leg of the procession (provision a stage).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Provision a single stage (you touch the procession). → own the whole outfitting (retinue size scales with kokudaka — a legible status display you fund and dress). → send the meibutsu UP with the lord as gifts/tribute (the product rides the procession into Edo society). → receive 'how it landed' reports from Konoe (what the lord's peers said, which gifts opened which doors). → capstone: the lord returns from an attendance year visibly higher in standing because of what the house equipped him with, and says so — the single warmest mediated-recognition beat. A biennial cadence gives the upper tier a heartbeat.

key_npcs.name: Lord Kurosawa Munenori
key_npcs.role: Existing, wider role: the proxy himself — carries the house's face to Edo; his lifted standing IS the protagonist's Edo achievement; returns with weary, genuine acknowledgement that is never personal elevation.

key_npcs.name: Procession-master Saburozaemon
key_npcs.role: Existing senior retainer, promoted: marshals the retinue, teaches the brutal economics of a status procession, gatekeeps the outfitting quality tiers.

key_npcs.name: Heir Naoyuki
key_npcs.role: Existing, wider role: travels in the retinue as the house's future face; the rivalry-turned-brotherhood pays off as he reports back from Edo what the protagonist's work bought them. (Shared with the Tomita contrast role.)

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A periodic, expensive ATTENDANCE-OUTFITTING sink (procession scale, gifts, road logistics) tied to the existing porter/caravan supply chains from the origin faction (making T3 origin trade-ties directly useful at T5); routes the meibutsu into Edo as tribute; a biennial cadence.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: The protagonist spends koku/coin/goods on the lord's attendance; recognition lands on the LORD; the house's Influence rises by reflection. Explicitly NOT the protagonist's personal standing.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE — outfitting the attendance is a core T5 driver. Lavishness tier is an optional accelerant.

factions_zones.name: The Nihonbashi Wholesale & Edo Cultural/Banzuke Milieu (the meibutsu's gatekeepers and fame-makers)
factions_zones.kind: tier-local faction + NPC-cluster (wholesale web + fame-milestone shape)
factions_zones.theme: The two halves of the capital recognition of the house's product: the great wholesale houses who decide whether the meibutsu gets shelf-space (the commercial half), and the salons/gazetteers/ranking-chart (mitate-banzuke) culture that manufactures its FAME (the cultural half). The meibutsu must become not just sold but celebrated — all courted through agents, samples, and the rusui, never in person. (Merged: the facet's separate 'Nihonbashi houses' and 'banzuke milieu' entries, since they are the commercial and reputational faces of the one outcome.) GROUNDED: Nihonbashi tonya and houses like Echigoya/Shirokiya, and mitate-banzuke ranking charts + meibutsu gazetteers, are historically exact; fame was genuinely manufactured by writers, salons, and tastemakers; no supernatural element; honours the ceiling — the product is famous in rooms the protagonist will never enter, fame attaches to the goods and the house name, not the man; the entertainer's role is strictly the arts sense, reached only indirectly.
factions_zones.first_encounter: Commercial: a specialist tonya (wholesaler in the meibutsu's category) sends, via the rusui, a single trial order for a sample bale — one buyer, one inbound order. Cultural: Konoe forwards a single woodblock broadsheet — a meibutsu gazetteer or mitate-banzuke — on which a rival domain's product appears and the Kurosawa's does not (one artifact, one stinging absence).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Fill one trial order through Konoe (one tonya, one transaction). → the tonya names its terms and rivals — you learn the category's wholesale web; reach a great Nihonbashi house (Echigoya/Mitsui-scale) whose endorsement is a tier of legitimacy, courted with consistent quality and volume; secure a standing contract / the house's mon on the packaging (the meibutsu is now a branded Edo good); late, an optional bidding tension between rival tonya for exclusive supply. → Cultural: see the chart that omits you; learn its makers (a writer/publisher, a tea connoisseur, a celebrated entertainer whose use sets fashion); court taste not bribes (get the meibutsu into the right hands via the lord's attendance and salons); the product first APPEARS on a minor chart (a named, recorded fame beat); capstone — it CLIMBS toward the top ranks and a famous name publicly praises it. The remote Osaka→Edo supply chain (via the Finance network's kura-yashiki + fudasashi) is the abstract throughput board feeding both.

key_npcs.name: Tonya Izumiya Mohei
key_npcs.role: New: category wholesaler who places the first trial order; the meibutsu's first capital-side buyer and the read on what Edo markets want.

key_npcs.name: Banto Kahei
key_npcs.role: New: head clerk of a great Nihonbashi specialty house — the real decision-maker behind the famous shopfront; courted patiently; warms to reliability over flash.

key_npcs.name: Gesakusha Santo Bunzaemon
key_npcs.role: New: a popular Edo writer/publisher who compiles mitate-banzuke and meibutsu gazetteers; getting his pen on your side is a slow courtship of quality, never a payment.

key_npcs.name: Naka-gai Sokichi-no-Edo-en
key_npcs.role: New (echo of village peddler Sokichi, scaled up): an Edo middleman connecting provincial suppliers to Nihonbashi houses — the bridge NPC into the wholesale web.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: The Edo WHOLESALE market (trial orders → standing contracts → branding → exclusive-supply bidding; quality/volume/consistency as levers, not haggling; capital-market price discovery) AND a FAME/RENOWN meter advanced by chart placement + tastemaker endorsements rather than volume (a reputation layer ABOVE sales that multiplies every unit's worth and feeds back into demand). Deliberately abstract/remote (holdings/throughput + a fame progress bar), keeping focus on the home estate while the world widens.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Trade ties / inter-han / Osaka market (lever 7) made famous: a great house carrying the meibutsu with the house's mon on the packaging is loud public recognition; the meibutsu's chart-rank is the most legible single Influence display of the whole game — a published, ranked, capital-wide acknowledgement, the tier-5 spine's visible win condition, mirroring the house-rank ladder at grand scale.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE for the FIRST contract and the FIRST chart appearance (the meibutsu reaching and being named in Edo IS a tier-5 headline beat); the exclusive-supply bidding war, the deepest house-goodwill, and the top chart ranks are OPTIONAL accelerants that enrich but never block the capstone.

factions_zones.name: The Touring Inspector's Survey (junkenshi / metsuke) — the contested capstone set-piece
factions_zones.tier: T5 Edo (the survey reaches the domain; cross-tier in stakes)
factions_zones.theme: The single moment the abstract climb is JUDGED by a real human authority: a touring shogunal inspector whose periodic survey can validate or threaten everything the house has built — standing contested face to face (with the LORD, not the protagonist). The dramatized capstone and the canonical source of the documented service the Edo merit-commendation requires.
