GROUNDED: junkenshi circuits and metsuke surveillance of domains are historically exact mechanisms of contested standing; the protagonist stands behind the lord; recognition is read into a record, not personally bestowed; no folklore, no magic — institutional judgement honouring the ceiling absolutely.
factions_zones.first_encounter: Word travels down the kaido (via Konoe and the post-town network) that a junkenshi circuit will pass through the domain — one rumour, one looming date, one verb: prepare.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: The inspection is announced as a distant threat/opportunity. → learn what it weighs (fields, ledgers, order, the meibutsu, the people's condition — the whole game's accumulated work becomes the answer to one exam). → stage the domain's best face (the protagonist marshals what he's built; rival House Tomita stages its own display to outshine or undermine). → the inspection set-piece: the LORD and senior retainers face the inspector, the house's record is read aloud, the protagonist's work speaks but he stands at the back. → aftermath: a favourable report travels UP to Edo and becomes the documented service the capstone commendation is built on — or, if neglected, a recoverable dent that must be answered.

key_npcs.name: Junkenshi Toyama Saemon-no-jo
key_npcs.role: New (fulfils the foundation's pre-named 'Touring Inspector' apex slot): the touring shogunal inspector — the apex authority the protagonist ever comes near; surveys the domain, judges its order, decidedly not impressed by mere wealth; impartial, exacting.

key_npcs.name: Inspector's secretary Mabuchi
key_npcs.role: New: the record-keeper who writes the report that travels to Edo; the reachable human whose pen captures the house's worth — honestly informed, never bought.

key_npcs.name: Rival House Tomita
key_npcs.role: Existing rival, capstone role: stages its own display to win the inspector's favour — the inter-house rivalry's climactic, status-contested confrontation, decided by substance vs show. (Shared with the Tomita network.)

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A periodic INSPECTION set-piece: a scored review converting the house's standing accumulation (fields reclaimed, ledgers clean, meibutsu renowned, people content) into a single high-stakes recognition event, plus a preparation sub-loop. The one place the upper-tier abstraction becomes a tense, dramatized, human judgement rather than a rising bar.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: The largest single positive Influence swing in the game when met well — and the canonical SOURCE of the documented service the Edo merit-commendation requires. Neglecting it triggers a scripted, recoverable dent, preserving the 'can stall/dent, never wipe' rule.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE — the dramatized capstone confrontation and the origin of the merit record. Its preparation DEPTH scales with optional side-investment but the event itself is core.

expanded_world.how_they_layer_incrementally:
The cadence is governed by ONE rule that protects the locked fractal-incrementality pillar: never surface more than one or two NEW first-contacts per Influence sub-step, gate each behind a distinct sub-threshold + a story flag, and open the SPINE node of a tier FIRST so the rest drip as optional accelerants. The 18 nodes resolve into roughly 14 first-contact gates across the climb, because the 6 cross-cutting networks each have ONE first-contact and then re-reveal as deepening RUNGS at higher tiers rather than as new clusters — this is the key anti-overload move.

SEEDS (T1-T2, already in the starter region — FORESHADOWING, not new factions): the porter's-knot beat (Porter Guild seed, T1, zero bonus); Ranpo + Obaa Sato (Scholars seed); Gonta/Risuke/Onatsu/Tokuemon (Artisan seed); the Asagiri rice-broker (Finance seed); Priest Ryoa's shrine + register (Pilgrimage seed); Magobei's skim → Yagoemon's ledgers (Racket seed). The player learns SIX thread-shapes in miniature inside the cozy starter loop, each one node, before any expansion. None reads as a 'faction' yet — just a person and a verb.

T3 OPENING ORDER (the region band, hard-staggered): (1) the trade BACKBONE first — Porter Guild proper (Kanta's favour run) + the first sekisho pass + the first lowball broker sale (Heikichi) — because surplus + a way to move it + a way to convert it is the minimum spine. These three arrive close together but each is one contact/one verb. (2) Then the headline SPINE beat — Kuzuhara (survey the broken seki) — once memory + conditioning + standing align. (3) THEN, as optional accelerants dripping one at a time behind rising district-standing thresholds: the Wider Post-Town commercial layer (toiya registration), the Neighbouring Valleys (Hibara, then Toge-mura), the charcoal-burners (Tasuke, via Toge-mura), the matagi (Shozo, via Toge-mura/Genza), the regional Pilgrimage circuit (Ryoa's neighbouring-shrine offering), the Scholars' han-school. (4) Cross-cutting PRESSURE threads (Racket escalation to Kuroiwa/Zenroku/Hanzaki; Tomita's first underbid) are not 'opened' on a gate — they INTRUDE as story beats triggered by the player's expansion (the first raid loss, the first contested deal), so they feel like consequences of growth, not menu unlocks.

T4 OPENING ORDER (gated on distinguished service rendered): (1) the Townhouse beachhead first (the hub — one bare room) — nothing else in T4 is reachable until the introductions hub exists, exactly mirroring the T1 kura cold-open. (2) Then officialdom (the Daikan's summons) — the recognition-recording engine. (3) Then, dripping as the player has business that makes each relevant: Marutaya finance (debt restructuring), the entertainment district's introductions, the artisan kabu-charter + wholesale grading, samurai society + the academy. (4) PRESSURE: Kuroiwa-at-the-office and the racket-evidence track sharpen; Tomita contests broker terms and society standing. (5) DEEP/OPTIONAL/LATE: the marriage-adoption brokerage matures only once samurai-society standing is non-trivial.

T5 OPENING ORDER (gated on domain sponsorship + presentable standing, carried via the lord): (1) the sankin-kotai outfitting (seeded late T4) + the first Edo yashiki dispatch (Konoe's letter) — the two conduits — arrive first. (2) Then the meibutsu reaches Nihonbashi (one trial order) and the Osaka→Edo finance chain returns its first rice-bill — the wholesale + finance rungs. (3) Then the banzuke milieu (the chart that omits you) and bakufu-permits-via-rusui. (4) The touring-inspector survey is the CAPSTONE set-piece, announced as a distant date so anticipation builds, climaxing the tier and producing the merit record that flows DOWN through the lord. Throughout T5 multiple slow async loops (finance/wholesale/fame/attendance/correspondence) run in PARALLEL so something always resolves and no loop goes fully cold (mitigating end-game waiting tedium).

The felt experience: the player is never handed a new continent of content. They meet a thread as one face, grow comfortable, watch THAT thread enlarge as they climb, and only occasionally open a genuinely new tier-local frontier — and even then it is one valley, one office, one teahouse, one letter at a time.
expanded_world.integration_with_starters:
The expansion is built as concentric growth OUTWARD from the three starters — none is redesigned; each is the literal seed and shape-template for what blooms above it.

FROM THE ESTATE (gated rank ladder, main spine): the rank ladder itself is untouched (Stray → chief steward at the local tier). Above Rank 7 the climb stops adding personal ranks and instead tracks House Influence outward — exactly as §1.14 specifies. The estate's existing gatekeepers graduate into the expansion without new roles being invented: Genemon hands off the sankin-kotai outfitting (T5); Tanomo runs the debt-restructuring interface (Finance network); Lady Chiyo becomes the alliance-strategist (Marriage brokerage); Dowager Toku's flood-venture memory motivates Kuzuhara + the Scholars' engineering; Jubei's creed is restated at domain scale by academy-head Rai Genpachi; Tokujiro is seconded to staff the townhouse (re-homing the 'teach others from zero' layer); Naoyuki turns from internal rival into the ally against external Tomita and the Edo-attendance reporter. Crucially, the protagonist's achievements always convert to the HOUSE's / the LORD's standing, never personal elevation — the mediated-recognition ceiling is honoured by routing T4 recognition through the daikan and T5 through Konoe + Munenori.

FROM ASAGIRI (reputation WEB, side): the village's per-node web is the TEMPLATE replicated, slimmer, at district and castle-town scale — the Neighbouring Valleys are literally 'Asagiri, fractally, x N' (each a headman + shrine + craft + grievance); samurai society and the Nihonbashi houses reuse the same per-node patron-meter shape. The village's existing cast extends outward without redesign: Sokichi the peddler becomes the road-scale information broker and pilgrim-bridge (and his Edo echo Sokichi-no-Edo-en); Ryoa's single shrine + register becomes the regional Pilgrimage circuit + cross-valley evidence net; the village artisans (Gonta/Risuke/Onatsu/Tokuemon) are the base of the Artisan Lineage that climbs to the meibutsu; the rice-broker is the base of the Finance network; Magobei/Yagoemon are the entry rungs of the Racket; the inn's rumours-board motif survives as a single light T4 'castle-town ghost story' wink so the castle-town's deliberate folklore-absence still nods to the starter.

FROM SAWATARI-JUKU / ORIGIN (memory-gated support track, side): the origin is the engine room of the cross-cutting TRADE networks. Denbei/Kanta/Zenroku ARE the Porter Guild's first contacts and compromised apex; Okimi's trading family IS the Finance/Osaka-facing trade-tie multiplier; the porter-guild mates become the recruited idle carriers. The Wider Post-Town region is literally the OUTWARD commercial face of the existing reunion hub — registration needs standing, not the reunion, so the commercial road stays available to a spine-only player while the emotional content stays optional. Kuzuhara is the locked faction-3 fusion node (Origin payoff + spine river-works), access-only and grind-built, with Oharu/the real Tama and the cover-up resolving there.

The §1.5.4 multiplier intent is preserved exactly: village allies become idle producers seconded to estate/region works; origin trade-ties become the supply chains that make region/Edo expansion affordable; weaving both in roughly halves time-to-tier — felt, never a wall. The expansion never replaces the starters' currencies (Estate Service XP / Village Reputation / Origin Ties); it adds region/domain/Edo BANDS of the same House Influence macro-resource over the same three feeders.
expanded_world.scope_v1_vs_later:
V1 VERTICAL SLICE (per §1.14: Tiers 1-2 complete, T3 stubbed as the cliff-hanger): NONE of the 18 expanded nodes ship as full factions in v1. What v1 SHIPS is the six cross-cutting network SEEDS already embedded in the starter region (porter's-knot, Ranpo/Sato, Gonta-et-al artisans, the rice-broker, Ryoa's shrine+register, Magobei/Yagoemon's skim) — these are part of the T1-2 starter design, not new content, and they exist precisely so the expansion can build outward without retrofitting. The single T3 STUB shown as the cliff-hanger should be ONE node only — recommend the Porter Guild's first-contact (Kanta's favour run + the first sekisho turn-back), because it (a) directly pays off the protagonist's own porter past, (b) is the trade backbone everything else hangs from, and (c) demonstrates the fractal-incremental motion repeating at a new frontier with the least new art/systems. The estate's Rank 7 'gate to the wider world' opens onto exactly this one teaser, then ends.

EARLY ROADMAP (first post-v1 milestone — completes T3): the T3 SPINE pair (Kuzuhara fusion + the regional broker runway) plus the trade backbone (Porter Guild proper + pass-tiers + the Wider Post-Town commercial layer). This is the minimum that makes T3 a real tier rather than a stub, and it reuses the most existing cast. Then the T3 optional accelerants (Neighbouring Valleys, charcoal, matagi, regional Pilgrimage, Scholars han-school) and the cross-cutting pressure deepening (Racket to Kuroiwa, Tomita's first contests).

LATER ROADMAP (T4): Townhouse hub + Daikan's office (the spine pair) first, then Marutaya finance, entertainment district, artisan kabu-charter, samurai society — with the racket-evidence prosecution and marriage/adoption brokerage as the deepest optional layers.

FINAL ROADMAP (T5): the conduits (sankin-kotai + Edo yashiki), then Nihonbashi wholesale + Osaka-Edo finance chain + banzuke fame, then bakufu-permits-via-rusui, climaxing in the touring-inspector set-piece and the mediated merit commendation.

SCOPE DISCIPLINE: per the §1.13 number-range/scope guard, design ALL upper-tier nodes so they can ship FIRST as thin abstract holdings/influence/correspondence boards (the §1.14 'macro-tier spatiality' recommendation: abstract above T3) before any full sub-area art — especially Osaka (never visited; pure throughput board) and Edo (mostly correspondence + a fame bar). This keeps each roadmap milestone affordable and lets depth be added without bloating the first playable slice.
open_questions: Exact Influence sub-thresholds and the staggered unlock ORDER within each tier band (especially T3's busiest band) need a dedicated pacing pass — confirm the recommendation: open the spine node first, drip optional accelerants one-at-a-time behind distinct sub-thresholds + story flags, and let Racket/Tomita pressure INTRUDE on story beats rather than open on gates. This is the single biggest reveal-fatigue risk.

Conversion-ledger WEIGHTS across the now-deduped levers (a delivered freight contract vs a restructured debt vs a won petition vs a guild charter vs a meibutsu chart-rank vs the inspector swing) are deferred to the balance facet / §4 — and the ~halves-time multiplier budget must be apportioned across the 6 cross-cutting networks so they STACK sensibly without trivializing the climb.

WHICH product is the canonical meibutsu (silk/sericulture, sake, textile, lacquer, paper, or a regional rice brand)? This pins the Artisan Lineage endgame, the Finance/Porter payload, the Nihonbashi tonya category, the banzuke chart type, and the Edo tastemaker — should be locked before T4/T5 are authored in detail (it threads through five nodes).

A consistent SECONDED-ALLIES-COST rule is needed across ALL the new producer-recruits (Origin guild-mates, returning Kuzuhara families, matagi scouts, charcoal-burners, village allies seconded to the townhouse): do they count against their home node's production (a real loan) or are they free helpers? §1.14 flags this and the expansion multiplies it.

A per-NPC ARC MAP is needed for the heavily-reused existing cast graduating into 'wider roles' (Kanta, Denbei, Zenroku, Okimi, Sokichi, Ryoa, Risuke, Toku, Tanomo, Naoyuki, Oharu, Hanzaki, Kuroiwa, Naozane, Genza, plus the rusui Konoe as the new through-line) so each graduates coherently across tiers rather than being stretched thin.

Racket-arc resolution TIMING and personal risk: does the osso petition climax at T4 (touching the daikan) and merely glimpse the untouchable Edo factor at T5, and whose neck is on the line (protagonist, ally, or a gimin-style martyr)? Reconcile with the locked partial-justice ceiling and §1.14's 'do side-mysteries climax with the tier or earlier' question. Recommendation: partial T4 redress, untouchable apex glimpsed at T5.

Tier-seam confirmation: do the Finance network's paper/credit and Rival House Tomita belong wholly in their lower tier or straddle upward as designed (cross-tier bridges)? And does region (T3) vs domain (T4) stay split or merge per the §1.14 tier-count question? The whole curation assumes 5 distinct tiers with T3=trade/holdings/Kuzuhara, T4=castle-town social/financial seat.

Macro-tier SPATIALITY: do region/domain/Edo get walkable sub-area maps or abstract holdings/correspondence boards? The curation recommends abstract above T3 (and especially for never-visited Osaka and arm's-length Edo) to control scope — confirm, since it changes how 'arrive minimal' is presented.

Does being beloved across many valleys / running a big regional power base trigger the locked estate-vs-village jealousy tension at scale (the daikan or lord wary of a retainer's own power base)? Could be a rich late-T3/T4 tension or pure flavour — §1.14 open question, now amplified by the district bloc.

Folklore budget audit region-wide: the regional Pilgrimage circuit, charcoal fox-fire, matagi high-woods rumours, bandit 'mountain god', and the one T4 castle-town wink must collectively hold the locked ≤1 residual-ambiguity cap (the single capped beat staying at the Asagiri boundary-stone) with every other rumour resolving to a mundane cause — confirm the cap survives the expanded surface area.

Sensitivity pass needed for the marginalised mountain/poor groups (matagi, charcoal-burners, famine-band, Kuzuhara survivors) and for the entertainment-district/marriage-adoption content, so none read as 'resources to strip', 'wild people to tame', or culturally careless — binds the locked guardrail.

Big-number formatting becomes acute at T4-T5 (regional broker + multi-valley aggregation + Osaka/Edo finance push magnitudes up an order) — confirm whether abbreviated/scientific formatting is needed and that recognition-gating + the saturation-crash keep growth bounded and earned, not runaway (§1.13 / §1.14).

violations.constraint: Fractal incrementality — every node arrives MINIMAL, never overloads (the single most-stressed rule for THIS task)
violations.problem: Three cross-cutting networks violate the rule they claim to honour by smuggling tier-sized structures into one 'node.' The Rice & Silver Network's unlock list enumerates SEVEN financial verbs (sell -> forward-contract -> store -> rice-bills/paper -> credit -> debt-restructure -> goyokin -> bill-discount) PLUS market-saturation, diversified-goods hedging, a creditworthiness meter, kakeya auctions and Edo fudasashi — an entire economic simulator behind one first_encounter. The Porter Guild stacks routes + a 4-rung pass-tier ladder (single->standing->cargo->guarantor) + porter-roster management + route-risk (insurance/escort/reroute/toll) + seasonal closures + sukego corvee politics + horse-strings + a caravan-line holdings node. The Townhouse+Entertainment hub fuses a multi-room upgradeable home base + introductions + entertaining + an info-broker feed + a patron-of-arts angle. Sharing one first contact does not make these 'one node'; the per-node burden is a tier's worth of systems each — exactly the 'dump complexity at once' the constraint forbids.

violations.constraint: GROUNDED / NO MAGIC — folklore is light flavour; residual ambiguity capped at EXACTLY ≤1 off-screen mundane-readable token, fixed at the Asagiri boundary-stone (locked D-002 / PRD §1.13)
violations.problem: The expansion multiplies the folklore surface and only gestures at the cap in an open_question rather than holding it. It introduces new believed-omens at nearly every node: the 'one-eyed mountain god of the pass', the 'fox-fire ridge' kiln, matagi 'yamanba/tengu/mountain-god' rumours, the bandit 'mountain god', a Pilgrimage 'miracle cure', per-valley 'small kamikakushi-flavoured rumours' across an unbounded N of valleys, AND a NEW 'one permitted T4 castle-town ghost story wink' the PRD never authorised. Sanctioning a brand-new T4 ambiguity beat is by construction a second residual token; canon fixes the single token at the boundary-stone. The sheer volume of debunk-beats also courts the Scooby-Doo failure canon explicitly warns against.

violations.constraint: Do not redesign / contradict the locked cast; build OUTWARD from canon NPCs
violations.problem: The expansion labels NPCs 'Existing' that are NOT in the authoritative PRD §1.8 cast. GENZA is tagged 'Existing region NPC' and given a load-bearing bridge role to the Matagi, but Genza appears ONLY in the explicitly SUPERSEDED grounded-story-spine doc (the tengu ex-foot-soldier); grep confirms 0 hits in docs/prd.md. Reviving a superseded-canon NPC as current canon is a direct contradiction. The foundation also says the starters' casts are 'already designed; do NOT redesign,' yet the expansion silently RENAMES a canon-adjacent NPC ('Onui' -> 'Osato') and hands many starter NPCs sweeping cross-tier portfolios with no arc-map (it flags the over-stretch risk in open_questions but ships it anyway).

violations.constraint: Lock dependencies before authoring; the meibutsu product is an OPEN question (PRD §1.13/§1.14, not yet chosen)
violations.problem: Five nodes (Artisan Lineage endgame, Finance payload, Porter payload, Nihonbashi tonya category, banzuke chart + Edo tastemaker) are authored as if the canonical meibutsu is settled, while canon explicitly leaves WHICH product is the meibutsu unresolved (the expansion even re-flags this in its own open_questions). The most load-bearing T5 content — the visible win condition, the meibutsu's chart-rank — is therefore not actually specifiable yet; building the T4/T5 spine payload on an unlocked variable is designing on sand.

violations.constraint: Scope discipline / v1 vertical slice = Tiers 1-2 complete, T3 STUBBED (locked §1.14); number-range/scope guard (§1.13)
violations.problem: The total designed surface is 18 multi-system nodes (an admitted dedupe of ~37) with ~50 named NPCs, a 7-NPC antagonist web, a rival house, and a 3-NPC capstone set-piece, with six parallel async loops at T5. This is a multi-year content mountain presented as 'the broader world' and directly triggers the canon's own scope-explosion guardrail. The v1 carve-out is correct (only the 6 seeds ship, already part of T1-2), but the WORLD this commits the project to building remains badly bloated.

weaknesses: Spine-vs-side is muddied by a new in-between label ('SPINE-ADJACENT'/'SPINE-ENABLING'/'SPINE-CRITICAL') on the Porter Guild, Townhouse hub, Daikan's office and Finance network. The canon binary is: spine completable thin-but-complete, ALL side content optional and never blocking. Declaring the Porter Guild and Townhouse de-facto mandatory ('cannot export surplus without some logistics'; 'nothing in T4 reachable until the introductions hub exists') quietly converts side infrastructure into soft gates. Defensible only if each 'thin form' is provably trivial — which is asserted, not demonstrated.

The Racket is called 'STRUCTURALLY woven into the spine' and 'the one rotten root,' and dismantling it is said to 'feed the spine by REMOVING friction' — but canon (PRD §1.10, §1.5.2) makes the lost-child/racket mystery an OPTIONAL side-thread that never gates tiers. The friction-removal framing risks making racket progress feel necessary for efficient play, eroding its optional status; 'always achievable by economic means alone' is asserted but not shown.

The 'halves time-to-tier' multiplier is a LOCKED single budget for the TWO side factions combined. The expansion now has SIX cross-cutting networks plus many tier-local clusters all claiming to be 'optional accelerants.' Stacking 6+ accelerants against a ~2x budget is a real balance contradiction: either most are near-worthless (why build them) or together they blow past 'felt, never a wall' and trivialise the grind that IS the dominant intended behaviour. The doc defers this to a budget-apportionment open_question rather than solving it.

Reveal-fatigue at T3 is named 'the single biggest reveal-fatigue risk' but not solved: after the spine pair, the T3 band still introduces six optional accelerants (Post-Town, Neighbouring Valleys, charcoal, matagi, Pilgrimage circuit, Scholars han-school) PLUS two intruding pressure threads (Racket escalation, Tomita) — crowding far more than 'one or two new first-contacts per sub-step.'
Neighbouring Valleys is unbounded ('as many as make sense', district roll-up, alliance bloc) with no cap on N. Each valley is 'Asagiri fractally xN' — a recipe for samey content sprawl.

Combat surface quietly grows (Hanzaki fight, famine-band mercy-vs-force, matagi expeditions through 'terrain that kills', high-route bandit security). Tone stays consistent with 'combat is the rare exception,' but the aggregate weight should be checked against the 'peaceful labour is the daily texture' pillar.

Upper-tier nodes assume an 'abstract holdings/correspondence board' presentation to control scope, but macro-tier spatiality is still an OPEN question in canon (§1.14). The recommendation is sensible but is presented closer to decided than it is.
fixes: CUT the node count hard. Beyond the 3 starters, a sane buildable set is ~6-8 nodes, not 18. Recommended keep-set, all reusing existing cast: (1) Porter Guild (trade backbone; pays off Tahei's porter past), (2) Rice & Silver finance network (the conversion engine), (3) the Racket meta-track (the one antagonist web, already half-canon), (4) Kuzuhara river-works (locked faction-3 fusion; headline T3 spine beat), (5) Daikan's Office (T4 recognition engine), (6) the Edo yashiki/rusui + sankin-kotai conduit cluster (treat as ONE), (7) Tomita as the single status rival, (8) ONE meibutsu producer line (Artisan Lineage collapsed to just the meibutsu chain). MERGE Nihonbashi/banzuke and the touring-inspector set-piece into the T5 conduit cluster as its two payoff beats, not standalone factions.

DEMOTE or CUT: Neighbouring Valleys (hard-cap at exactly 2 named valleys, Hibara + Toge-mura; forbid open-ended N), Matagi, Pilgrimage Order (most folklore-dense and fully optional — defer), Scholars & Physicians as a *network* (keep Ranpo/Obaa Sato as the existing seed only; do not build a han-school faction), and the Marriage/Adoption brokerage (canon itself flags adoption as possibly exceeding v1 scope). Reintroduce later deliberately per the 'start lean, add back' preference.

FIX canon-fidelity errors: remove or re-label every NPC tagged 'Existing' that is absent from PRD §1.8. Genza must be reintroduced as an explicitly NEW invented NPC (and even then justified against the folklore budget) or cut. Stop renaming canon NPCs ('Onui'->'Osato'); give genuinely new NPCs non-colliding new names and leave canon names untouched. Produce the per-NPC arc-map the doc flags as missing BEFORE assigning any canon NPC a cross-tier portfolio.

ENFORCE fractal incrementality at the node level, not just the first-contact level: for each kept node, cap to ONE new system-verb per Influence sub-threshold and explicitly defer deeper layers (paper/credit/goyokin for finance; pass-tiers/roster/route-risk for porters; introductions/entertaining/info-broker for the townhouse) to later sub-thresholds with their own story flags. Rewrite the unlock lists as a strictly staggered drip with named gates instead of a full-system menu.

HOLD the folklore cap explicitly: state that the single residual-ambiguity token stays at the Asagiri boundary-stone and DELETE the invented 'T4 castle-town ghost-story wink' (the castle-town's value is being the rational counter-world). Provide a region-wide belief->cause table for every new omen (pass god, fox-fire, matagi yokai, bandit god, miracle cure) confirming each fully resolves with zero residual ambiguity, before authoring those nodes.

LOCK the meibutsu product with the human before authoring any T4/T5 payload node (it threads through 5 nodes). Until locked, author those nodes against a placeholder and mark them blocked-on-decision rather than detailing tonya category / banzuke chart / tastemaker.

PROVE the 'thin spine' for every node labelled spine-adjacent/enabling/critical by writing the literally-minimal path (e.g. protagonist's own pass + one route + one broker; no roster, no paper, no introductions-luxe) and confirming it carries a player to the next tier, so these never become disguised gates. Re-classify everything else strictly as optional, matching the canon binary.
APPORTION the 'halves-time' multiplier explicitly across the kept accelerants (a fixed budget split) so the stack cannot exceed ~2x, and confirm the grind stays the dominant intended behaviour.
verify.scope_assessment: Bloated. 18 multi-system nodes + ~50 NPCs + a 7-NPC antagonist web + a rival house + a 3-NPC capstone set-piece is a multi-year content mountain dressed as 'the broader world.' The dedupe from ~37 to 18 is genuine and the SHAPE taxonomy (route/web/milestone/meta-track) and spine-through-line are strong, but the deduped result is still far too much and several 'one node' entries are entire economic/social simulators. For a sane v1 + early roadmap, cut to ~6-8 nodes that reuse the existing cast and pay off the spine directly: Porter Guild, Finance network, the Racket meta-track, Kuzuhara, the Daikan's Office, the Edo conduit cluster (yashiki/rusui/sankin-kotai with Nihonbashi/banzuke/inspector folded in as payoff beats), Tomita, and ONE meibutsu producer line. Defer or cap the rest (Neighbouring Valleys at 2; cut Matagi/Pilgrimage/Scholars-as-faction/Marriage-brokerage for now). This matches the locked v1 scope (T3 stubbed to a single Porter Guild first-contact, which the doc itself recommends) and the user's stated 'start lean, add back deliberately' preference. The v1 carve-out is sound; the problem is the world it commits the project to building afterward.
verify.verdict: FLAGGED — does not pass as-is. The expansion is thoughtful, mostly grounded, and preserves the locked tier/lever/ceiling architecture with real craft (the SHAPE taxonomy and spine-through-line are genuinely strong). But it trips several hard constraints: (1) it violates its own most-stressed rule — fractal incrementality — by packing tier-sized system stacks into single 'nodes' (Finance's 7 verbs; the Porter Guild's pass+roster+risk+caravan layers; the Townhouse mega-hub); (2) it expands the folklore surface beyond the locked ≤1-ambiguity cap and invents an unauthorised T4 'ghost-story wink'; (3) it mislabels superseded-canon NPCs (Genza) as 'Existing' and renames a canon NPC, contradicting the do-not-redesign rule; (4) it authors five T4/T5 payload nodes on the still-unlocked meibutsu decision; and (5) the total scope is bloated to ~18 multi-system factions. Net: SCOPE BLOAT plus per-node overload are the dominant problems. Cut to ~6-8 spine-paying nodes that reuse the canon cast, hard-cap the per-node drip to one-verb-per-threshold, lock the meibutsu first, hold the folklore cap, and fix the canon-fidelity errors before this graduates from brainstorm to design. Key files: /Users/raynos/projects/games/incremental-game-1/docs/prd.md (authoritative §1 — §1.5 starters, §1.6 the 7 levers + 5 tiers, §1.10 optional-folklore rule, §1.13-§1.14 scope guards/open questions) and /Users/raynos/projects/games/incremental-game-1/docs/history/decisions.md (D-001..D-006; note D-004 reset is reversed by PRD §1.14). The superseded /Users/raynos/projects/games/incremental-game-1/brainstorms/2026-06-25-grounded-story-spine.md is where Genza/Kiku originate and is NOT canon.

facets.scope: Tier 3 (Region) — the cluster of factions/zones/NPC-clusters that open as the Kurosawa estate's House Influence reaches beyond Asagiri valley. Designed strictly WITHIN the locked KAMIKAKUSHI foundation (PRD §1, ADRs D-001…D-006): grounded/no-magic, mediocre-start/no-hidden-edge, open-ended quests, fractal incrementality, no reset, everything ties to House Influence and the estate-rise spine; side content optional and never blocks the spine. T3 entry gate (per PRD §1.6): village anchored + a tradeable surplus + roads/porters under estate control + enough standing to travel the controlled roads. Builds OUTWARD from the 3 starters (Estate / Village of Asagiri / Origin: Sawatari-juku) without redesigning them. Edo plausibility checked via WebSearch (sukego corvée 2 porters+2 horses/100 koku, toiya/tonya shipping agents, honjin/waki-honjin, sekisho/tegata, matagi hunters who also burn charcoal & float firewood downriver, kuramoto/fudasashi/nakagai rice brokers, kurayashiki, rice-bills, the road toward Osaka/Dojima at T4).
facets.summary: Eleven T3 region-tier factions/zones/NPC-clusters that bloom outward from the home valley once the estate is the valley's anchor. They form a coherent regional layer: a ring of NEIGHBOURING VALLEYS & allied/rival villages (a reputation-web cousin of Asagiri, sukego-corvée backbone); the WIDER POST-TOWN REGION around Sawatari-juku (the kaidō waystation network, honjin/toiya, the wider world of the Origin faction's town); the PORTER & TRANSPORT GUILD (the logistics meta-system that makes all regional trade possible — fuses Origin's guild thread up a tier); the SEKISHO CHECKPOINT & TEGATA-PASS network (the road-control friction layer that gates travel and is bought down with standing); the re-foundable UPSTREAM KUZUHARA (Origin/spine fusion node — resettlement labour base + the embankment river-works); a REGIONAL SHRINE/TEMPLE CIRCUIT & PILGRIMAGE ROUTE (folklore-light reputation across the region, the priest-register evidence network spanning valleys); the CHARCOAL-BURNERS' GROUNDS (sumiyaki sub-economy + firewood river-floating); the MATAGI HUNTERS of the high range (wilderness allies, a guarded hunting compact, expedition safety); the BANDIT/RŌNIN MENACE of the pass (the danger gradient & road-security spine pressure, Hanzaki's network); the REGIONAL RICE & GOODS BROKER NETWORK (the kuramoto/fudasashi/nakagai paper-economy that turns surplus into Influence and is the runway to T4 Osaka); and RIVAL HOUSE TOMITA, a competing goshi/merchant house (the inter-house status rival woven across the whole region). Each is itself fractal-incremental (you arrive at ONE node/contact/verb, then slowly unlock its people/places/systems as story beats), each ties to House Influence (region-band) and the estate-rise, and every side strand is confirmed non-blocking to the spine. Two are spine (Kuzuhara fusion + the broker runway to T4); the rest are optional accelerators/colour, tuned per the locked side-multiplier (weaving them in roughly halves time-to-next-tier — felt, never a wall).

factions_zones.name: The Neighbouring Valleys (allied & rival villages of the district)
factions_zones.theme: The estate stops being 'the big house in one valley' and becomes a presence across a CLUSTER of valleys — each a small Asagiri-cousin (a headman, a shrine, a craft speciality, its own grief and grievances) the estate can court, supply, arbitrate, or out-compete. Asagiri itself is the model; this is that model, fractally, at district scale.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE node only: a single down-valley hamlet, Hibara, sends its headman's son to Asagiri to beg the now-anchored estate to broker its surplus (one verb: agree to take Hibara's first consignment). You never 'open the whole district' — you meet one valley.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Hibara contact → after one fulfilled consignment, Hibara's per-valley goodwill meter appears and its tiny shop-row opens (1 craft speciality: ramie/hemp cloth). → Hibara goodwill threshold reveals a SECOND valley on the district map (Tōge-mura, a mountain-mouth village whose speciality is timber/charcoal access — a bridge to the charcoal-burners & matagi). → each valley unlocks the SAME shape Asagiri taught (headman regard + per-shop standing + per-family help + a craft-guild node) but slimmer, so it's familiar not overwhelming. → a 'district standing' roll-up bar fades in only after 2+ valleys are courted, then later a 'valley alliance' beat (a shared festival, a joint relief-rice pool) that converts the cluster into one bloc the estate speaks for. Rival valleys (those leaning to Tomita) appear as contested meters you can flip by out-supplying or out-arbitrating, never by force.

key_npcs.name: Headman Kyūbei of Hibara
key_npcs.role: First neighbouring-valley contact & gatekeeper; cautious, proud, owes a rice debt he fears the estate will weaponise (echoes Yagoemon's shape, not a copy) — warm quest-giver who tests whether the rising house extracts or uplifts.

key_npcs.name: Onui
key_npcs.role: Hibara's ramie-weaver and the district gossip-spine; the 'Sayo of the wider valleys' — tutorializes neighbouring-valley reputation and carries news of which valleys lean Tomita.

key_npcs.name: Headman Tarōzaemon of Tōge-mura
key_npcs.role: Mountain-mouth village headman; the human gateway to Tōge-mura's charcoal grounds and the matagi compact; gruff, transactional, respects delivered results.

key_npcs.name: Genrokurō
key_npcs.role: A rival-leaning headman down-valley quietly contracted to House Tomita; the contested-meter NPC you win over by out-performing, the legible face of the inter-house contest at village level.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds a district-wide reputation WEB (the Asagiri web, multiplied across valleys), each valley a craft speciality the estate can aggregate, route, and take a cut of; a 'district standing' roll-up; a valley-alliance bloc lever. Feeds the broker network (aggregated surplus) and the porter guild (more routes). Carries the locked estate-vs-village tension upward: high-rank duties may demand extracting quota from a courted valley (Influence up, valley goodwill down) with an always-available 'soften it quietly' option.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Each courted/allied valley is a recognized expansion of the house's reach → region-band House Influence on each alliance/contract sealed (the authority that 'recognizes' it is the district's headmen and, later, the daikan noticing a house that speaks for a bloc). Being the broker-anchor for a cluster of valleys is a direct conversion-ledger lever (trade ties + patronage/alliance).
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SIDE / optional accelerator. Courting valleys speeds Influence and unlocks craft/trade depth but is never required: a spine-only player can reach T4 through the estate's own surplus + the broker runway. Confirmed non-blocking. (Tuned as part of the ~halves-time multiplier.)
factions_zones.grounded_note: Pure socio-economics: headmen, surplus, debts, brokered consignments, festival diplomacy. Folklore-light: each valley carries its OWN small kamikakushi-flavoured rumour (a vanished traveller, a 'cursed' field) that, if pulled, resolves to a mundane cause — and several feed the regional priest-register evidence net. No magic; the contest with rival valleys is commercial and reputational only.

factions_zones.name: The Wider Post-Town Region (Sawatari-juku's outer world & the kaidō waystation network)
factions_zones.theme: The Origin faction's home town opened in T3 as Tahei's personal reunion; this is its OUTWARD, commercial face as a regional node — the post-town's honjin/waki-honjin, its toiya (transport office), its market, and the chain of smaller waystations up and down the kaidō. Where the estate stops being a buyer-from-afar and becomes a recognized trading house with a presence on the road.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE verb at ONE node: once the dream-gate + travel-standing open Sawatari-juku for the Origin reunion, a single additional door appears — the toiya master will register the estate's mon to receive a standing porter-and-horse allocation IF the estate posts a guarantor. That's the only regional handle at first.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Toiya registration → unlocks the waystation MAP as a thin line (Sawatari-juku only) with a freight-throughput meter. → first fulfilled freight run reveals the NEXT waystation up the kaidō (a half-day stage) and its tiny market. → reputation with the toiya unlocks the waki-honjin (the estate may now lodge there with status — a recognition beat) and a 'regional information broker' service (paid rumours that surface district opportunities & Tomita's moves). → late: a branch agent / consignment office the estate keeps at Sawatari-juku (a holdings node), and the honjin becomes reachable only for ceremonial recognition, never daily use. Each stage is one new dot on the line, never the whole road at once.

key_npcs.name: Toiya-master Chōbei
key_npcs.role: Runs the post-town transport office; the primary regional-trade gatekeeper — registers the estate's allocation, gates the waystation network and freight throughput; coolly professional, loyalty bought by reliable volume.

key_npcs.name: Sister Okimi (existing — wider role)
key_npcs.role: The Origin faction's married-into-a-trading-family sister becomes the estate's NATURAL in at the post-town's commerce — her family's manifests legitimise the estate's consignments. Access-only bridge, zero mechanical gift; existing NPC reused, not redesigned.

key_npcs.name: Waki-honjin keeper Itoya Han'emon
key_npcs.role: Keeper of the sub-honjin; a status-gatekeeper whose recognition of the estate (lodging it with honour) is itself a small Influence recognition beat.

key_npcs.name: Sokichi (existing peddler — wider role)
key_npcs.role: The village peddler graduates into a road-scale information broker across the waystation chain; grounded source of cross-valley rumour and the human thread between Asagiri and the wider region. Reused, not redesigned.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds the freight-throughput / waystation logistics layer (the physical road over which the porter guild operates), a paid regional information-broker service, a branch consignment office (first holdings node), and status-lodging recognition beats. The commercial supply-spine that the broker network and the neighbouring valleys both feed into and draw from.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: A registered mon on the toiya's books, a standing porter allocation, and lodging at the waki-honjin are all RECOGNITIONS of the house's regional standing → region-band Influence. Directly powers the 'trade ties' and 'road works' conversion levers; the road presence is the literal canvas the T3 Influence number sits on.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: MIXED. The post-town reunion is Origin-side (optional support track); but the toiya/waystation TRADE layer is a primary runway to surplus-export and thus part of the practical spine to T4. Designed so the Origin-emotional content stays optional while the commercial road stays available even to a spine-only player (registration needs standing, not the reunion).
factions_zones.grounded_note: Built on attested Edo institutions: toiya/tonya shipping agents, honjin/waki-honjin official inns, sukego-supported porter-and-horse allocation, status-marked lodging. No magic; the 'information broker' is gossip and paid manifests, not prophecy.

factions_zones.name: The Porter & Transport Guild (regional logistics meta-system)
factions_zones.theme: The logistics engine of the whole region — the body that actually MOVES the estate's surplus along the kaidō. Fuses the Origin faction's porter-guild thread (Tahei's old life) up a tier into a standing institution the estate must contract, ally with, and eventually co-direct. This is where 'roads/porters under estate control' (the T3 entry gate) becomes a live system.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE contact: Tahei's old friend Kanta (existing Origin NPC), now a journeyman porter, offers to run the estate's FIRST consignment off the books as a favour. One route, one porter, one verb (dispatch a load). No guild UI yet — just a friend with a carrying-pole.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Kanta's favour run → success reveals the 'routes' panel with exactly one route (Asagiri→Sawatari-juku) and its risk/throughput/cost. → contracting a SECOND porter reveals porter-roster management (assign carriers, balance load vs. speed vs. sukego-corvée availability). → guild standing unlocks horse-string hire, then a formal contract with the guild (volume discount, priority dispatch). → the sukego obligation surfaces as a real constraint and lever (the rate ~2 porters + 2 horses per 100 koku — the estate can petition for relief, or, as it rises, help ORGANISE the sukego for the district, a recognition beat). → late: the estate co-finances a guild branch or its own caravan line; recruited Origin guild-mates become idle producer-carriers; route security ties into the bandit/rōnin menace (insured vs. raided cargo).

key_npcs.name: Kanta (existing — wider role)
key_npcs.role: First porter contact and the human face of the guild; childhood-friend warmth becomes the on-ramp to the whole logistics system. Recruitable as a lead carrier. Reused, not redesigned.

key_npcs.name: Master Denbei (existing — wider role)
key_npcs.role: Tahei's old employer; the legitimate-manifests-and-know-how source becomes the estate's senior logistics ally — supplies route knowledge and guild introductions. Access-only, ZERO mechanical bonus (he taught the porter's-knot; still no stat gift). Reused, not redesigned.

key_npcs.name: Guildmaster Zenroku (existing — wider role)
key_npcs.role: The guild boss who wrote Tahei off; now the institutional GATEKEEPER/obstacle of the guild — extracts terms, resents the rising house, and is the reachable culprit whose contraband-on-tax-routes thread ties the guild into the regional racket (defeated by leverage/evidence, not violence). Reused, not redesigned.

key_npcs.name: Sukego-tedai Mohei
key_npcs.role: The minor official who allocates assisting-village corvée porters; a friction NPC the estate negotiates with, petitions for relief from, or eventually helps coordinate (a recognition beat).

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds the ROUTES/logistics meta-system: route map, porter & horse-string roster, throughput vs. cost vs. risk, guild contracts & discounts, the sukego corvée constraint/lever, cargo insurance vs. raid risk, and a co-financed caravan line (holdings). This is the connective tissue every other T3 trade strand routes through.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Moving recognized surplus to market is the conversion step itself — a fulfilled, acknowledged consignment converts koku/goods into region-band Influence; a formal guild contract and helping organise the district sukego are explicit recognitions. Powers 'trade ties' + 'road works' levers and is the practical bottleneck on T4 (no Osaka shipping without a logistics spine).
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE-adjacent / largely required in practice. You can't export surplus without SOME logistics, so a thin version is on the critical path; but its DEPTH (full roster mgmt, caravan line, guild co-direction, Origin guild-mate recruits) is optional accelerator. The Origin-emotional layer stays optional; the bare route stays available via standing alone.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Directly modelled on attested systems: toiya-dispatched porters/horses, the sukego corvée rate, kaidō freight, indentured/guild porter culture, contraband on tax-rice routes. No magic; route risk is bandits, weather, and washed-out fords, all mundane.

factions_zones.name: The Sekisho Checkpoints & Tegata-Pass Network (road-control friction layer)
factions_zones.theme: The bakufu/domain control over movement made into a tangible, climbable friction system. Free travel is impossible without standing (the locked premise that the controlled kaidō gates the Origin faction); at T3 the checkpoints stop being a wall and become a LADDER the estate climbs via passes, guarantors, and recognition.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE gate, ONE obstacle: the first time the estate tries to send goods or the protagonist past the home-valley sekisho, he's turned back for lack of a tegata (travel pass). The only verb is 'obtain a pass' (request one from the estate's standing).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: First single-use tegata (obtained via estate rank) → opens exactly one onward stage. → repeated lawful passage + estate standing unlocks a STANDING pass for the protagonist (no more per-trip begging). → then cargo tegata for consignments (the guild routes stop being throttled). → guild/estate guarantor status lets the estate VOUCH for its own carriers (a recognition beat: the checkpoint trusts the house's mon). → late: the estate's word smooths a whole route, and a contested checkpoint (one Tomita-influenced, one racket-linked) becomes a small evidence/leverage thread rather than a payment. Each step removes one specific friction, revealed as a story beat ('the barrier-keeper waves your mon through now').

key_npcs.name: Barrier-keeper Sōbei
key_npcs.role: Keeper of the home-region sekisho; the gatekeeper who turns you back, then over time the man who waves the estate's mon through — a living progress bar of the house's recognized standing.

key_npcs.name: Aratame-yaku Kiheiji
key_npcs.role: The pass-inspector at a stricter down-kaidō barrier; stricter gate that needs higher standing / cleaner manifests — surfaces the contraband tension (the racket moves goods these inspectors are bribed to ignore).

key_npcs.name: Clerk Naozane (existing — wider role)
key_npcs.role: The guilt-sick clerk who issues/forges pass paperwork becomes a reachable conscience inside the checkpoint bureaucracy and a documentary crack in the racket. Reused, not redesigned.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds the travel-permission gating layer: single-use → standing → cargo → guarantor tegata tiers; each tier unthrottles a specific movement (the protagonist, then goods, then carriers, then routes). It's the friction that makes the porter guild's throughput and the post-town trade FELT as earned access, not free.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Each pass-tier earned is a recognition of standing → small region-band Influence beats, and removing route friction multiplies the guild/broker conversion. The checkpoint trusting the house's mon is one of the cleanest diegetic 'the books now show the house is somebody' beats.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE-adjacent (gate, not content). It paces the spine (you must climb the pass tiers to export and to reach the post-town/region), but it's friction the spine naturally retires, not optional side content. The contested-checkpoint EVIDENCE thread is optional flavour and never blocks passage (you can always pay/wait).
factions_zones.grounded_note: Built on attested sekisho barrier-stations and tegata travel-permits; controlled inter-domain movement is the authentic mechanism (per the locked spine note). No magic; smoothing the road is standing, paperwork, and the occasional bribe/leverage — all mundane.

factions_zones.name: Kuzuhara — the re-foundable upstream hamlet & embankment river-works
factions_zones.theme: The geographic and emotional KNOT of T3: the drowned upstream hamlet (the disaster that nearly killed Tahei and is one root of the racket) becomes a re-foundable resettlement node AND the site of the embankment (seki) river-works that 'secures the disaster so it cannot recur.' Origin-thread payoff fused with a spine Influence project — exactly the locked faction-3 fusion (access-only, grind-built).
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE site, ONE verb: with memory returned + conditioning + standing to range, the protagonist reaches the broken embankment and can do exactly one thing — clear/survey the ruined seki. No town, no producers yet; just a scarred site and a single labour verb.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Survey the broken seki → reveals the river-works PROJECT bar (a multi-stage embankment rebuild — the Tier-3 river/road-works Influence lever) AND the resettlement-site stub. → first works stage stabilises the channel and unlocks the FIRST resettlement plot (one returning family / one Origin recruit as an idle producer). → each works stage names more of the drowned (a temple-register/grief beat) and adds a resettlement producer slot. → completing the embankment 'secures' the valley (a major recognition beat) and converts Kuzuhara into a small standing producer-base + the backstory's resolution (the cover-up evidence surfaces here). Strictly fractal: a ruin → a wall → a plot → a hamlet, never handed whole.

key_npcs.name: Oharu / the real Tama (existing — wider role)
key_npcs.role: The living lost child, connected to Kuzuhara's resettlement and the rice-debt cover-up that drowned it; her choice whether to be part of the re-founding is the costly-and-incomplete emotional payoff. Reused, not redesigned.

key_npcs.name: Returning Kuzuhara families (cluster)
key_npcs.role: Scattered survivors/descendants who, as the embankment is secured, return as resettlement labour — idle producers seconded to the new node. Grind-built, access-only.

key_npcs.name: Carpenter Risuke (existing — wider role)
key_npcs.role: Village builder graduates to the river-works/construction lead for the embankment stages — gates the building system at regional scale. Reused, not redesigned.

key_npcs.name: Dowager Toku (existing — wider role)
key_npcs.role: Estate backstory-keeper whose knowledge of grandfather Sadamune's failed flood-venture makes Kuzuhara's works the house ATONING for its own root-sin — ties the project to the estate's restoration. Reused, not redesigned.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds a multi-stage river-works construction project (the canonical Tier-3 flood-control lever), a new resettlement producer-base (idle producers, a fresh small economy), and the backstory-resolution evidence layer. A genuine new canvas node, not a menu.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: The embankment is a flagship 'distinguished service / river-works' conversion lever — securing it is RECOGNIZED by the district/daikan and is a large region-band Influence award, and arguably the cleanest bridge beat toward T4 (flood-control service is exactly the T4 entry gate). The resettlement adds standing producers feeding the climb.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE (one of the two clearly-spine T3 nodes). It's a headline T3 beat per PRD §1.6 and a natural T4 gateway. The Origin-emotional resolution layered on it stays optional in TONE but the works themselves are core. Confirmed: pursuing it never requires completing optional side-mysteries first (memory-gate is access, not a side-quest wall).
factions_zones.grounded_note: No magic anywhere: the disaster was a neglected embankment diverted to protect tax fields (already locked canon); the rebuild is corvée + engineering + koku; 'naming the drowned' is grief-work and a temple register, not a rite that 'works'. Folklore-light: the 'cursed valley' belief resolves to the documented negligence.

factions_zones.name: The Regional Shrine/Temple Circuit & Pilgrimage Route
factions_zones.theme: Folklore-light reputation and soft-power across the region: a chain of valley shrines and a small Buddhist temple on a minor pilgrimage route. The estate earns regional GOODWILL and a respectable name by patronising rites, sponsoring road/shrine upkeep, and (optionally) the priest-register evidence network that spans valleys. The region-scale cousin of Asagiri's single shrine.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE node: Priest Ryōa (existing Asagiri NPC) asks the now-prominent estate to send an offering to a neighbouring valley's neglected shrine for the regional Bon — one verb (sponsor the offering). No circuit yet, just one favour to one shrine.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: First sponsored offering → reveals a 'regional goodwill / piety' soft meter and a second shrine node on the circuit. → patronising rites & repairing a shrine path unlocks the next shrine and a modest reputation perk (smoother dealings with that valley's headman — soft-power, never stats). → the pilgrimage route itself appears as a thin line linking shrines (foot-traffic the estate's waystations & goods benefit from). → the priest-register EVIDENCE net (Ryōa's register of the vanished, now cross-referenced with other valleys' temples) becomes an optional investigation layer spanning the region — hard evidence for the racket, the lost-child thread, and the road-death toll. Each shrine added is one small story beat about that valley's grief.

key_npcs.name: Priest Ryōa (existing — wider role)
key_npcs.role: Asagiri's shrine-keeper becomes the protagonist's introduction to the regional clergy and the keeper of the cross-valley register-evidence net. Reused, not redesigned.

key_npcs.name: Abbot Tetsugen
key_npcs.role: Head of the small pilgrimage-route temple; the regional clergy gatekeeper whose endorsement lends the estate a respectable, pious name (soft Influence) and who curates the register of vanished travellers across the road.

key_npcs.name: Pilgrim-guide Osen
key_npcs.role: A devout itinerant who leads the route; a grounded source of cross-region rumour and the human face of the pilgrim foot-traffic the estate's road presence serves.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds a regional 'piety/goodwill' soft-reputation meter, shrine/path-upkeep sponsorship sinks (koku/goods → recognition), pilgrim foot-traffic that lightly boosts waystation trade, and a cross-valley register EVIDENCE network for the optional mysteries. Soft power, never combat or stats.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Sponsoring rites and upkeep, and being endorsed by the temple, are RECOGNITIONS of the house as a worthy regional patron → modest region-band Influence (the 'patronage & alliance' lever in its pious form). A respectable name greases headman/broker/official dealings, indirectly accelerating the climb.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SIDE / optional. Pure accelerator + colour + optional-mystery evidence; a spine-only player skips it entirely and loses only soft-power and lore. Explicitly confirmed: no rite and no shrine node ever gates tier progression (binds the locked folklore rule).
factions_zones.grounded_note: Carefully distinguishes Shinto shrines/rites from Buddhist temple/Bon/register per the locked folklore decision; respectful grief-coping texture. Every omen on the circuit resolves to a human/natural cause; NO rite ever mechanically 'works'; the register is paperwork, not divination. Counts toward, never exceeds, the ≤1 residual-ambiguity cap (the one capped beat stays at Asagiri's boundary-stone).

factions_zones.name: The Charcoal-Burners' Grounds (sumiyaki sub-economy)
factions_zones.theme: A scattered community of mountain charcoal-burners (sumiyaki) on the forest fringe between the valleys and the high range — the source of the 'fox-fire on the ridge' rumour debunked into a hidden kiln. At T3 they become a real industrial sub-economy (charcoal + firewood floated downriver) the estate can organise, supply, and take a cut of.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE contact, ONE verb: a lone charcoal-burner, Tasuke, comes down to Tōge-mura to trade and offers the estate a single load of charcoal at a poor price (buy his one load). One kiln, one man, one transaction.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Buy Tasuke's load → reveals a charcoal supply node + a poor 'fox-fire ridge' rumour you can investigate (debunks to his hidden kiln — a grounded reveal, builds his trust). → trust unlocks the wider scattered kiln community and a charcoal sub-economy meter. → the estate can provision the burners (better tools/rice up front for guaranteed output — a classic by-employment organisation lever) → unlocks firewood RIVER-FLOATING (float cut wood down the snowmelt river to sell downstream — an attested matagi/mountain practice → a new throughput line for the porter guild & broker). → late: the estate becomes the district charcoal aggregator, a renowned-product seed (charcoal/iron-fuel toward a domain meibutsu at T4).

key_npcs.name: Charcoal-burner Tasuke
key_npcs.role: First contact & gatekeeper to the burner community; the 'fox-fire' debunk in person; suspicious of lowland houses until the estate proves it provisions fairly rather than skims.

key_npcs.name: Old Kama
key_npcs.role: The eldest kiln-master who holds the community's trust and the firewood-floating know-how; gates the river-float line and the aggregation deal.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds a charcoal/sumiyaki sub-economy (a spend→produce→sell loop), the provisioning/by-employment organisation lever, and a firewood river-floating throughput line that plugs into the porter guild and broker. Seeds a renowned-product (meibutsu) candidate for T4.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Organising a dispersed industry into a recognized estate-run by-employment is a textbook 'cash crops & proto-industry' conversion lever; aggregated, branded charcoal recognized by the broker/market converts to region-band Influence and seeds the T4 meibutsu story.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SIDE / optional accelerator. A solid Influence lever and product-seed, but the estate can climb via rice/cloth alone; skipping it costs throughput and a meibutsu option, never blocks the spine. Confirmed non-blocking.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Entirely grounded: real sumiyaki + firewood-floating mountain economics; the 'fox-fire' is a hidden kiln (already locked belief→cause). No magic; the burners are poor labourers with dignity, organised fairly or exploitatively at the player's choice (carries the extract-vs-uplift tension).

factions_zones.name: The Matagi Hunters of the High Range
factions_zones.theme: A proud, semi-closed band of mountain hunters (matagi) of the high cedars and snow-range — masters of terrain that kills outsiders. At T3 they're the gateway to safe high-mountain expeditions, rare mountain goods (hides, bear-fat medicine, game), and a guarded compact; the grounded reality behind the 'yamanba/tengu/one-eyed mountain god' high-woods rumours. NOT a combat-power source for the protagonist (no-hidden-edge holds).
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE contact, ONE verb: the matagi don't recruit — a lone hunter, Shozo, found near Tōge-mura, will only let the estate buy ONE bear-fat salve and warns him off the high range. The single verb is a respectful trade; trust is the whole gate.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: First respectful trade → a thin 'matagi regard' meter (earned by fair dealing, gifts of rice/iron, and NOT trespassing). → regard threshold: a hunter will GUIDE one high-range expedition (safe passage through terrain that otherwise kills — the matagi as living expedition insurance). → deeper regard unlocks a hunting/forage compact (rare goods supply: hides, medicine, game for the broker), then a SHARED-RANGE agreement (the estate respects their grounds; they secure a high route for the porter guild against the bandit menace). → late: a matagi 'shrine-of-the-mountain' courtesy beat (folklore-light, respectful) and recruited hunter-scouts who make pass expeditions survivable. Their high-range knowledge debunks the 'mountain god' rumours one expedition at a time.

key_npcs.name: Matagi Shozo
key_npcs.role: First contact; the wary trade-and-warning gatekeeper who slowly becomes the estate's high-range ally if treated with respect, not as a resource to strip.

key_npcs.name: Matagi headman Kuranosuke
key_npcs.role: Leader of the band; gates the hunting/forage compact and the shared-range agreement; embodies a second, dignified effort-over-talent model (skilled by a lifetime of mountain work, never magic).

key_npcs.name: Genza (existing — wider role)
key_npcs.role: The fugitive ex-foot-soldier 'tengu' of the high cedars becomes the bridge BETWEEN the estate and the matagi — a wary teacher, witness, and the human debunk of the high-woods rumour. Reused, not redesigned.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds a 'matagi regard' trust track, expedition-guide/safe-passage (high-range exploration & logistics safety), a rare mountain-goods supply line for the broker, a shared-range agreement that secures high routes against bandits, and recruited hunter-scouts. Crucially: utility (access, safety, goods, scouts) — NOT a personal-combat upgrade.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Their goods feed the broker (trade-ties lever); their secured high routes protect throughput (road-works/security); a formal compact with a respected mountain band is a recognition beat (patronage/alliance). Indirect but real Influence, all access/utility, zero hidden-edge.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SIDE / optional. Gates the deepest wilderness exploration and the pass route's safety, but those are optional frontiers; the spine to T4 runs through trade/works, not the high range. Confirmed non-blocking. Pairs with the bandit menace as the wilderness counter-weight.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Grounded matagi culture (terrain mastery, bear-fat medicine, game, charcoal links, a respectful mountain-courtesy) handled with dignity, never 'wild people to tame' (binds the locked sensitivity note). Their skill is lifelong labour, not innate gift (binds no-hidden-edge / talent-foil rules). All high-woods 'yokai' resolve to mundane causes.

factions_zones.name: The Bandit / Rōnin Menace of the Pass (road-security pressure)
factions_zones.theme: The danger gradient and the antagonist pressure on the whole regional trade layer: famine-driven bandits and a small network of masterless rōnin preying on the fog-blind pass and lonely kaidō stages — fronted by Hanzaki, the 'one-eyed mountain god.' They make routes risky, cargo raidable, and the protagonist's mediocre body's limits FELT. The reason logistics, matagi safe-passage, and road-works matter.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE event, not a faction-board: the estate's first real consignment is partly raided on the pass — reported, off-screen at first (a loss line in the route ledger), with Hanzaki's name attached as rumour. The only initial 'verb' is to respond (escort, reroute, or pay tolls).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: First raid loss → reveals a route-RISK/security layer (insurance, escort hire, reroute, toll). → investigating the raids unlocks the bandit network as discrete, reachable nodes (a desperate famine-band you may FEED/resettle as a mercy quest — not a kill — vs. Hanzaki's hardened enforcers tied to the racket). → securing a stage (escort + matagi shared-range + a road-works repair) measurably drops that route's risk (a recognition beat). → the Hanzaki thread escalates as a recurring PHYSICAL danger the protagonist survives by labour-built endurance, never out-talents (the locked talent-foil). Resolution is leverage/evidence + the osso-racket thread, with the powerful patron above Hanzaki largely escaping (partial justice).

key_npcs.name: Hanzaki (existing — wider role)
key_npcs.role: The scarred rōnin enforcer & 'one-eyed mountain god'; recurring physical threat and the talent-gone-rotten mirror — trained/brutal, never innate; survived, never out-fought. The face of road-insecurity. Reused, not redesigned.

key_npcs.name: The famine-band under Gonpachi
key_npcs.role: Desperate displaced farmers turned road-robbers; a reachable, humanised cluster the player can feed/resettle (mercy quest) rather than slaughter — echoes the yamanba-widow mercy framing at regional scale.

key_npcs.name: Tedai Kuroiwa (existing — wider role)
key_npcs.role: The deputy magistrate's agent whose racket Hanzaki enforces; the patron-above-the-enforcer who largely escapes — the regional human antagonist defeated by evidence, not violence. Reused, not redesigned.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds the route-security layer (risk %, raid losses, insurance, escort hire, reroute, toll), a mercy-vs-force resolution choice on the famine-band, and the Hanzaki recurring-danger thread feeding the racket/osso evidence line. The pressure that gives the porter guild, matagi, and road-works their stakes.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Securing routes is a 'road works / distinguished service' lever — a recognized-safe road earns region-band Influence and unthrottles trade conversion. Cracking the racket (evidence) is a major distinguished-service beat that helps bridge toward T4's 'service to the domain' gate.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: MIXED. SOME road-security is on the practical spine (raids throttle the surplus export you need for T4), so a thin engage-the-risk layer is near-required; but the DEPTH (the full bandit-network investigation, the mercy resettlement, the Hanzaki/Kuroiwa racket arc) is optional and never blocks passage — you can always pay tolls/insure/reroute. Confirmed.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Fully grounded: famine-driven banditry and rōnin road-predation are period-real; combat stays idle/dangerous and gated behind conditioning (no-hidden-edge); the 'mountain god' is a man with a lookout (locked belief→cause); justice is partial and the untouchable patron escapes (locked). Mercy-not-kill for the desperate band binds the sensitivity guardrail.

factions_zones.name: The Regional Rice & Goods Broker Network (the paper-economy runway to Osaka)
factions_zones.theme: The financial heart of T3 and the literal runway to T4: the network of rice/goods brokers (nakagai middlemen → a regional kuramoto-style warehouse agent → the road toward Osaka/Dojima) that turns the estate's physical surplus into recognized value, paper (rice-bills), credit, and eventually a renowned market presence. Where 'numbers go up' becomes financial, not just agricultural.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE contact, ONE verb: a travelling middleman, nakagai Heikichi, offers to buy the estate's first exportable surplus at a lowball regional price (sell one surplus lot). No exchange, no paper, no warehouse yet — one buyer, one sale.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: First lowball sale → reveals a 'broker standing / price' meter and the saturation/price-crash mechanic at REGIONAL scale (flooding one good craters its price — the locked market-saturation rule, scaled up). → standing unlocks a better broker tier (a regional warehouse agent / proto-kuramoto who STORES surplus for a fee and pays fairer prices). → unlocks RICE-BILLS (kome-kitte paper the estate can hold, trade, or borrow against — credit appears: the 'debt restructuring' lever turns from begging to creditworthy). → diversifying goods (rice + cloth + charcoal + mountain goods) hedges the saturation crash and raises standing. → late: a sealed standing contract with the warehouse agent and a glimpse of the Osaka/Dojima exchange up the road — the explicit T4 gateway. Each tier is one new financial verb (sell → store → paper → credit → contract).

key_npcs.name: Nakagai Heikichi
key_npcs.role: First broker contact & lowball buyer; the on-ramp to the paper-economy; sharp, opportunistic, won over by reliable diversified volume.

key_npcs.name: Warehouse-agent Kuraya Jinbei
key_npcs.role: The regional kuramoto-style warehouse master; gates storage, fair pricing, rice-bills, and credit; the institution whose sealed contract is a major recognition beat and the bridge toward Osaka. Cautious, ledger-minded, the financial counterpart to the estate's clerk Tanomo.

key_npcs.name: Clerk Tanomo (existing — wider role)
key_npcs.role: The estate's accountant graduates to managing the rice-bill/credit/debt-restructuring interface with the broker network — the in-house thread that turns the strangling old usurious loan into proper merchant finance. Reused, not redesigned.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds the financial meta-layer: broker standing & price meters, regional market-saturation/price-crash, warehouse storage-for-fee, rice-bills (tradeable paper), credit/borrow-against-paper, debt restructuring (usurious loan → merchant finance), diversified-goods hedging, and the sealed standing contract. The single clearest mechanical runway from T3 surplus to T4 Osaka.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: This is the primary CONVERSION engine of T3: surplus only becomes Influence when the broker/market RECOGNIZES it (a recorded sale, a sealed contract, a held rice-bill, restructured debt = the house is creditworthy). Hits four conversion levers at once (cash crops/proto-industry, debt restructuring, trade ties, patronage). Crossing into a standing warehouse contract is the cleanest T4 (domain/Osaka) bridge beat.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE (the second clearly-spine T3 node, with Kuzuhara). Some broker is unavoidable to convert surplus and reach T4; its DEPTH (paper, credit, full debt-restructuring, diversification, the Osaka glimpse) is the practical spine of the financial climb. Side strands (which broker, how aggressively to diversify) stay optional.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Built directly on attested institutions: nakagai middlemen, kuramoto warehouse masters, fudasashi-style credit-against-rice, kurayashiki warehouses, rice-bills, and the Osaka/Dojima exchange as the T4 horizon. No magic; the 'numbers go up' is honest Edo finance, and the saturation-crash keeps it from being free money.

factions_zones.name: Rival House Tomita (competing goshi/merchant house)
factions_zones.theme: The inter-house STATUS rival woven across the entire region (and onward into T4): a competing goshi/merchant house that prospered exactly as the Kurosawa fell — by talent + luck + ruthlessness, never innate gift (the locked talent-foil rule). They contest every regional prize the Kurosawa reach for: valleys, broker terms, routes, the daikan's favour. A rival to OUT-MANEUVER, never to kill.
factions_zones.first_encounter: ONE collision, not a faction-board: the estate's first attempt to court a neighbouring valley or sign a broker contract is quietly underbid by 'House Tomita' — a name on a competing manifest. The only initial handle is the contested deal itself (win it or lose it commercially).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: First underbid → Tomita appears as a single rival name. → repeated contests reveal a 'house standing vs. Tomita' relative meter across the region (valleys leaning, routes contested, broker terms compared). → an emissary beat introduces Tomita's heir/agent in person (negotiation, not duel). → the rivalry deepens into discrete contests (a courted valley, a broker exclusivity, a road-works credit, the daikan's notice) each won by out-performing — out-supplying, out-pricing, out-arbitrating, building goodwill they neglect. → late: detente, a forced alliance, or a clean commercial defeat, and the rivalry CARRIES into T4 as the domain-tier status competitor. Crucially: Tomita is also a foil that shows ruthlessness has a cost (a valley they squeezed flips to the Kurosawa for being fairer).

key_npcs.name: Tomita Sōzaemon
key_npcs.role: Head of the rival house; shrewd, established, dismissive of the upstart Kurosawa — the strategic adversary across the region whose every advantage is shown as bought/lucked/ruthless, never innately superior (binds the talent-foil rule).

key_npcs.name: Tomita's agent Yasubei
key_npcs.role: The rival's regional fixer who underbids deals, courts the same valleys, and leans on brokers; the legible day-to-day face of the contest the player actually out-maneuvers.

key_npcs.name: Rival heir Naoyuki (existing — contrast role)
key_npcs.role: The estate's own heir, now an ALLY against Tomita — the internal talent-foil-turned-brother contrasts the external rival; his converted-not-innate growth mirrors the thesis. Reused, not redesigned.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds a relative 'house standing vs. rival' meter, contested-deal mechanics across valleys/brokers/routes/officials (zero-sum prizes won by performance, not violence), and a detente/alliance/defeat resolution that carries to T4. A competitive pressure that gives the whole T3 economy stakes and direction.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Every contest won is a recognized regional gain at the rival's expense → region-band Influence; the daikan noticing the Kurosawa edging out an established house is a bridge beat toward T4's 'be reckoned with by those who rule.' The rivalry directly shapes the Influence climb's pace and texture.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: MIXED, leaning SIDE. The rivalry COLOURS the spine (Tomita contests prizes you'd want), but no single Tomita contest is mandatory — you can climb by growing your own pie rather than beating theirs in every instance. Confirmed: losing a Tomita contest never hard-blocks the spine, only slows/reshapes it. The deepest rivalry arc is optional.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Pure status/economic competition between two real-type houses (goshi/merchant); no magic, no innate-talent gap (Tomita's edge is capital, connections, ruthlessness — all matchable by work, binding the talent-foil guardrail). Defeated/out-maneuvered commercially and politically, never by a power fantasy; partial outcomes honoured.

risks: T3 introduces ELEVEN new clusters — a real risk of reveal-fatigue that violates the fractal-incremental promise. Mitigation/discipline required: hard-stagger their first-contact gates across the whole region band (each behind a distinct Influence sub-threshold + a story flag), never surface more than one or two new clusters per Influence step, and confirm the spine (Kuzuhara + broker) opens first with the rest dripping as optional accelerators. A balance/pacing pass must define the exact unlock order and thresholds.

Heavy reuse of existing NPCs in 'wider roles' (Kanta, Denbei, Zenroku, Okimi, Sokichi, Ryōa, Risuke, Toku, Tanomo, Naoyuki, Oharu, Hanzaki, Kuroiwa, Naozane, Genza) is good for legibility but risks overloading single characters across tiers. Need an explicit per-NPC arc map so each graduates coherently rather than being stretched thin.

Financial layer (broker network: rice-bills, credit, saturation-crash, debt restructuring) could drift into city-builder/4X spreadsheet tedium — the locked guardrail against exactly this. Must stay diegetic and story-framed (Tanomo's voice, recognition beats), with the cozy daily-labour texture still dominant.

Number-range blowup: regional broker + multi-valley aggregation + Osaka horizon pushes koku/Influence magnitudes up an order. Confirm big-number formatting and that the saturation-crash + recognition-gating keep growth bounded and earned, not runaway.

Several clusters carry the extract-vs-uplift tension (neighbouring valleys, charcoal-burners, matagi, famine-band). If the 'soften it quietly / mercy' option is always strictly better or strictly worse, the choice collapses. Tuning must make both leans viable and in-character, per the locked allegiance rule.

Spine vs. side classification: I marked Kuzuhara and the broker network as spine, and several others (post-town trade, porter guild, sekisho, road-security) as 'spine-adjacent / mixed.' There's a risk the practical critical path quietly REQUIRES too many 'optional' systems (you can't export without logistics+passes+some security). Needs an explicit audit that a true spine-only player has a thin-but-complete path to T4 through these, with all DEPTH genuinely optional.

Folklore budget: the regional shrine circuit, charcoal fox-fire, matagi high-woods rumours, and bandit 'mountain god' all add belief→cause beats. Must hold the ≤1 residual-ambiguity cap region-wide (the single capped beat stays at Asagiri's boundary-stone) and keep every regional rumour resolving to a mundane cause, debunked with dread not gotcha.

Sensitivity load: matagi, charcoal-burners, famine-band, and Kuzuhara survivors are all marginalised mountain/poor groups. Sustained care required so none become 'resources to strip' or 'wild people to tame' — each must keep dignity and agency (binds the locked guardrail).
open_questions: Exact unlock ORDER and Influence sub-thresholds for the eleven clusters within the region band — which open first (recommend: sekisho-pass + porter guild + broker as the trade backbone, then Kuzuhara as the headline spine beat, then valleys/post-town/charcoal/matagi/shrine as accelerators, with bandits/Tomita as cross-cutting pressure throughout). Needs a dedicated pacing pass.
