Region vs. domain tier boundary: do the broker network's paper/credit and Rival House Tomita belong wholly in T3, or do they straddle into T4 (Osaka exchange, domain-tier status contest)? I designed them as cross-tier bridges — confirm the seam.

How abstract should the regional map be (PRD open question on macro-tier spatiality)? Do neighbouring valleys / waystations / Kuzuhara get walkable sub-areas, or a holdings/influence board with a thinner location set? This shifts how 'arrive minimal' is presented.

Seconded-allies cost (PRD open question, now multiplied): recruited Origin guild-mates, returning Kuzuhara families, matagi scouts, charcoal-burners — do these count against their home node's production (a real loan), or are they free helpers? Needs one consistent rule across all T3 producer-recruits.

Does the estate-vs-village jealousy penalty (PRD open question) scale to the region — e.g. does being beloved across many valleys make the daikan or the lord wary of a retainer building his OWN regional power base? Could be a rich late-T3 tension or pure flavour.

Sukego-corvée depth: is the assisting-village porter obligation a real managed constraint/lever (petition for relief, help organise the district sukego as a recognition beat) or background flavour? Affects how heavy the porter-guild system gets.

Which T3 product becomes the renowned-meibutsu seed carried to Edo (charcoal? a specific cloth? a regional rice brand?) — this should be chosen now since it threads through broker, valleys, charcoal-burners, and the T4/T5 payoff.

Hanzaki/Kuroiwa racket arc resolution timing: does the regional racket (and the osso petition) climax at T3, or stay partially open as a bridge into T4's domain-official chain? Reconcile with the locked partial-justice and the PRD's 'do side-mysteries climax with the tier climb or earlier' open question.

Confirm v1 scope treatment: per PRD §1.14, T3 is currently 'stubbed as the cliff-hanger.' Which (if any) of these eleven clusters are part of the v1 stub vs. the post-v1 roadmap? This whole facet may be roadmap-tier content pending that scope lock.

facets.scope: Tier 4 — the Domain / Castle-Town tier. Factions, zones, and NPC-clusters of the domain seat and its castle-town: domain officialdom (daikan's office, tedai, magistrate's apparatus, rice/finance administration), great merchant & money-lending houses (rice-broker/kuramoto-style, silk & sake wholesalers, the domain-finance kakeya), artisan quarters & chartered craft guilds (za / kabu-nakama), a castle-town townhouse for the Kurosawa estate, the inter-han / Osaka-facing market, samurai society (other retainers + a domain academy), and a castle-town entertainment/social district where standing and introductions are brokered. Built OUTWARD from the 3 locked starter factions; each itself fractally incremental; tied to House Influence (家威) and the estate-rise spine; grounded/no-magic; side content never blocks the spine. Houses the racket/cover-up antagonist Tedai Kuroiwa plausibly at the domain level.
facets.summary: T4 is the moment the Kurosawa stop being a valley curiosity and become a name the people who actually rule must reckon with. Entry is gated (per the locked §1.6) on DISTINGUISHED SERVICE rendered to the domain (flood-control, surplus rice in a lean year, or a formal case won), so the castle-town opens as a reward, not a purchase. I designed NINE T4 factions/zones plus one cross-tier network anchor, each arriving MINIMAL (one contact, one node, one verb) and unfolding fractally into its people and systems. They cluster into five social estates that mirror the real castle-town: (1) DOMAIN OFFICIALDOM — the Daikan's office and its tedai, the magistrate's apparatus (machi-bugyō), and the rice/finance administration (kanjō-kata); this is where Tedai Kuroiwa lives, his quota-skim racket now scaled to the domain ledger, defeated by evidence not violence and shielded from above to honour the partial-justice ceiling. (2) THE GREAT MERCHANT HOUSES — the domain-finance house (a kakeya/goyō-shōnin who can restructure the strangling grandfather-era debt from usury into proper finance), the rice-broker/warehouse interests, and the silk/sake wholesalers who route Asagiri's meibutsu toward Osaka; money flows here, and the debt-restructuring + goyōkin levers are the spine's T4 economic heart. (3) THE ARTISAN QUARTER & CHARTERED GUILDS (za / kabu-nakama) — a higher craft tier than the village, gating master commissions, a guild charter for the house's meibutsu, and quality/branding that lifts the product's worth. (4) THE KUROSAWA CASTLE-TOWN TOWNHOUSE — the estate's beachhead in town, a literal upgradeable node that is the player's home-base for all T4 introductions, staffing, and entertaining. (5) SAMURAI SOCIETY — the domain's other retainers and the domain academy (hankō), where standing, etiquette, and alliances (marriage/adoption) are won, and where rival House Tomita schemes. Plus the OSAKA-FACING / INTER-HAN MARKET (the kuramoyashiki warehouse-residence link) that turns meibutsu into renown and seeds T5, and a cross-tier MARRIAGE & ADOPTION ALLIANCE BROKERAGE that spans T2–T5. The castle-town ENTERTAINMENT/SOCIAL DISTRICT is the connective tissue: a teahouse/ryōtei quarter where introductions across all the above are brokered, gating who you can even meet. Every node feeds House Influence through authentic upward-mobility levers (debt restructuring, meibutsu reaching Osaka, distinguished service, goyōkin, guild charter, marriage/adoption alliance) and is optional-where-side / spine-where-load-bearing. Folklore is near-absent here by design (the castle-town is the rational, ledgered world the valley's superstition is contrasted against), kept to one light "castle-town has its own rumours" wink so the rumours-board motif survives without re-importing yokai.

factions_zones.name: The Daikan's Office (代官所) — domain officialdom & the racket's nerve-centre
factions_zones.theme: Being reckoned with — and watched — by the men who actually administer the land. The polite, ledgered face of power, with rot underneath: the same quota machine that menaced the valley, now seen from the top of the funnel. Bureaucracy as both ladder and trap.
factions_zones.first_encounter: A single summons. After the T4 entry-gate service is rendered, a low tedai delivers a sealed note ordering the Kurosawa's representative to present himself at the daikan's office — one node (the receiving anteroom), one verb (present credentials / answer questions).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Arrive minimal: you are kept waiting in the anteroom and dealt with by junior clerks (one contact: the courteous, careful Tedai Kuroiwa). STEP 1 — repeated correct conduct + your service record earns an audience with the daikan proper (Daikan Iemasa), unlocking the 'standing with officialdom' meter. STEP 2 — the rice/finance sub-office (kanjō-kata) opens as a node: you may now have the house's reclaimed yields and contracts FORMALLY RECORDED (the koku→Influence conversion at domain scale). STEP 3 — the magistrate's apparatus (machi-bugyō-sho) opens: the petition/case hall where formal disputes and the osso-style petition are heard. STEP 4 (deep, optional, late) — the audit/evidence sub-thread: cross-referencing recorded yields against quota ledgers exposes the skim; the racket-evidence track unlocks. Each step is one new door inside the compound, announced as a story beat. Officialdom is never overloaded — you meet maybe three named officials at first and learn the rest only as your business with them becomes real.

key_npcs.name: Tedai Kuroiwa
key_npcs.role: The deputy-administrator's senior agent (tedai); the polite, patient, dangerous primary regional antagonist. Your first and main contact at the office; outwardly your gracious facilitator, secretly the local architect of the tax-quota skim and smuggling cover-up, who opposes the Kurosawa rise because a creditworthy, well-documented house starts asking where the rice went. Defeated by accumulated evidence (rigged masu, doctored tallies, the guilt-sick clerk's testimony) routed ABOVE him, never by the sword.

key_npcs.name: Daikan Iemasa
key_npcs.role: The deputy magistrate himself — overworked, status-conscious, not personally corrupt but incurious and dependent on Kuroiwa to make his numbers; signs what he is handed. The apex you must impress for officialdom standing; embodies how negligence dressed as competence shields the guilty above.

key_npcs.name: Clerk Naozane
key_npcs.role: A guilt-sick junior clerk in the kanjō-kata who transcribed the lies (carried up from the cast); the crack in the cover-up and a reachable conscience who supplies documentary proof — the human key that makes the racket-evidence track resolvable.

key_npcs.name: Inspector (Metsuke / touring official)
key_npcs.role: The senior, hard-to-reach domain/circuit inspector to whom the over-the-head petition is ultimately made; the apex authority embodying the honoured ceiling — reachable only at the very top of T4/into T5, and only partial justice is ever won.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Formal RECORDING of achievements (the explicit koku→House Influence conversion at domain scale: a recorded yield, a sealed contract, a registered reclamation); the petition/case system (win formal disputes for Influence; the osso-style petition as the high-risk capstone); a 'standing with officialdom' meter gating which offices you may approach; and the optional racket-EVIDENCE track (collect/cross-reference proof, sway witnesses) that resolves the cover-up. Bureaucratic friction is a soft cost — wrong etiquette or low standing slows recording.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: This is the authority that RECOGNISES T4 achievements, so it is where most T4 House Influence is actually minted (distinguished service acknowledged, contracts sealed, a won case). The racket sub-thread ties the estate's backstory (grandfather Sadamune's covered-up disaster), the village lost-child mystery, and the origin caravan into the 'one rotten root,' and a partial win against Kuroiwa lifts Influence while the magistrate above largely escapes — the ceiling thesis made structural.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE-CRITICAL for the formal-recognition mechanic (you cannot bank T4 Influence without engaging officialdom) — but the RACKET-EVIDENCE prosecution sub-thread is OPTIONAL side content and never blocks the spine; a player may rise to creditworthiness and Osaka renown without ever taking down Kuroiwa (it costs only justice and some Influence, never the climb).
factions_zones.grounded_note: Pure administrative realism: daikan/tedai/kanjō-kata/machi-bugyō are attested Edo institutions; the racket is mundane fraud (rigged kyō-masu measuring-box, doctored tally-sticks, short-weighted shipments). NO folklore here at all — the castle-town is deliberately the rational, ledgered counter-world to the valley's superstition; that contrast is the point.

factions_zones.name: Marutaya — the domain-finance merchant house (掛屋 / 御用商人)
factions_zones.theme: Turning a strangling, usurious inheritance into honest creditworthiness. The merchant who is more powerful than most samurai but must never say so; money as the real engine of the castle-town, and proper finance as the lever that lifts a dying house.
factions_zones.first_encounter: One introduction, brokered. Once the townhouse exists, a soft-spoken factotum leaves Marutaya's card; the first node is the house's front counter and one verb — request an interview about the Kurosawa's old debt.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Arrive minimal: you deal only with a clerk at the counter, who quotes terms you cannot yet meet. STEP 1 — proving the estate's books are honest (carried up from clerk Tanomo's work) earns an interview with the master, Marutaya Sōbei, unlocking the 'creditworthiness' meter. STEP 2 — DEBT RESTRUCTURING opens: convert the grandfather-era usurious loan into a proper merchant/domain loan (a multi-stage sub-loop: present collateral = recorded yields → negotiate terms → service the new loan). STEP 3 — once solvent, the relationship inverts: Marutaya begins to court the Kurosawa, opening the GOYŌKIN lever (a strategic 'contribution' that buys formal standing) and merchant-backed working capital for expansion. STEP 4 (deep) — Marutaya becomes the financial counterparty for the Osaka shipping push (letters of credit / rice-coupon settlement), tying into the inter-han market node. You meet new staff (the appraiser, the Osaka correspondent) only as each deal makes them relevant.

key_npcs.name: Marutaya Sōbei
key_npcs.role: Master of the domain-finance house — shrewd, courteous, quietly the most powerful commoner in town; appraises men as coldly as ledgers. Begins as a wary lender, becomes a patron once the house proves honest and useful. The grounded face of merchant power that out-ranks samurai in fact but never in form.

key_npcs.name: Onui
key_npcs.role: Marutaya's sharp daughter / heir-presumptive who actually runs the back office; a possible marriage-alliance thread (cross-tier brokerage) and a pragmatic ally who values demonstrated competence over birth — another effort-over-talent mirror, commoner-side.

key_npcs.name: Kichibei
key_npcs.role: The house's grain appraiser and Osaka correspondent; gates the quality/price the Kurosawa meibutsu can command and the credit-settlement mechanics for shipping to Osaka.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: DEBT RESTRUCTURING (the canonical 'liability → solvency → creditworthiness' sub-loop, one of the §1.6 Influence levers); a creditworthiness meter that lowers borrowing cost and unlocks working capital; the GOYŌKIN contribution lever (spend accumulated coin/koku for a formal standing bump); merchant-backed finance that makes the Osaka shipping and region/Edo expansion AFFORDABLE; and price-appraisal mechanics for the house's product.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Directly mints Influence via two of the named conversion levers — debt restructuring (the single most thematically important T4 lever, since the grandfather's debt is the house's original wound) and goyōkin. Solvency is also a hard PRE-CONDITION the officialdom and samurai-society tracks check, so finance underpins the whole tier.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE-LOAD-BEARING: restructuring the strangling debt is the defining T4 economic beat and a stated headline beat for the tier — but it is achieved through play, not forced as a single quest, and the deeper goyōkin/Osaka-credit layers are paced, not gated walls. The marriage thread via Onui is optional flavour/alliance, never required.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Kakeya / goyō-shōnin / fudasashi-style finance, rice-coupon settlement, and goyōkin are all attested Edo practices; debt restructuring of a samurai house by a merchant creditor is historically true to life (samurai indebtedness to merchants was endemic). No magic, no folklore — just the cold arithmetic of money.

factions_zones.name: The Kuramae Rice-Warehouse Quarter & Brokers (蔵前 / 蔵元・札差筋)
factions_zones.theme: Where rice becomes silver and where the domain's lifeblood is stored, weighed, and skimmed. The logistics underbelly that connects the honest field to the corrupt ledger — the physical place the racket's stolen koku actually passes through.
factions_zones.first_encounter: One node, one verb: the warehouse weighing-yard, where the estate's first formally-shipped surplus is measured in and a receipt issued. You meet one warehouse master (kuramoto).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Arrive minimal: you simply deliver and receive a warehouse receipt. STEP 1 — repeated dealings unlock the BROKER relationship: you may sell forward / borrow against stored rice (rice-coupon mechanics), smoothing cash-flow seasonality. STEP 2 — the quarter's geography opens: rival warehouses, the public weighing-station with its standard masu, the porter gangs (origin-faction porter allies plug in here). STEP 3 (optional, ties to officialdom) — discrepancies at the weighing-station are where the rigged-masu evidence is physically found; the racket-evidence track routes through here. STEP 4 — preferential storage/shipping rates as a recognised supplier, feeding the Osaka push. The quarter teaches itself one warehouse, one scale, one gang at a time.

key_npcs.name: Kuramoto Zenya
key_npcs.role: Master of the warehouse the Kurosawa use; a gruff, scrupulous logistics man who hates short-weighting on principle (it dishonours his scales) — a useful, honest ally who can corroborate masu fraud.

key_npcs.name: Yard-foreman Tōkichi
key_npcs.role: Runs the porter gangs at the weighing-yard; the hook for seconding origin-town porter allies (Kanta, guild mates) into the castle-town logistics layer.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Warehouse storage + RICE-COUPON / forward-sale mechanics (cash-flow smoothing, borrow-against-stored-rice); standardised weighing (and the fraud detectable against it); preferential supplier rates; the physical logistics chain from estate granary → castle-town warehouse → Osaka. A cash-flow and throughput layer rather than a standing layer.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Indirect but essential: it is the conduit that lets meibutsu volume actually REACH the inter-han/Osaka market (an Influence lever) and where shipped surplus gets formally measured for recording. It is also the physical evidence-site that supports the officialdom racket sub-thread.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: MOSTLY OPTIONAL infrastructure that ACCELERATES the spine (better cash-flow, cheaper shipping) without gating it — you can ship through default channels more slowly. The evidence sub-use is purely optional side content.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Kuramoto (warehouse masters) and fudasashi/札差 (brokers advancing cash against rice) are precisely attested; rice-coupon (米切手) settlement was real and a forerunner of paper finance. The rigged kyō-masu is the locked grounded cause for an old 'tanuki/theft' belief — folklore stays a faint echo, fully debunked, never live.

factions_zones.name: The Wholesale Row — silk & sake tonya (問屋) routing Asagiri's meibutsu
factions_zones.theme: Turning the valley's by-employment crafts (sericulture/silk, sake, textiles organised back in T2) into a renowned DOMAIN PRODUCT — a meibutsu — by getting it graded, branded, and into the wholesale pipes that reach Osaka and beyond.
factions_zones.first_encounter: One contact: a wholesaler's buyer comes to the townhouse to taste/inspect a sample of the estate-organised village product. One verb — submit a sample for grading.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Arrive minimal: a single buyer, a single product line graded low. STEP 1 — improving quality (via the artisan-quarter craft tier and village producers) raises the grade and unlocks a standing CONTRACT with one wholesaler. STEP 2 — a second product line and a second wholesaler open (silk vs sake vs textile each their own buyer), turning it into a small portfolio. STEP 3 — a wholesaler agrees to MARKET it under a recognised name (proto-branding), the precursor to formal meibutsu status (which the guild-charter node completes). STEP 4 — wholesale demand feeds the Osaka-facing market node. Cluster reveals one buyer, one product, one contract at a time.

key_npcs.name: Silk-tonya Iseya Han'emon
key_npcs.role: Silk/textile wholesaler; meticulous grader whose standing contract is the gateway for Asagiri sericulture to leave the domain. Cautious, brand-protective, rewards consistent quality.

key_npcs.name: Sake-tonya Kōnoike-no-Tatsugorō
key_npcs.role: Sake/brewing wholesaler, expansive and social (does much of his business in the entertainment district); a bridge between the wholesale row and the social-district introductions.

key_npcs.name: Onatsu (visiting)
key_npcs.role: The T2 village weaver, surfaced here as the production-side counterpart — her cloth is what's being graded; ties the T2 village faction's output directly into the T4 wholesale pipeline.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Product GRADING (quality from craft-tier inputs → price); standing wholesale CONTRACTS (steady demand sinks for village/estate output, with the market-saturation crash softened by relationships, echoing the village mechanic); a multi-line product PORTFOLIO; and proto-branding toward meibutsu status. This is the demand engine that makes the whole cash-crop economy worth scaling.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Core to the '(2) cash crops & proto-industry → famous domain product' and '(7) trade ties' Influence levers. Getting a product graded high and contracted is recognised standing; a named, sought-after product IS visible house renown — and the seed of the T5 'meibutsu celebrated in Edo' endgame.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE-SUPPORTING: building a meibutsu is a stated T4 headline beat and feeds the T5 climax, but WHICH lines you pursue and how far is open-ended player choice; you can advance the tier on service/finance alone, just less richly. No single contract is a hard gate.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Tonya/問屋 wholesalers, domain meibutsu cultivation, and sericulture/sake/textile by-employments organised by local elites are all historically attested upward-mobility paths for rural houses. Entirely mundane commerce.

factions_zones.name: The Artisan Quarter & the Chartered Guilds (座 / 株仲間)
factions_zones.theme: A master-tier craft world above the village's honest-but-rough artisans — where quality is gatekept by guilds that hold monopolies and charters, and where the estate must earn a SEAT to put its product on a respected footing.
factions_zones.first_encounter: One node: a single master's workshop in the craft quarter, entered with a referral from a village artisan (Smith Gonta / Carpenter Risuke vouch upward). One verb — commission or apprentice a single master piece.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Arrive minimal: you may commission ONE master craftsman (better tools/components than the village can make). STEP 1 — completing commissions and demonstrating reliable supply earns recognition from a guild (the 'guild regard' meter). STEP 2 — a craft tier above the village unlocks (master tools, higher-tier components, master-craft commissions that feed combat-gear and proto-industry). STEP 3 — the GUILD CHARTER lever opens: petition for the Kurosawa meibutsu to be recognised under a kabu-nakama charter (quality assurance + protected branding + the right to sell into chartered channels). STEP 4 (deep) — a guild SEAT/share (kabu) confers ongoing standing and a say in the trade. You meet guild masters one at a time as your business with each craft becomes real; never a roster dump.

key_npcs.name: Guild-elder Kanshirō
key_npcs.role: Head of the relevant kabu-nakama (e.g. the textile or smithing guild); proud, protectionist gatekeeper of quality and charters — won over by demonstrated craft standards, not coin, an effort-over-status mirror in the merchant-artisan world.

key_npcs.name: Master-smith Tessai
key_npcs.role: A castle-town master smith above village Gonta's tier; gates master tools and (carefully, grounded) better spear/staff fittings for the conditioning-gated combat — never a power shortcut, just better-crafted gear earned by reputation.

key_npcs.name: Master-dyer Okō
key_npcs.role: A master dyer/finisher whose work raises Asagiri silk/textile to graded, brandable quality — the craft-side counterpart to the wholesale grading and meibutsu push.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A higher CRAFT TIER (master tools, higher components, master-craft commissions); a 'guild regard' standing meter per guild; the GUILD CHARTER for meibutsu (quality + branding + access to chartered sales); an optional guild SEAT/kabu (ongoing standing + trade voice). Crafting recipes/quality from here feed both the proto-industry economy and the (grounded) combat-gear track.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Feeds the meibutsu lever (a charter is formal quality recognition that raises the product's worth and renown) and the proto-industry lever; a guild seat is itself standing that the samurai-society and officialdom tracks register. Quality from here multiplies the value of everything the wholesale row ships.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: OPTIONAL-RICH: a powerful accelerator and the cleanest path to high-grade meibutsu, but never a hard gate on the tier — a spine-focused player can reach Osaka with lower-grade product, just for less. Confirmed: it never blocks the spine.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Za and kabu-nakama (chartered/monopoly guilds authorised by the shogunate/domain, holding kabu 'shares') are precisely attested Edo institutions. All craft, no magic; the only folklore is a faint workshop superstition kept as light colour, never mechanical.

factions_zones.name: The Kurosawa Castle-Town Townhouse (江戸/城下の屋敷) — the estate's beachhead
factions_zones.theme: A foothold in the seat of power: the literal home-base that makes everything else in T4 reachable. The house planting its mon in town — the physical proof, and instrument, of the rise.
factions_zones.first_encounter: One verb: secure a modest rented townhouse (the T4 entry reward). It starts as a single bare room with a gate — exactly mirroring the T1 kura cold-open, restating the fractal-incremental motion at the new frontier.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Arrive minimal: one bare room, no staff, you can do little but sleep and receive a caller. STEP 1 — a caretaker/houseman is installed, opening the 'receive introductions' verb (the social hub for all other T4 factions routes through here). STEP 2 — upgrade rooms: a receiving room (host officials/merchants → entertaining systems), a strongroom (hold coin/credit for finance dealings), staff quarters (house seconded allies in town). STEP 3 — the townhouse becomes a small operations base: a town agent who can transact while you're at the estate (idle/auto T4 dealings), a stable for travel between estate and town. STEP 4 (late) — own (not rent) the townhouse and upgrade it to befit the house's new standing — a visible Influence trophy. Each upgrade is a small reveal, never a menu of all rooms at once.

key_npcs.name: Town-agent Heikurō
key_npcs.role: The townhouse caretaker/agent the house installs; runs introductions, holds the social calendar, transacts on the house's behalf when the protagonist is away (the idle/automation hook for the tier). A loyal, dryly competent fixer.

key_npcs.name: Tokujirō (seconded)
key_npcs.role: The green recruit from the estate roster, sent to staff the townhouse — re-homing the 'you now teach others from zero' layer at T4: the protagonist who climbed now mentoring a junior into castle-town duties.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A HOME-BASE / hub node that gates access to the rest of T4 (introductions are received here); upgradeable rooms each unlocking a system (entertaining, finance-holding, ally-housing); a town agent enabling idle/automated T4 transactions; and a visible standing trophy (rent→own→befit-standing) that tracks the rise. The connective infrastructure of the whole tier.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: The townhouse's grade IS a legible Influence display, and several Influence-minting acts (entertaining officials, hosting alliance negotiations, formal contract signings) physically happen here. Upgrades are paid in coin/koku but their RECOGNITION (a befitting town residence for a house of standing) reads as Influence.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE-CRITICAL as infrastructure (it is the T4 hub — most T4 content is reached through it), but its UPGRADE depth is paced reward, not a wall; the minimal townhouse already unlocks the tier, and luxe upgrades are optional standing-flex.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Retainers and rising houses maintaining a castle-town residence/townhouse is historically standard. Wholly mundane property and staffing; no folklore — though a one-line wink that 'even the castle-town has its rumours' preserves the motif without importing yokai.

factions_zones.name: Domain Samurai Society & the Domain Academy (藩校 / 家中)
factions_zones.theme: Acceptance by one's peers — the hardest currency of all for a taken-in stray turned right-hand. Etiquette, scholarship, and alliance as the gates of true samurai standing; where birth still whispers even as merit shouts. The home of the inter-house rivalry and the marriage/adoption endgame lever.
factions_zones.first_encounter: One invitation: the protagonist, as the Kurosawa's representative, is grudgingly admitted to a single domain function (a poetry/tea gathering or a lecture at the academy). One node, one verb — present yourself correctly and not embarrass the house.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Arrive minimal: tolerated at one gathering, snubbed by most, with a single cordial contact. STEP 1 — correct conduct + the house's rising fortunes open the DOMAIN ACADEMY (hankō) as a node: study etiquette/letters/military arts to raise a 'samurai-society standing' meter (and unlock status-appropriate dialogue that smooths every other T4 track). STEP 2 — individual retainers warm one at a time (each a small relationship, web-shaped like the village, not a ladder); a few become allies, one or two stay hostile. STEP 3 — the RIVAL HOUSE TOMITA thread sharpens here (status competition: contested introductions, undercut contracts, social slights to out-maneuver). STEP 4 (deep, late, optional) — the MARRIAGE/ADOPTION ALLIANCE lever matures: a sanctioned alliance (or symbolic adoption into a better-placed line) is the most authentic top-rung standing lever, negotiated through the cross-tier brokerage. You learn the cast of retainers gradually, by who deigns to speak to you next.

key_npcs.name: Academy-head Rai Genpachi
key_npcs.role: The hankō's principal/instructor — a stern Confucian scholar who, pointedly, grades effort and character over birth; the domain-scale restatement of Jūbei's creed ('talent is a story the lucky tell'), and the gatekeeper of samurai-society respectability.

key_npcs.name: Retainer Tadahiro
key_npcs.role: A mid-ranking, fair-minded domain retainer who becomes the protagonist's first genuine peer-ally in samurai society; a sponsor who vouches him upward (patronage Influence lever).

key_npcs.name: Tomita Kageyuki
key_npcs.role: Head/heir of rival House Tomita — the prospered-as-Kurosawa-fell competitor; talent + luck + ruthlessness, never innate gift. The inter-house antagonist to OUT-MANEUVER (contracts, alliances, reputation), not kill; his pressure is social and economic, raising the stakes of every T4 introduction.

key_npcs.name: Lord Kurosawa Munenori (in town)
key_npcs.role: The head of the house, surfaced at the castle-town: the protagonist's labour now materially lifts HIS standing among peers and officials — the mechanism by which the house (not the protagonist personally) gains face, honouring the mediated-recognition ceiling.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A 'samurai-society standing' meter (raised at the academy + by correct conduct) gating status-appropriate access and smoothing all T4 dealings; the academy as a study sub-loop (etiquette/letters/military arts — grounded, no magic, conditioning-respecting); a peer-relationship WEB; the inter-house RIVALRY (Tomita) as contested standing; and the PATRONAGE and MARRIAGE/ADOPTION ALLIANCE levers (sponsors who vouch upward; a sanctioned alliance for a standing leap).
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Hosts two named Influence levers — patronage/alliance and (via the cross-tier brokerage) marriage/adoption — and is where the protagonist's service is converted into the LORD'S lifted standing, the load-bearing mechanism of the T5 mediated-recognition endgame. Out-maneuvering Tomita is direct contested Influence.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: MIXED: a baseline of samurai-society standing is SPINE-relevant (status-gating smooths required officialdom/finance dealings and is the path to lifting the lord's face for T5), but the DEPTH — full academy mastery, the Tomita rivalry's optional escalations, and the marriage/adoption alliance — is largely optional/player-chosen and never a hard wall; the Tomita thread in particular is rivalry-to-outpace, not a gate.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Hankō (domain academies), kachū (the domain's retainer corps), and merit/adoption/marriage as real (if rare) mobility levers are all attested. Strictly social and scholarly — no folklore, no magic; Tomita's edge is explicitly made (trained/lucky/ruthless), per the locked talent-foil rule.

factions_zones.name: The Entertainment & Social District (盛り場 / 茶屋・料亭町)
factions_zones.theme: The grease of the castle-town machine — the teahouse-and-restaurant quarter where deals no office will host are actually struck, where introductions are bought in courtesy and conversation, and where standing is performed. The connective tissue of the entire tier.
factions_zones.first_encounter: One node, one verb: a single reputable teahouse ( chaya) the town-agent points you to, where you may host ONE modest gathering or be brought as a guest. You meet the proprietress.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Arrive minimal: one teahouse, one introduction at a time, modest means. STEP 1 — building rapport with the proprietress (the district's true broker) unlocks the INTRODUCTIONS system: spend coin + standing to be presented to a specific official, merchant, or retainer you couldn't otherwise reach (gating who across ALL other T4 factions you can even meet). STEP 2 — a ryōtei (fine restaurant) and additional venues open, raising the tier of guest you can host (entertaining officials = Influence). STEP 3 — the district becomes an information broker (grounded rumour: who is in debt, who is feuding, who can be allied — fueling the Tomita rivalry and the racket-evidence track). STEP 4 (deep, optional) — a patron-of-the-arts angle (sponsor a respected performer/scholar) as a soft-power standing flex. Reveal is slow: one venue, one broker, one contact at a time.

key_npcs.name: Proprietress Okatsu (女将)
key_npcs.role: Mistress of the leading teahouse; the district's discreet super-broker who knows everyone's business and trades in introductions and confidences. Warm, shrewd, neutral-for-hire — the single most useful social contact in town; rapport with her opens doors to every other faction.

key_npcs.name: Geisha/entertainer Koyuki
key_npcs.role: A respected accomplished entertainer (strictly the period-accurate ARTS sense — music, dance, conversation, NOT prostitution) whose favour signals taste and standing; a soft-power patronage thread and a quiet information conduit.

key_npcs.name: Sake-tonya Tatsugorō (recurring)
key_npcs.role: The wholesale-row sake merchant who does his real business here — embodying how the district stitches the merchant, official, and samurai worlds together over the same table.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: The INTRODUCTIONS system (the master gate for WHO you can meet across officialdom/merchants/samurai-society — spend coin + standing to unlock a contact); ENTERTAINING (host gatherings to raise standing / mint Influence with officials and allies); a grounded INFORMATION-BROKER feed (rumours that drive the rivalry and evidence threads); and a soft-power patronage angle. It is the hub that makes the other T4 factions REACHABLE rather than a content silo of its own.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Entertaining officials/peers is a direct Influence-minting act (recognition performed socially), and introductions are the enabling layer for nearly every other Influence lever (you must be presented before you can render service, restructure debt at the top, or negotiate an alliance). It quietly multiplies the whole tier's throughput.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE-ENABLING but flexible: SOME social-district use is effectively required (introductions gate access), yet it is generous and player-paced — there is always at least one affordable introduction route, so it never hard-blocks; the deeper entertaining/patronage/information layers are optional richness.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Castle-town pleasure/teahouse quarters as the real venue for political and commercial dealmaking are well attested. Handled with restraint and period accuracy (geisha as performing artists; no explicit content). The ONE permitted folklore wink lives here — a single light 'castle-town ghost story' rumour as a nod to the inn's rumours-board motif, fully optional, instantly mundane, never mechanical.

factions_zones.name: The Osaka-Facing / Inter-Han Market & the Domain Warehouse-Residence (大坂蔵屋敷 link)
factions_zones.theme: The horizon beyond the domain — where a renowned product stops being local and becomes part of the realm's great rice-and-goods market, the bridge that turns T4 meibutsu into T5 Edo recognition.
factions_zones.first_encounter: One verb, remote: consign a first shipment toward the domain's Osaka warehouse-residence (kuramoyashiki) via the rice quarter and Marutaya's credit; you never go to Osaka yourself — you deal with its correspondent. One contact: the domain's Osaka agent (kakeya/kuramoto link).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Arrive minimal: a single small consignment, settled at a poor price. STEP 1 — reliable supply + graded quality earns a standing place in the domain's Osaka shipments (the meibutsu enters the great market). STEP 2 — price/volume scale as renown grows; inter-han demand opens (other domains' buyers want the product). STEP 3 — the product becomes NAMED in the wider market (the bridge beat: 'they ask for Asagiri silk in Osaka now'). STEP 4 (T5 seam) — the Osaka renown becomes the supply chain that, via sankin-kōtai, carries the product to EDO — the explicit handoff into the T5 endgame. Deliberately abstract/remote (a holdings/throughput board, not a full sub-map), keeping focus on the home castle-town while the world widens.

key_npcs.name: Osaka agent Yodoya-no-Kuroemon
key_npcs.role: The domain's Osaka warehouse-residence correspondent (kuramoto/kakeya for the han); a far-off, powerful counterparty dealt with by letter and credit — the conduit that turns shipped meibutsu into realm-wide renown. Never met in person early; embodies the world beyond the domain.

key_npcs.name: Okimi (origin trade-tie)
key_npcs.role: The protagonist's sister, married into a trading family (from the origin faction), whose connections plug the origin-town routes INTO this Osaka-facing pipeline — the concrete cross-faction synergy that makes shipping cheaper/faster.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A remote SHIPPING / throughput layer (consign → grade → price → renown), abstracted as a holdings/market board; inter-han DEMAND scaling; product RENOWN as a tracked value that becomes the meibutsu's fame; and the credit-settlement link to Maruishi/Maruishi finance and the warehouse quarter. The economic bridge from domain to realm.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Embodies the '(7) trade ties / inter-han / Osaka market' Influence lever and converts the meibutsu into RENOWN — the direct precursor of the T5 'product celebrated in Edo' beat. Origin trade-ties (Okimi) act as the §1.5.4 multiplier that makes this faster/cheaper, exactly as the spine intends.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: SPINE-SUPPORTING and forward-looking: it is the designed BRIDGE from T4 to T5, so engaging it is how the spine advances toward the endgame — but its scale is player-paced and the origin-multiplier path is optional acceleration, not a wall.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Osaka as 'the nation's kitchen,' domain kuramoyashiki (warehouse-residences) staffed by kuramoto/kakeya, and the Dōjima rice market are precisely attested; meibutsu reaching Osaka then Edo via sankin-kōtai supply chains is historically how rural products gained fame. Pure commerce; no folklore.

factions_zones.name: The Marriage & Adoption Alliance Brokerage (縁組・養子 networks)
factions_zones.theme: Family as strategy — the most authentic (and slowest) lever a small house has to leap in standing: a sanctioned marriage or symbolic adoption that ties the Kurosawa to a better-placed line. Belonging engineered through kinship.
factions_zones.first_encounter: One quiet contact: a go-between (nakōdo) sounds out the house, brokered through the social district once samurai-society standing is non-trivial. One verb — entertain (or decline) a first proposal.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Arrive minimal: a single tentative proposal, modest in ambition. STEP 1 — the go-between network opens as relationships build (the social district + samurai society + merchant houses each surface candidate ties). STEP 2 — concrete alliance OPTIONS appear, each a multi-beat negotiation rather than a checklist (e.g. a domain-retainer line, the merchant Maruishi via Onui, a rival-neutralising tie with a Tomita-adjacent family). STEP 3 — a chosen alliance is negotiated over time (standing checks, gifts, goyōkin, patron endorsements). STEP 4 (capstone, optional) — a symbolic adoption into a better-placed house name (the aspirational top-rung flavour from §1.5.1) as a late standing leap. Threads surface slowly and are always declinable — the house can rise without ever marrying up.

key_npcs.name: Go-between Omiya-no-Sahei
key_npcs.role: A professional marriage/adoption broker (nakōdo) plugged into every faction; the discreet engine that surfaces alliance options and runs the negotiations. Trades in standing and discretion, not romance.

key_npcs.name: Lady Kurosawa Chiyo (escalated)
key_npcs.role: The lord's wife, whose domestic-economy and hospitality mastery (from the estate roster) makes her the house's actual alliance-strategist at T4 — she vets candidates and hosts the negotiations, extending her T1 role outward.

key_npcs.name: Onui (Maruishi heir)
key_npcs.role: Recurs as a concrete commoner-side alliance candidate (merchant-samurai tie via goyōkin/adoption), a historically real mobility path; an alliance grounded in mutual competence, not melodrama.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: An ALLIANCE-NEGOTIATION sub-loop (surface candidates → standing/gift/goyōkin checks → multi-beat negotiation → sanctioned tie); each alliance grants a discrete standing leap and a faction synergy (a retainer tie smooths samurai society; a merchant tie deepens finance; a strategic tie neutralises Tomita). Distinct, chunky milestone steps — closer to the origin track's shape than a meter.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Directly serves the '(6) patronage & alliance — strategic marriage and merchant/farmer buy-in via goyōkin and adoption' Influence lever, the single most authentic top-rung mobility tool for a goshi house; a sanctioned alliance is among the largest discrete Influence steps available at T4/into T5.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: FULLY OPTIONAL (but powerful): the most efficient standing-leap lever, yet every proposal is declinable and the house is completable without any alliance — confirmed it never blocks the spine; it is opt-in richness for players who want the fastest, most 'authentic Edo' rise.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Strategic marriage and especially merchant-to-samurai ADOPTION (buying into samurai rank via goyōkin / yōshi adoption) are well-attested Edo mobility mechanisms for the ambitious. Wholly mundane social engineering; no romance-fantasy, no magic, handled with period restraint.

risks: Scope/UI blowup: T4 introduces ~10 new faction/zone nodes at once; if their reveals aren't strictly paced behind the fractal-incremental gates (one contact/door at a time, each its own story beat), the castle-town will overload the player and violate the locked fractal-incrementality pillar. Needs a hard reveal-budget per in-game span, like every other tier.

City-builder/4X drift: finance + warehouses + shipping + guilds + alliances risk turning T4 into management tedium and away from the cozy daily-labour + grounded-character core (an explicitly flagged §1.13 risk). Must keep each sub-loop small and story-framed, and preserve the daily-labour texture even at the castle-town.

Spine vs. side ambiguity is denser here than in T1-T3: several T4 nodes are 'spine-enabling infrastructure' (townhouse, introductions, officialdom recording) rather than cleanly optional, blurring the locked 'side never blocks spine' line. Each gate must be audited so the minimal version always unblocks the spine and only DEPTH is optional.

Partial-justice / ceiling integrity: making Kuroiwa's racket prosecutable at the domain level risks tipping into a clean heroic takedown that violates the locked 'untouchable power largely escapes' thesis — the magistrate-above-Kuroiwa shield and the deadly osso risk must stay load-bearing.

Number-range balance across the T4 economic levers (debt restructuring, goyōkin, meibutsu pricing, alliance leaps) could destabilise the Influence curve between tiers; the relative Influence weights are an open §4 balance question and may need big-number formatting.

Authenticity-vs-legibility load: T4 is the most institution-dense tier (daikan/tedai/kanjō-kata/machi-bugyō/kuramoto/fudasashi/kakeya/za/kabu-nakama/hankō/kuramoyashiki). Risk of jargon fatigue; needs diegetic plain-language framing in the event log so terms read as story, not a history lecture.

Folklore near-absence at T4 is a deliberate design choice (rational castle-town vs. superstitious valley) but risks feeling tonally disconnected from the rumours-board motif; mitigated to ONE light wink, but the human may want the contrast confirmed or a little more T4 atmosphere.

Geisha / entertainment-district and marriage/adoption content carry cultural-sensitivity risk if handled carelessly; spec keeps them to attested arts/social-engineering senses with restraint, but they should get an explicit authenticity/sensitivity pass in §5 like the osso and disaster-survivor material did.
open_questions: Tier granularity: §1.14 already asks whether 'region' and 'domain' should merge or a 'province' tier be added — this T4 design assumes they stay distinct (T3 region = trade/holdings/Kuzuhara; T4 = the castle-town social/financial seat). Confirm that split before these factions are locked.

How spatial is T4? §1.14 asks whether macro tiers get full sub-area maps or abstract holdings boards. This design assumes a SEMI-spatial castle-town (a real walkable set of districts: officialdom compound, merchant quarter, craft quarter, townhouse, social district) with the Osaka link kept abstract/remote. Confirm.

Is marriage/adoption a real late-game MECHANIC or pure flavour? (§1.14 / open-questions flag.) This design specs it as a real but fully-optional cross-tier lever; the human must decide if relationship/family-line systems are in v1+ scope or narrative-only.

Top-title authenticity for a goshi house at the domain level — does the castle-town arc let the protagonist meaningfully enter samurai society as the house's agent, and how is his (commoner-origin) presence at samurai gatherings justified? Needs the §4/§5 research-harden flagged in §1.13.

How load-bearing is the Kuroiwa racket prosecution at T4 vs. carried into T5? §1.14 asks whether side-mysteries climax WITH the tier climb at T5 or resolve around T3-T4. This affects whether the officialdom evidence-track resolves here or is deliberately left dangling for the capital.

Whose neck is on the line in the osso-style petition (§1.14)? If the petition apparatus lives in the T4 machi-bugyō hall, the personal-risk question (protagonist vs. ally vs. gimin-martyr NPC) must be answered to design the case-hall content.

Which side-faction multipliers attach where at T4: do origin trade-ties (Okimi/Osaka link) and village allies (seconded to the townhouse/warehouse) keep the ~halve-time-to-next-tier tuning, and do seconded allies cost village/origin production (the §1.14 'seconded allies cost' question)?

Canonical naming: are the new T4 NPC/house names (Maruishi/Maruishi vs a single spelling, Daikan Iemasa, Tedai Kuroiwa already canon, etc.) acceptable, and which macron/romanization convention applies (the §1.13 policy is still open)? Several names here are placeholders pending the project-wide convention.

v1 scope: §1.14 proposes Tiers 1-2 complete, T3 stubbed, T4-5 as roadmap. Confirm this entire T4 design is roadmap-tier content (not v1), so its depth can be specced freely without bloating the first playable slice.

facets.scope: Tier 5 — Edo (the capital), reached INDIRECTLY and MEDIATED. New factions / zones / NPC-clusters of the capital that the Kurosawa house touches only at arm's length: the domain's Edo yashiki & rusui staff, the Osaka→Edo rice-finance chain (kura-yashiki / kakeya / fudasashi), the Nihonbashi merchant houses & tonya markets that celebrate the domain meibutsu, the banzuke/literati cultural milieu where a product's fame is made, bakufu-adjacent officialdom reachable only through the rusui, and a touring junkenshi/metsuke inspector before whom standing is contested. Honours the locked ceiling (no personal hatamoto status, no audience with the shogun); each node itself fractally incremental; all tie to House Influence; designed as a capstone, not a power fantasy.
facets.summary: T5 is the capstone canvas where the Kurosawa name is finally spoken in the capital — but ALWAYS through a proxy. The protagonist never sets foot in Edo as an equal; he reaches it through three arm's-length conduits, each a fractal-incremental sub-world: (1) the LORD's body, via sankin-kotai attendance — the lord goes to Edo, and the protagonist's work back home (and dispatched manifests/letters) materially lifts the lord's face there; (2) the domain's EDO YASHIKI, run by the rusui-yaku liaison, who becomes the protagonist's pen-pal proxy in the capital; (3) the domain MEIBUTSU (the famous product built from the T1-T4 shinden/cash-crop/sericulture economy), which travels to Edo's markets in crates while the man who made it stays in the valley. From these three conduits hang the factions below: the Edo yashiki & its staff; the Osaka kura-yashiki → Edo fudasashi rice-finance chain that converts the house's surplus into capital-side creditworthiness; the Nihonbashi tonya/great-merchant houses (Echigoya-scale) who decide whether the meibutsu gets shelf-space; the banzuke/literati milieu (mitate-banzuke ranking charts, gazetteers, salons) where a product's REPUTATION is manufactured; bakufu-adjacent officialdom (kanjo-bugyo / machi-bugyo offices) reachable only THROUGH the rusui, never directly; and the touring junkenshi/metsuke INSPECTOR whose periodic survey is the single contested set-piece where the house's standing is judged. The capstone payoff is the meibutsu climbing a real Edo ranking chart and a documented merit commendation flowing DOWN to the house through the lord — recognition that is heard, recorded, and celebrated, never personally conferred. Every node opens minimal (one letter, one crate, one broker, one clerk), reveals its people/systems in story beats, is optional-where-marked (never blocks the spine), and feeds House Influence through diegetic recognition events. The whole tier is built so its emotional weight comes from the GAP — the house is famous in a city the protagonist will never be received in — which is the game's effort-meets-ceiling thesis made structural.

factions_zones.name: The Domain's Edo Yashiki (Edo residence compound) & its standing staff
factions_zones.theme: The arm's-length embassy: the house's only fixed footprint in the capital is a corner of someone else's compound, run by people the protagonist will only ever know through letters.
factions_zones.first_encounter: A single sealed letter arrives at the estate from the domain's Edo residence — the rusui-yaku acknowledging receipt of the first crate of meibutsu the lord carried up on sankin-kotai. One contact, one inbound verb (read the dispatch).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: 1) Read the first dispatch (the yashiki exists, has a name, has one official: the rusui Konoe). 2) Earn a reply channel — you may now SEND a manifest/letter (the 'correspondence' panel, a slow async loop measured in road-days). 3) Konoe names the kami-yashiki (upper residence, the lord's seat) vs naka/shimo-yashiki (middle/lower, storehouses & retainers) — the compound becomes a small map of rooms you petition through. 4) Meet the yashiki's working staff as they become relevant: the kura-bugyo (warehouse officer) who logs incoming domain goods, the kanjo-kata clerk who tracks the residence's ruinous upkeep budget, the young dispatch-runner who carries letters down the kaido. 5) Late: the yashiki becomes a project of its own — the protagonist's surplus quietly underwrites its repair, lifting the lord's prestige among neighbouring domains' residences.

key_npcs.name: Rusui-yaku Konoe Settsu-no-suke
key_npcs.role: The domain's resident Edo liaison/留守居役 — runs the residence in the lord's absence, the protagonist's sole pen-pal proxy in the capital; polite, overworked, quietly judging whether this provincial steward is worth his ink.

key_npcs.name: Kura-bugyo Masaki
key_npcs.role: Warehouse officer of the Edo residence; logs each incoming crate of meibutsu and reports its reception — the diegetic source of 'your goods arrived and were noted' Influence events.

key_npcs.name: Clerk Hayami
key_npcs.role: Residence accounts clerk drowning in the yashiki's upkeep costs; the thread by which the protagonist learns the Edo footprint is a money-pit and an opportunity.

key_npcs.name: Dispatch-runner Tsunazo
key_npcs.role: Young kaido courier between domain and residence; humanises the slow async loop and carries the rare hand-written line of warmth from the capital.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds the async CORRESPONDENCE loop (send manifests/petitions/goods up, get acknowledgements/instructions/Influence back on a road-day delay) and a residence-upkeep sink the house can elect to underwrite. Gives the protagonist a remote 'desk' in Edo without ever being there. The rusui is the gateway NPC through whom ALL bakufu-adjacent contact must be routed — structurally enforcing the ceiling.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: The yashiki is the ledger where capital-side recognition is RECORDED: each acknowledged crate, each repair underwritten, each successful petition routed through Konoe posts an Influence event ('the residence books now show the Kurosawa name'). It is the conversion point from provincial achievement into capital standing.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: Spine — this is the primary T5 conduit; it is how the tier is reached at all. (Its sub-projects like funding the repair are optional accelerants.)
factions_zones.grounded_note: Entirely institutional and documentary; the protagonist's relationship to the capital is paperwork and crates, never presence. No folklore. Honours the ceiling: he commands a corner of the residence's logistics, never crosses its threshold himself.

factions_zones.name: The Lord's Sankin-kotai Attendance (the procession & the lord-as-proxy)
factions_zones.theme: The protagonist's hands reach Edo only inside the LORD's body of work: he equips, provisions, and burnishes the lord's mandatory attendance, and the credit accrues to the lord — and thence to the house.
factions_zones.first_encounter: Late T4, Chief Steward Genemon hands the protagonist a share of preparing the lord's biennial journey up the kaido — one verb: outfit one leg of the procession (provision a stage).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: 1) Provision a single stage of the journey (you touch the procession). 2) Own the whole outfitting (retinue size scales with kokudaka — a legible status display you fund and dress). 3) Send the meibutsu UP with the lord as gifts/tribute (the product rides the procession into Edo society). 4) Receive 'how it landed' reports from Konoe — what the lord's peers said, which gifts opened which doors. 5) Capstone: the lord returns from an attendance year visibly higher in standing because of what the house equipped him with, and says so — the single warmest mediated-recognition beat.

key_npcs.name: Lord Kurosawa Munenori
key_npcs.role: The proxy himself — carries the house's face to Edo; his lifted standing IS the protagonist's Edo achievement. Returns with weary, genuine acknowledgement that is never personal elevation.

key_npcs.name: Procession-master Saburozaemon
key_npcs.role: (existing senior retainer, promoted into this) Marshals the retinue, teaches the protagonist the brutal economics of a status procession; gatekeeps the outfitting quality tiers.

key_npcs.name: Heir Naoyuki
key_npcs.role: (existing) Travels in the retinue as the house's future face; the rivalry-turned-brotherhood pays off as he reports back, from Edo, what the protagonist's work bought them.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds a periodic, expensive ATTENDANCE-OUTFITTING sink (procession scale, gifts, road logistics) tied to the existing porter/caravan supply chains from the origin faction — making the T3 origin trade-ties directly useful at T5. A biennial cadence gives the upper tier a heartbeat. Routes the meibutsu into Edo as tribute.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: The cleanest expression of mediated Influence: the protagonist spends koku/coin/goods on the lord's attendance; recognition lands on the LORD; the house's Influence rises by reflection. Explicitly NOT the protagonist's personal standing.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: Spine — outfitting the attendance is a core T5 driver. (Lavishness tier is an optional accelerant.)
factions_zones.grounded_note: Sankin-kotai is the historically exact mechanism by which a rural house touched the capital. The protagonist never joins the audience; he provisions the man who does. Folklore-free; pure institutional logistics.

factions_zones.name: The Osaka Kura-yashiki & the Rice-Finance Chain (kakeya / kuramoto → Edo fudasashi)
factions_zones.theme: Turning a mountain of provincial rice into capital-side creditworthiness through the merchant financiers who actually banked the daimyo — the house graduates from begging usurers to being banked by Osaka and Edo houses.
factions_zones.first_encounter: A single rice-bill (kome-kitte) returns from the domain's Osaka warehouse against the house's shipped surplus — one document proving the house's grain became negotiable capital far away.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: 1) Ship surplus to the domain's Osaka kura-yashiki; get one rice-bill back (abstract, one node). 2) Meet the kakeya (the domain's Osaka financial agent) who auctions the rice and advances against it. 3) Restructure the strangling ancestral debt THROUGH this proper channel — the T4 debt-restructuring lever completes here, converting usury into merchant finance. 4) Reach the Edo fudasashi of Kuramae (Asakusa) who handle capital-side rice-bill discounting and short-term credit — the house can now finance its Edo footprint (the yashiki repair, the attendance) on its own paper, not loans. 5) Late: a 'house credit' standing emerges — the financiers extend terms unprompted because the Kurosawa name now carries weight.

key_npcs.name: Kakeya Yodoya-suji Kihei
key_npcs.role: The domain's Osaka financial agent (掛屋) — auctions the house's tax-rice, advances capital, issues rice-bills; the grounded face of 'your grain is now money in a city you've never seen.'

key_npcs.name: Kuramoto Suminokura-ya
key_npcs.role: Great Osaka warehouse-financier house (蔵元) that banks several domains; reaching its goodwill is a slow creditworthiness climb, not a quest.

key_npcs.name: Fudasashi Daikokuya Jube
key_npcs.role: Edo rice-broker of Kuramae (札差) who discounts the house's bills and bankrolls its capital footprint; sharp, transactional, the one who first treats the Kurosawa name as collateral worth having.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds the rice-bill (kome-kitte) instrument and a CREDITWORTHINESS meter: surplus → Osaka warehouse → auction → bill → discount/advance in Edo. Completes the debt-restructuring arc into a positive credit line that funds T5 projects. A finance sub-economy distinct from physical production — abstract paper standing, climbing.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Creditworthiness is recognition by the money-power, a distinct Influence feeder: when the great houses extend terms to the Kurosawa name, that acknowledgement posts Influence ('Osaka extends us credit on our name alone'). Funds, and so accelerates, the yashiki and attendance spine-projects.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: Mostly spine-adjacent (the debt-into-credit arc is a key Influence lever) but the deepest fudasashi creditworthiness grind is an OPTIONAL accelerant that never blocks the spine.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Historically exact: domains banked through Osaka kuramoto/kakeya and Edo fudasashi; rice-bills and the Dojima exchange were real instruments. No magic, no folklore — pure period finance. The protagonist negotiates by letter and agent; he does not personally walk Dojima.

factions_zones.name: The Nihonbashi Merchant Houses & Tonya Markets (the meibutsu's gatekeepers)
factions_zones.theme: The great wholesale houses who decide whether a provincial product gets shelf-space in the capital — the commercial gate the meibutsu must pass, courted entirely through agents and samples.
factions_zones.first_encounter: A specialist tonya (wholesaler in the house's product category) sends, via the rusui, a single trial order for a sample bale of the meibutsu — one buyer, one inbound order.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: 1) Fill one trial order through Konoe (one tonya, one transaction). 2) The tonya names its terms and rivals — you learn the category's wholesale web (a silk tonya, a sake tonya, a paper/lacquer tonya, depending on the chosen meibutsu). 3) Reach a great Nihonbashi house (Echigoya/Mitsui-scale) whose endorsement is a tier of legitimacy in itself; courted with consistent quality and volume, not a quest. 4) Secure a standing contract / the house's mon on the product's packaging — the meibutsu is now a branded Edo good. 5) Late: a bidding tension between rival tonya houses for exclusive supply — a soft optional mini-arc that raises the price the house commands.

key_npcs.name: Tonya Izumiya Mohei
key_npcs.role: Category wholesaler (問屋) who places the first trial order; the meibutsu's first capital-side buyer and the protagonist's read on what Edo palates/markets want.

key_npcs.name: Banto Kahei
key_npcs.role: Head clerk (番頭) of a great Nihonbashi dry-goods/specialty house — the real decision-maker behind the famous shopfront; courted patiently; warms to demonstrated reliability over flash.

key_npcs.name: Naka-gai Sokichi-no-Edo-en
key_npcs.role: An Edo middleman/broker who connects provincial suppliers to Nihonbashi houses (echo of village peddler Sokichi's role, scaled up) — the bridge NPC into the wholesale web.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds the Edo WHOLESALE market: trial orders → standing contracts → branding → exclusive-supply bidding. A demand-side counterpart to the production economy, with quality/volume/consistency as the levers (not haggling). Introduces capital-market price discovery for the meibutsu and a reputation-multiplier on every unit shipped.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: A great house carrying the meibutsu, and especially its mon on the packaging, is loud public recognition — a major Influence feeder ('Nihonbashi sells our name'). Branded shelf-space is the commercial half of the capstone; the cultural-fame half comes from the banzuke milieu below.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: Spine for the first contract (the meibutsu reaching Edo markets IS a tier-5 headline beat); the exclusive-supply bidding war and deepest house-goodwill are OPTIONAL accelerants that never block the spine.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Nihonbashi tonya and houses like Echigoya/Shirokiya are historically exact; provincial meibutsu reaching Edo via specialist wholesalers is real commerce. The protagonist deals through agents and the rusui; he never personally trades on Nihonbashi. No folklore.

factions_zones.name: The Edo Cultural Milieu & the Banzuke (where a product's fame is manufactured)
factions_zones.theme: Reputation as a made thing: salons, gazetteers, and the ranking-chart (mitate-banzuke) culture that turned products and places into celebrated names — the meibutsu must become not just sold but FAMOUS.
factions_zones.first_encounter: Konoe forwards a single woodblock broadsheet — a meibutsu gazetteer or mitate-banzuke — on which a rival domain's product appears and the Kurosawa's does not. One artifact, one stinging absence.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: 1) See the ranking chart that omits you (the fame-economy exists, and you are below the line). 2) Learn its makers — a hack writer/publisher, a tea-house host, a literatus tastemaker whose word moves the charts. 3) Court taste, not bribes: get the meibutsu into the right hands (gifted via the lord's attendance, sampled at a salon) so its quality earns mentions. 4) The product first APPEARS on a minor chart (a named, recorded fame beat). 5) Capstone: it CLIMBS the chart toward the top ranks, and a famous name publicly praises it — the cultural half of the capstone, recognition by acclaim.

key_npcs.name: Gesakusha Santo Bunzaemon
key_npcs.role: A popular Edo writer/publisher who compiles mitate-banzuke and meibutsu gazetteers; cynical, fashionable; getting his pen on your side is a slow courtship of quality and intrigue, never a payment.

key_npcs.name: Chajin Sorin
key_npcs.role: A tea-and-taste connoisseur whose salon endorsement legitimises a product among the discerning; the arbiter whose single nod can move a sake/tea/silk meibutsu up the ranks.

key_npcs.name: Geisha-no-Ogiya Koharu
key_npcs.role: A celebrated entertainer of the licensed quarter whose use of the meibutsu (a cloth, a scent, a sake) sets fashion; reached only indirectly, through the rusui's connections — fame by association, the protagonist never present.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds a FAME/RENOWN meter for the meibutsu, advanced by placement on charts and tastemaker endorsements rather than volume — a reputation layer ABOVE raw sales. Banzuke standing acts as a public progress bar for the capstone and as a price/demand multiplier feeding back into the Nihonbashi wholesale economy.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: The meibutsu's chart-rank is the most legible single Influence display of the whole game — a published, ranked, capital-wide acknowledgement of the house's product. Climbing it is the tier-5 spine's visible win condition, mirroring the house-rank ladder at grand scale.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: Spine-adjacent: the FIRST appearance on a chart is a headline T5 beat; reaching the very top ranks and the tastemaker endorsements are OPTIONAL depth that enrich but never block the capstone.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Mitate-banzuke ranking charts and meibutsu gazetteers were real Edo media; fame was genuinely manufactured by writers, salons, and tastemakers. No supernatural element. Honours the ceiling: the product is famous in rooms the protagonist will never enter; fame attaches to the goods and the house name, not the man.

factions_zones.name: Bakufu-Adjacent Officialdom (kanjo-bugyo / machi-bugyo offices, reachable only through the rusui)
factions_zones.theme: The shogunate's machinery glimpsed at maximum arm's length — the house can have a petition heard, a permit granted, a name noted, but only ever filtered through layers of intermediaries; the ceiling made bureaucratic.
factions_zones.first_encounter: Konoe reports that a routine matter (a transit permit for the meibutsu, a sumptuary question about its packaging) must go before a bakufu office — and that the protagonist will deal with NONE of it directly. One indirect node, one filtered request.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: 1) Learn the office exists and that the rusui is the only path to it (the wall is introduced as a wall). 2) Get one small filtered favour — a permit, a clarification — routed up and back. 3) Learn the officialdom's shape: the kanjo-bugyo's clerks (finance/permits), the machi-bugyo's reach (Edo commerce regulation), the gulf above them you cannot touch. 4) A documented petition (osso-adjacent, but lawful and routed) for formal acknowledgement of the house's service. 5) Capstone-feeding: a recorded merit notation enters an official register — the house's name in bakufu ink — delivered DOWN through the rusui and the lord, never handed to the protagonist.

key_npcs.name: Kanjo-kata clerk Oka Shinnojo
key_npcs.role: A mid-level finance-office clerk (勘定方) the rusui deals with; processes permits and notations; correct, slow, the human face of the machine — and the highest official the protagonist's influence will ever indirectly move.

key_npcs.name: Yoriki Mizuno
key_npcs.role: A machi-bugyo office enforcement officer (与力) who governs Edo commercial conduct; can help or hinder the meibutsu's market access; reached only via the rusui, never met.

key_npcs.name: Rusui Konoe (as broker)
key_npcs.role: (reprise) The indispensable filter — every official contact passes through him, structurally guaranteeing the protagonist's distance from power.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds a PERMITS & PETITIONS layer (transit licences, sumptuary compliance, formal acknowledgements) that gate-keeps the highest commercial reach — solved through correct routing and documented service, not force or wealth alone. A slow, high-friction bureaucratic loop that makes capital-level legitimacy feel hard-won and institutional.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Official notations are the heaviest, rarest Influence events — bakufu ink is the apex acknowledgement available, and it arrives mediated. Each permit/petition resolved widens what the meibutsu and the house may legally do at the capital, unlocking the final Influence band.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: Spine for the permit(s) that gate the capstone; the deeper petition for formal merit acknowledgement is the climactic spine beat. No purely-optional bloat here — but the protagonist's distance is non-negotiable.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Kanjo-bugyo and machi-bugyo offices and their clerk/yoriki layers are historically exact; commoners and provincial retainers reached them only through intermediaries. Strictly honours the ceiling: NO direct contact, no hatamoto status, no shogunal audience — the wall is the point. No folklore.

factions_zones.name: The Touring Inspector (junkenshi / metsuke survey) — the contested set-piece
factions_zones.theme: The single moment the abstract climb is JUDGED by a real human authority: a touring shogunal inspector whose periodic survey can validate or threaten everything the house has built — standing contested face to face (with the lord, not the protagonist).
factions_zones.first_encounter: Word travels down the kaido (via Konoe and the post-town network) that a junkenshi circuit will pass through the domain — one rumour, one looming date, one verb: prepare.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: 1) The inspection is announced as a distant threat/opportunity (it exists, it is coming). 2) Learn what it weighs — fields, ledgers, order, the meibutsu, the people's condition — turning the whole game's accumulated work into the answer to one exam. 3) Stage the domain's best face (the protagonist marshals what he's built; the rival house Tomita tries to outshine or undermine). 4) The inspection itself — a tense set-piece where the LORD and senior retainers face the inspector and the house's record is read aloud; the protagonist's work speaks, but he stands at the back. 5) Aftermath: a favourable report travels UP to Edo and becomes the documented service the capstone commendation is built on — or, if neglected, a recoverable dent that must be answered.

key_npcs.name: Junkenshi Toyama Saemon-no-jo
key_npcs.role: The touring shogunal inspector (巡見使) — the apex authority the protagonist ever comes near; surveys the domain, judges its order, before whom standing is contested; impartial, exacting, decidedly not impressed by mere wealth. (Fulfils the foundation's pre-named 'Touring Inspector' apex-authority slot.)

key_npcs.name: Inspector's secretary Mabuchi
key_npcs.role: The junkenshi's record-keeper who writes the report that travels to Edo; the reachable human whose pen captures the house's worth; can be honestly informed, never bought.

key_npcs.name: Rival House Tomita
key_npcs.role: (existing) Stages its own display to win the inspector's favour over the Kurosawa — the inter-house rivalry's climactic, status-contested confrontation, decided by substance vs show.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Adds the periodic INSPECTION set-piece: a scored review that converts the house's standing accumulation (fields reclaimed, ledgers clean, meibutsu renowned, people content) into a single high-stakes recognition event, plus a preparation sub-loop. The one place the upper-tier abstraction becomes a tense, dramatized, human judgement rather than a rising bar.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: The largest single positive Influence swing in the game when met well — and the canonical SOURCE of the documented service the Edo merit-commendation requires. Neglecting it triggers a scripted, recoverable dent, preserving the 'can stall/dent, never wipe' rule.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: Spine — the inspection is the dramatized capstone confrontation and the origin of the merit record. Its preparation depth scales with optional side-investment but the event itself is core.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Junkenshi circuits and metsuke surveillance of domains are historically exact mechanisms of contested standing. The protagonist stands behind the lord; recognition is read into a record, not personally bestowed. No folklore, no magic — institutional judgement honouring the ceiling absolutely.

risks: Ceiling erosion: T5's drama could tempt a beat where the protagonist personally impresses an Edo official or is summoned. GUARDRAIL: every official contact is hard-routed through the rusui Konoe or the lord; the protagonist never crosses a yashiki/bakufu threshold or meets the shogun. The emotional payoff must come from the GAP (famous in a city he'll never be received in), not from breaching it.

Async-loop tedium: the correspondence/road-day delays and slow creditworthiness/banzuke climbs risk feeling like dead waiting at the very end of the game. GUARDRAIL: keep multiple parallel slow-loops in flight (finance, wholesale, fame, attendance) so something always resolves; cadence-test headlessly so no loop ever goes fully cold.

Finance/economy abstraction drift: the Osaka→Edo rice-finance chain plus wholesale plus fame is three new abstract economies stacked at the capstone — high risk of city-builder/4X spreadsheet tedium the foundation explicitly warns against. GUARDRAIL: each stays diegetic and story-framed (a returned bill, a chart you're absent from, a clerk's letter), and is gated/paced so they reveal one at a time, fractally, not all at once.

Scope/number blowup: T5 is the order-of-magnitude-largest canvas with the most new factions; risks balance blowup and exceeding any v1 slice (foundation flags v1 as T1-2 only). GUARDRAIL: treat all of T5 as roadmap/post-v1; design the conduits so they can ship as a thin abstract 'holdings/influence board' (per the foundation's open question) before any full sub-area art.

Meibutsu under-specification: half these factions (tonya category, banzuke chart type, finance instrument flavour) hinge on WHICH product the house's meibutsu is, which is unchosen (silk? sake? paper? lacquer?). Designing too concretely before that is locked risks rework.

NPC-cluster overload at the finale: ~18 new named Edo NPCs arriving in the last tier could overwhelm. GUARDRAIL: fractal reveal (one contact per node first), heavy reuse of existing cast as proxies (Munenori, Naoyuki, Saburozaemon, Sokichi-echo, the pre-named Touring Inspector), and let the rusui Konoe be the single recurring through-line face.
open_questions: Which meibutsu does the house build (silk/sericulture, sake, paper, lacquer, a specialty rice)? This determines the tonya category, the banzuke chart, the finance flavour, and the cultural tastemaker — should be locked before T5 is authored in detail.

Pacing-lock (a flagged foundation question): do the side-mysteries (lost-child, origin) and the tier climb CLIMAX TOGETHER at T5/Edo, or resolve at T3-4 leaving T5 a quieter restoration victory lap? The inspector set-piece and capstone commendation are sized for a true climax, but could be dialled to a coda.

Macro-tier spatiality (flagged foundation question): does Edo get any sub-area map (yashiki rooms, Nihonbashi, Kuramae) or is it a purely abstract holdings/influence/correspondence board? This design supports either; recommend abstract-first given the ceiling (the protagonist isn't there).

Is the rusui-yaku Konoe a warm recurring ally (the human face of the capital) or a cool impartial functionary? The async-loop's emotional temperature depends on this; recommend warming him gradually as a small relationship arc to humanise the abstract tier.

How loud should the rival House Tomita's role be at the inspection — a genuine threat that can cost the house standing, or a foil that merely raises the stakes? Reconcile with the 'dent never wipe' rule and the partial-justice guardrail.

Does the documented merit commendation flow to the LORD only (purest ceiling reading) or include a footnoted notation of the steward's service (a softer, still-mediated nod)? Either honours 'no hatamoto status'; the second is more emotionally satisfying but edges closer to personal recognition — a tone call for the human.

Banzuke as literal mechanic vs flavour: is the meibutsu's chart-rank a real tracked number with thresholds (a satisfying capstone progress bar) or narrative colour? Recommend a real tracked rank as the tier-5 visible win condition, mirroring the house-rank ladder.

Big-number formatting (flagged foundation question) becomes acute here — the finance chain and Edo-scale economy are where koku/coin magnitudes peak; confirm whether abbreviated formatting is needed for T5.

facets.scope: Cross-cutting NETWORKS that span tiers (T1/T2 seeds → T3 region → T4 domain/castle-town → T5 Edo). These are recurring factions the player deepens as they climb the House Influence spine, not tier-locked clusters. Six networks: (1) Transport/Porter Guild logistics; (2) Religious/Pilgrimage order; (3) Trade Syndicate / merchant-finance; (4) Criminal Underworld / rice-quota-&-smuggling racket (the grounded antagonist web); (5) Artisan Masters' Lineage; (6) Scholarly/Medical network. All grounded, no magic, optional-where-appropriate, fractal-incremental, woven into the influence climb without ever blocking the spine. Built OUTWARD from the three locked starter factions (Estate ladder, Asagiri village web, Sawatari-juku origin support track) and their existing cast — nothing redesigned.
facets.summary: Six cross-tier networks, each one a thread the player picks up MINIMAL at a low tier (one contact, one node, one verb) and re-meets, larger, at every higher tier — the whole-game UI-reveal motion in miniature, repeated as you climb.
