Each is anchored on EXISTING canon so it builds outward rather than inventing a parallel world: the Porter Guild grows from Denbei/Zenroku (origin) to regional caravans to Osaka/Edo freight; the Pilgrimage order grows from Priest Ryoa's Asagiri shrine to regional temples to a named circuit; the Trade Syndicate grows from the village rice-broker and Sokichi the peddler to a castle-town kuramoto to an Edo finance house; the RACKET is the spine's grounded antagonist web, already half-built in canon (Magobei -> Yagoemon's doctored ledgers -> Tedai Kuroiwa -> Guildmaster Zenroku, enforced by Hanzaki), here extended upward to a domain magistrate and an Edo factor and downward into every other network as friction; the Artisan Lineage grows from Smith Gonta/Carpenter Risuke into a master-commission chain culminating in the house's famous meibutsu; the Scholarly/Medical network grows from estate physician Ranpo into a domain academy and Edo learning. FIVE of the six are optional accelerants that feed Influence and roughly halve time-to-tier without ever gating the spine; the RACKET is the one that is structurally woven into the spine (its quota machine is the same rotten root under the estate's debt, the village's grief, and the origin's caravan) yet is still defeated by evidence and economy, not required combat, and its side-mysteries never block the climb. Every belief/omen these networks carry (pilgrimage miracles, road-kami, the smugglers' kappa-ford) resolves to a human/natural cause; folklore stays light atmosphere. Each network deepens via a tier-gated step ladder where each new node is a story beat carrying its own Influence-banded unlock predicate. Two design notes I flag for the human (see open_questions): exact Influence weights and whether the upper-tier networks get sub-area maps vs. abstract holding boards are deferred balance/§4 calls; and the apex racket figures must stay 'untouchable / partial justice' to honour the honoured-ceiling thesis.

factions_zones.name: The Kaido Porters' Guild (the road network / logistics spine)
factions_zones.tier: cross-tier: seeds in Origin (Sawatari-juku, T3-gated) and the shared mountain Pass (T1-3); deepens T3 region -> T4 Osaka rice shipping -> T5 Edo sankin-kotai freight
factions_zones.theme: The roads as the circulatory system of the rise. Honest muscle, manifests, knots, and safe-passage — the literal means by which the Kurosawa name and goods travel outward. The protagonist's OWN past trade (he was a porter), turned from a wound (the caravan that 'killed' him) into the supply chain that carries the house upward.
factions_zones.first_encounter: MINIMAL: a single broken porter's-knot beat at the Stables/Woodlot (T1, Rank 2) — 'huh, you've hauled before' (the locked zero-bonus identity clue). The network proper opens with ONE contact: Master Denbei's goods-house in Sawatari-juku (the origin hub), met as a single re-engaged tie when memory + standing first let you travel the kaido (T3).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Step 1 (T3, one node): Denbei gives you ONE legitimate manifest and ONE pack-route (Sawatari-juku <-> the estate) — a single new producer-route, one verb (dispatch a load). Step 2: route reliability unlocks a second leg (region post-stations / tsugi-tsugi relay) and the first hired porter slot — gated on Influence band + a route run a set number of times. Step 3: friend Kanta and old guild mates recruit in as porter-allies (caravan throughput multiplier). Step 4 (T4): the guild's regional sub-master grants you a freight contract to the castle-town and onward to Osaka rice shipping; pack-trains scale to boats. Step 5 (T5): your goods ride the lord's sankin-kotai convoy to Edo — the same road that killed Tahei's caravan now carries the house's meibutsu to the capital. Each leg is a story beat: a flooded ford repaired, a sekisho pass earned, a relay station allied. Guildmaster Zenroku (who wrote Tahei off) sits across this network as its compromised top — see the Racket.

key_npcs.name: Master Denbei
key_npcs.role: Origin employer; first guild contact; supplies legitimate manifests, route know-how, and the porter's-knot's grounded origin (taught it — ZERO bonus). The honest face of the road.

key_npcs.name: Kanta
key_npcs.role: Childhood friend / fellow porter recruited as the first porter-ally; throughput multiplier and warmth anchor.

key_npcs.name: Naozane (the guild's office clerk in canon)
key_npcs.role: Reachable conscience inside the paperwork — manifests and short-weight evidence flow through him; bridges into the Racket.

key_npcs.name: Guildmaster Zenroku
key_npcs.role: The road network's compromised apex (origin antagonist) — wrote Tahei off to hide contraband/short-weighted cargo; later a reachable witness/culprit tying the road into the Racket.

key_npcs.name: A regional relay sub-master (new, T4)
key_npcs.role: Castle-town logistics gatekeeper who grants the Osaka/Edo freight contracts once standing is proven.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A logistics layer over the existing producer economy: pack-routes are recurring auto-transports that convert estate/origin surplus into delivered goods + Influence (a 'recognized delivery'); each new leg adds a route producer with cost/throughput scaling like the koku tiers. Hired porters are assignable idle helpers (like seconded village allies). Safe-passage (sekisho) is a soft gate eased by standing, not a wall. Route saturation/seasonality (snowed-in passes) gives the damper texture.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Trade ties are conversion-ledger lever (7): routes to post-town/origin and onward to the inter-han/Osaka/Edo market are the literal pipes that make region/domain/Edo expansion AFFORDABLE — they cut the cost of conquering each tier (part of the side-faction 'roughly halve time-to-tier'). The capstone delivery (meibutsu reaching Edo via sankin-kotai) is a headline T5 Influence beat.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: Optional accelerant. Skippable — a spine-only player ships nothing far and climbs slower/lonelier, never blocked. NOTE the origin track (which this extends) already guarantees at least one non-rep-gated beat so the thread never fully stalls.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Fully mundane: roads, knots, manifests, weather, checkpoints. The 'one-eyed mountain god of the pass' rumour resolves to Hanzaki the ronin enforcer + lethal fog-blind terrain; the caravan's 'curse' resolves to a neglected embankment failing. No road-kami ever mechanically acts.

factions_zones.name: The Pilgrimage Order (shrine -> temple circuit / folk-religion network)
factions_zones.tier: cross-tier: seeds at Asagiri's shrine/temple (T2, Village) under Priest Ryoa; deepens to regional temples (T3) -> a named multi-stop pilgrimage circuit + domain head-temple patronage (T4) -> an Edo temple's recognition / a celebrated offering at the capital (T5)
factions_zones.theme: Soft power and legitimacy through piety. In Edo Japan a house's standing was partly its visible devotion and patronage; restoring the Kurosawa name means restoring its place in the religious-social fabric. Folk-religion TEXTURE (Shinto shrine vs Buddhist temple, Bon, jizo, soul-calling) as believed-atmosphere, never mechanized miracle.
factions_zones.first_encounter: MINIMAL: the Asagiri shrine opens (T2) with ONE act — leaving a single offering / hearing the soul-calling rite from Priest Ryoa. One contact, one node, one verb. (His register of the vanished is also a lost-child clue — double duty, existing canon.)
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Step 1 (T2): tend/repair the local shrine; small village goodwill + the temple register opens as a lore node. Step 2: sponsor a modest local rite/festival (ties to Brewer Tokuemon's festival hub) — the estate's mon appears as a patron. Step 3 (T3): Ryoa introduces a regional temple on the road; a pilgrimage LEG opens (visit a stop = a story beat + small Influence as recognized piety). Step 4: the multi-stop circuit assembles node by node (each temple a fresh minimal arrival — fractal). Step 5 (T4): patronage of the domain head-temple buys legitimacy and a reishi/letter that vouches the house upward (a patron who vouches — conversion lever 6). Step 6 (T5): a celebrated dedication/offering at an Edo temple lifts the house's face at the capital (indirect, mediated — honours the ceiling).

key_npcs.name: Priest Ryoa
key_npcs.role: Asagiri shrine/temple keeper; first contact; runs the soul-calling rite and the register of the vanished (also hard evidence in the lost-child thread). Gates local piety and the pilgrimage seed.

key_npcs.name: A mendicant/pilgrim peddler (extend Peddler Sokichi)
key_npcs.role: Already the canon 'bridge' beyond the village and a source of yokai tales; here doubles as the figure who first walks you toward the regional circuit and carries word of the house's piety down the road.

key_npcs.name: A regional head-temple abbot (new, T4)
key_npcs.role: Domain-level religious gatekeeper whose patronage and written vouch confer legitimacy upward.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Patronage sub-loop: spend koku/coin/goods on rites, repairs, and offerings -> recognized piety -> Influence + reputation + occasional ally (lay supporters, festival labour). Pilgrimage stops are discrete milestone nodes (like origin ties in shape) — chunky one-time unlocks, each a sensory story beat. Festivals tie into the existing seasonal/festival scheduler.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Patronage & alliance is conversion lever (6): visible devotion + a head-temple's vouch are an authentic Edo route to legitimacy that 'numbers go up' can ride. Feeds Influence as RECOGNIZED standing (the band that gates the UI), and supplies upward-vouching patrons for the T4->T5 climb.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: Optional. A fully secular player skips all of it and is never blocked — only less legitimate/slower. Confirmed: no pilgrimage beat gates a tier.
factions_zones.grounded_note: GROUNDED is load-bearing here. No rite ever mechanically 'works' — the soul-calling brings comfort, not a soul; a 'miracle cure' at a temple resolves to Ranpo's medicine or coincidence; an omen resolves to weather/human action. Devotion buys human social standing, never supernatural power. Respectful Shinto/Buddhist distinction per the locked folklore decision; this is the home of the one capped residual-ambiguity beat (the boundary jizo offering) only as believed-atmosphere.

factions_zones.name: The Rice & Silver Syndicate (merchant-finance network)
factions_zones.tier: cross-tier: seeds at the Asagiri rice-broker/dry-goods shop (T2, Village) and Okimi's Sawatari-juku trading family (T3, Origin); deepens to a castle-town kuramoto / rice-warehouse financier (T4) -> an Edo merchant house / fudasashi-style factor (T5)
factions_zones.theme: Money is the lever that turns the strangling inherited debt into creditworthiness. The legitimate counter-weight to the criminal Racket: the same rice-and-silver economy can be run honestly (proper merchant finance, sealed contracts, a domain loan) instead of through quota-skimming and smuggling. Restructuring debt -> solvency -> credit is the financial spine of the rise.
factions_zones.first_encounter: MINIMAL: ONE contact — the village rice-broker / dry-goods shop (T2), where you first SELL surplus for coin (the existing per-shop patron meter). One node, one verb (sell). Sokichi the peddler and sister Okimi's family later extend it outward.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Step 1 (T2): build patron standing at the village broker — better prices, first forward-contract. Step 2 (T3): Okimi's trading family + Sokichi open inter-village/post-town trade and the first proper LOAN to refinance a slice of the usurious debt (clerk Tanomo's books thread). Step 3 (T4): a castle-town kuramoto (rice-warehouse financier) extends real merchant finance — restructure the strangling grandfather's debt into a domain/merchant loan; the house becomes CREDITWORTHY (a headline T4 beat in canon). Step 4: goyokin-style buy-in and a strategic marriage/alliance lift formal standing (conversion lever 6). Step 5 (T5): an Edo merchant/factor finances the meibutsu's entry into the capital's markets. Each financier is a fresh minimal arrival (one introduction, one instrument), unlocked step by step.

key_npcs.name: The Asagiri rice-broker / dry-goods merchant (extend the existing per-shop node)
key_npcs.role: First finance contact; forward-contracts and the on-ramp to credit.

key_npcs.name: Okimi
key_npcs.role: Tahei's sister married into a trading family (existing canon) — the origin-side trade-tie that routes goods and opens post-town finance.

key_npcs.name: Clerk Tanomo
key_npcs.role: Estate accountant (existing) — the in-house thread into debt restructuring; the player works the ledgers WITH him to convert liability into solvency.

key_npcs.name: A castle-town kuramoto / rice financier (new, T4)
key_npcs.role: Domain-level financier who restructures the inherited debt and makes the house creditworthy.

key_npcs.name: An Edo merchant factor (new, T5)
key_npcs.role: Finances the meibutsu's celebrated entry into Edo markets.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Finance sub-loops layered on the coin/koku economy: forward-contracts (lock a sell price, soften the market-saturation crash), loans (spend future yield to buy present capacity), debt-restructuring (convert a high-interest liability into a serviceable one — a real Influence beat). Per-merchant patron standing mirrors the village shop meters. Honest finance is the cheaper-but-slower alternative to the Racket's fast-but-corrosive money.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Debt restructuring is conversion lever (4) — liability into solvency into creditworthiness — and trade ties is lever (7). This network is how the house AFFORDS the upper tiers and how 'the books now show...' converts grind into recognized Influence. A direct enabler of the T4 creditworthiness gate.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: Mostly optional accelerant, with ONE spine-adjacent obligation: the inherited debt must be serviced/restructured to reach T4 solvency — but that can be done minimally through the estate's own grind + Tanomo; the broader Syndicate just makes it far faster and cheaper. The wider network never blocks the spine.
factions_zones.grounded_note: No magic; pure Edo commercial practice (kuramoto, forward rice contracts, goyokin, merchant loans). The only 'mystery' texture is which money is clean — the honest Syndicate vs the Racket's dirty silver — and that resolves to documents and people, not omens.

factions_zones.name: The Rice-Quota & Smuggling Racket (the grounded antagonist web)
factions_zones.tier: cross-tier: roots in T2 (Foreman Magobei skimming; Yagoemon's doctored village ledgers) -> T3 (Tedai Kuroiwa's quota cover-up; Guildmaster Zenroku's contraband caravans; Hanzaki enforcing the pass) -> T4 (the deputy magistrate / daikan who profits) -> T5 (an Edo factor laundering the silver). The 'one rotten root' under the spine, the village grief, and the origin.
factions_zones.theme: The grounded human antagonist of the whole saga — not a monster but a QUOTA MACHINE: officials and middlemen skimming tax-rice and moving smuggled silver, dressing negligence as fate. The same machine nearly sold Tama, drowned Kuzuhara (the neglected embankment), and wrote off Tahei's caravan. Defeated by EVIDENCE and economic daylight, not by becoming a swordsman; justice is deliberately PARTIAL (the reachable answer; the powerful largely escape).
factions_zones.first_encounter: MINIMAL: ONE thread — Foreman Magobei, 'the tanuki of the rumours board' (T2), skimming village rice. You meet the racket as a single petty-theft mystery (the existing first hard thread), not as a conspiracy. One contact, one suspicious detail (a doctored measuring-box).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Step 1 (T2): expose Magobei's skim -> a strand leads to Yagoemon's doctored ledgers (the reachable, frightened middleman — existing canon). Step 2 (T3): the strand climbs to Tedai Kuroiwa (the patient, dangerous architect) and Zenroku's short-weighted/contraband caravans; the smugglers' 'kappa' sinking-spot at the ford yields recoverable evidence/drowned-witness clues. Step 3: Hanzaki appears as the enforcer guarding the pass (the trained-not-gifted talent-foil — survived by endurance, never out-fought). Step 4 (T4): the trail reaches the daikan/deputy magistrate who profits; an osso-style petition is mounted on documented proof (priest's register + Naozane's testimony + a porter-witness + the measuring-box). Step 5 (T5): the silver's Edo laundering factor is glimpsed but UNTOUCHABLE. Each rung is discovered open-endedly through labour/travel/rising trust (a suggestion + a story, never A->B->C). The racket also THREADS into the other five networks as friction (see ties).

key_npcs.name: Foreman Magobei
key_npcs.role: Entry rung — petty skimming foreman; reachable, human, the 'tanuki'.

key_npcs.name: Headman Yagoemon
key_npcs.role: The doctored-ledger middleman (existing double-duty: village quest-giver AND humanly-reachable culprit; never a monster).

key_npcs.name: Tedai Kuroiwa
key_npcs.role: Primary human antagonist of the regional tiers — architect of the cover-up; opposes the rise because a rising house asks questions. Defeated by evidence, not violence.

key_npcs.name: Guildmaster Zenroku
key_npcs.role: Moves the contraband; wrote Tahei off (links the racket into the Porter Guild).

key_npcs.name: Hanzaki
key_npcs.role: Scarred ronin enforcer ('one-eyed mountain god'); the talent-gone-rotten mirror — trained/brutal, never innate; the rare dangerous combat beat, survived by labour-built conditioning.

key_npcs.name: Clerk Naozane
key_npcs.role: Guilt-sick junior clerk — the crack in the cover-up; supplies documentary proof.

key_npcs.name: The deputy magistrate / daikan (new apex, T4)
key_npcs.role: Profits from the machine; the figure the osso petition contests; largely escapes (honoured ceiling).

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Not a producer network — an EVIDENCE/petition meta-track woven through the others: clues are collected via ordinary work, travel, and trust across tiers (a manifest scrap from the Porter Guild, a temple register entry from the Pilgrimage order, a doctored measuring-box from the Syndicate's books, a porter-witness from the origin). Exposing a rung can DENT the racket's drag on your economy (it skims/saturates against you) and yields a recoverable, scripted Influence beat (a won petition = conversion lever 5, distinguished service). The rare Hanzaki fight uses the idle-combat suite, gated behind conditioning.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Distinguished service to the domain is conversion lever (5): a resolved case/won petition is a headline Influence beat. CRITICAL inversion: while active, the racket is the chief diegetic DRAG on the climb (extractive quotas, market manipulation, blocked passage) — dismantling reachable rungs unlocks cheaper trade, safer roads, cleaner finance, so it feeds the spine by REMOVING friction as much as by adding Influence.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: STRUCTURALLY woven into the spine (it is the rotten root the whole story turns on) — BUT its side-mysteries (lost-child, full caravan truth) are optional and never block the climb. You can advance Influence and tiers by economic means alone; pursuing the racket is a parallel justice track that enriches and unblocks but is never a hard gate. Honours 'side content never blocks the spine' while keeping the antagonist load-bearing.
factions_zones.grounded_note: The keystone of NO-MAGIC: every folk-belief the racket hides behind resolves one-to-one to it — the kappa ford = undertow + the smugglers' weighted-net sinking-spot; the tanuki stealing rice = Magobei's skim; the cursed/unlucky village = diverted relief-rice and a neglected embankment; kamikakushi itself = people who fled debt/violence or died on a lethal road. Debunked with dawning DREAD, never a Scooby-Doo unmasking. Justice stays partial; the apex escapes.

factions_zones.name: The Artisan Masters' Lineage (apprenticeship -> master commission -> meibutsu)
factions_zones.tier: cross-tier: seeds with Smith Gonta, Carpenter Risuke, Weaver Onatsu, Dyer (T2, Village artisans' guild); deepens to a regional master / named workshop (T3) -> domain-product commissions and a castle-town craft reputation (T4) -> the meibutsu celebrated in Edo (T5)
factions_zones.theme: Mastery passed hand to hand. The grind made social — the protagonist organizes (and the artisans perfect) a signature regional PRODUCT (silk/textile, sake, lacquer, or fine paper) that becomes the house's claim to fame. 'The hero gets better at what he does' externalized: a lineage of craft that climbs from village tools to a domain meibutsu.
factions_zones.first_encounter: MINIMAL: ONE artisan, ONE recipe — Smith Gonta gates basic metalcraft (a single tool repaired/forged), valuing shown effort over flash (existing canon). One contact, one verb (craft). The craft-guild standing then opens further nodes.
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Step 1 (T2): patron/apprentice standing with Gonta unlocks the first craft chain (tools, then spearheads). Step 2: the artisans' guild meter opens Risuke (building), Onatsu (cloth), the dyer, the charcoal grounds — each a fresh minimal node with its own component tier. Step 3 (T3): a regional MASTER takes a commission — the first proto-industry product (sericulture/silk, sake, or textile as a village by-employment the estate organizes and takes a cut of). Step 4 (T4): the product earns a castle-town reputation and reaches Osaka (a renowned domain product). Step 5 (T5): the meibutsu is celebrated in Edo's markets (the spine's headline T5 outcome). Apprenticeship side-quests (each open-ended, not a checklist) deepen specific lineages.

key_npcs.name: Smith Gonta
key_npcs.role: First artisan contact; gates metalcraft; effort-over-flash ethos.

key_npcs.name: Carpenter Risuke
key_npcs.role: Gates building/construction used to rebuild and expand the estate (ties craft to the physical tier reveal).

key_npcs.name: Weaver Onatsu
key_npcs.role: Cloth/textile — ties into Lady Chiyo's household economy and origin-town routes; a lead candidate for the meibutsu lineage.

key_npcs.name: Brewer Tokuemon
key_npcs.role: Sake — proto-industry candidate and festival/social hub (cross-links to the Pilgrimage festivals).

key_npcs.name: A regional craft master (new, T3)
key_npcs.role: Takes the first master commission; the lineage's upward rung toward a domain product.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: Craft chains over the existing crafting/component economy: recipes and component tiers gated by per-artisan patron standing (mirrors village shop meters); a proto-industry sub-loop (organize a by-employment -> produce a refined good -> take a cut) that scales into the meibutsu. High standing softens the market-saturation crash on the signature good. Carpenter-built construction physically unlocks estate/region nodes.
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Cash crops & proto-industry is conversion lever (2): building toward a famous domain product / meibutsu is one of the most direct Influence engines and the literal substance celebrated at Edo in the endgame. River/road works (lever 3) cross-link via Risuke's construction. Feeds the spine by manufacturing the very thing the upper tiers recognize.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: Optional in its depth, but its OUTPUT (a tradeable surplus / a signature product) is the natural fuel for the T3+ economy. A player can climb on raw rice alone (slower); the lineage makes the climb richer and supplies the meibutsu that crowns it. Never a hard gate; apprenticeship quests are pure side content.
factions_zones.grounded_note: Wholly grounded — real Edo by-employments and craft economics (sericulture, brewing, weaving, lacquer, papermaking). No magic forging, no enchanted goods; 'masterwork' is skill + time + good materials. The only legend-texture (a 'blessed' brew, a 'lucky' cloth) is marketing/belief that resolves to quality and reputation.

factions_zones.name: The Scholars' & Physicians' Network (Ranpo -> domain academy -> Edo learning)
factions_zones.tier: cross-tier: seeds with estate physician Ranpo (T1) and Obaa Sato's herb-lore (T2); deepens to a domain academy / han school and a town physician (T3-T4) -> Edo learning / rangaku-adjacent scholarship and a recognized text or appointment (T5)
factions_zones.theme: Knowledge as the quiet third kind of power, beside muscle (Porter Guild) and money (Syndicate). Practical learning — medicine, agronomy, accounting, surveying, water-engineering — that materially improves yields, heals the workforce, and earns the house intellectual legitimacy. The rational counter-voice to folklore: it NAMES symptoms not visions, embankment failures not curses.
factions_zones.first_encounter: MINIMAL: ONE contact, ONE service — Ranpo dresses the healing scalp wound at the open (T1), naming symptoms not visions, gating healing/medicine, flatly disbelieving the kami story. One node, one verb (be treated / later, gather a remedy).
factions_zones.incremental_unlock: Step 1 (T1-T2): Ranpo gates the healing/medicine sub-system; Obaa Sato teaches foraging/herb-craft (folk-medicine texture, grounded). Step 2 (T2-T3): practical knowledge applied — agronomy improves paddy yield, surveying enables the shinden reclamation, water-engineering plans the embankment (cross-links to the river-works lever and the Kuzuhara backstory resolution). Step 3 (T3-T4): a domain academy / han school and a town physician open as a node — formal learning confers legitimacy and better techniques; a scholar vouches the house upward. Step 4 (T5): a recognized treatise on the house's agronomy/water-works, or a rare scholarly appointment, lifts the house's name at the capital (indirect, mediated). Each step is a fresh minimal arrival (one teacher, one technique).

key_npcs.name: Ranpo
key_npcs.role: Estate physician; first contact; gates healing/medicine; the rational, kami-disbelieving voice; grounds the mundane amnesia.

key_npcs.name: Obaa Sato
key_npcs.role: Village herbalist/wise-woman (existing) — folk-medicine and the sympathetic folklore-keeper who KNOWS the legend is coping; bridges folk-lore and practical herb-craft.

key_npcs.name: Dowager Toku
key_npcs.role: Living backstory keeper (existing) — her memory of the grandfather's failed flood-venture is the knowledge that motivates getting the water-engineering RIGHT this time.

key_npcs.name: A domain academy scholar / han-school master (new, T3-T4)
key_npcs.role: Gates formal learning, better agronomy/engineering, and an upward vouch.

key_npcs.name: An Edo scholar / publisher (new, T5)
key_npcs.role: Recognizes a treatise or grants a rare scholarly appointment — the network's capstone, mediated.

factions_zones.systems_and_economy: A research/technique sub-loop: invest time/coin into learning (agronomy, surveying, medicine, accounting) -> permanent technique upgrades that improve producer yields, heal/condition the workforce faster, and unlock the engineering needed for river/road works. Medicine sub-system reduces downtime (a soft conditioning/recovery aid — NOT a combat bonus, honouring the no-hidden-edge rule). Knowledge nodes are discrete milestones (chunky, like origin ties).
factions_zones.ties_to_influence: Cross-links to river & road works (lever 3 — the surveying/engineering that secures the embankment and earns official credit, also resolving the backstory) and distinguished service (lever 5 — a treatise or famine-relief technique recognized by the domain). Feeds Influence by making every OTHER lever more efficient (better yields, healthier workers, sound engineering) and by buying intellectual legitimacy for the T4->T5 climb.
factions_zones.optional_or_spine: Optional accelerant. A player can climb without ever investing in learning (slower, less efficient, more downtime). The only near-spine touchpoint is that sound engineering makes the river-works/embankment beats safer and richer — but those remain achievable minimally. Never blocks the spine.
factions_zones.grounded_note: The explicit ANTI-magic anchor of the cast: Ranpo names trauma not spirits; agronomy/engineering replace omen-reading; a 'cursed harvest' resolves to blight/weather and is fixed by technique. Folk-medicine (Obaa Sato) is respected as real herb-craft + placebo-comfort, never mechanized miracle. Honours the locked grounded/no-magic and mediocre-start (knowledge is grind-built access, not a gift).

risks: Six cross-cutting networks ON TOP of three factions and five tiers risks system/content explosion and balance blowup across five order-of-magnitude tiers (already flagged in PRD 1.13). Mitigation: each network must stay fractal-minimal at first contact (one node/verb) and most depth should land at T3+ where the canvas is bigger; the v1 vertical slice (T1-2) should expose only the SEEDS of each network, not full chains.

Risk of these reading as 'one Influence bar painted six more colours.' Mitigation: keep distinct SHAPES (Porter Guild = route producers; Pilgrimage/Scholarly = discrete milestone nodes; Syndicate/Artisan = per-node patron meters like the village web; Racket = an evidence/petition meta-track, not a producer) — mirror the existing three-shapes discipline.

The Racket being 'structurally woven into the spine' could violate 'side content never blocks the spine' if its mysteries become required. Mitigation enforced in the design: tier progress is always achievable by economic means alone; the racket is friction-removal + optional justice, never a hard gate.

Partial-justice / honoured-ceiling could be undercut if the player can fully dismantle the racket (touch the daikan / Edo factor). The apex must stay untouchable per the locked thesis — needs explicit gating so the powerful escape.

Folklore-as-light-flavour could creep into mechanized miracle via the Pilgrimage order (rites that 'work') or the Scholarly network's folk-medicine. Mitigation: no rite/remedy ever grants supernatural effect; piety/medicine buy human standing and reduce downtime only.

No-hidden-edge risk: the medicine sub-system (recovery aid) and porter-knot must confer ZERO mechanical combat/stat gift — only access/efficiency. Must be enforced in code per the dream rule and mediocre-start ADR.

Upper-tier spatiality is unresolved (PRD open question): do these networks get sub-area maps or abstract holding/influence boards? Designing six full sub-maps for T4/T5 would blow scope; recommend abstract boards above T3.
open_questions: Influence weights / conversion-ledger values for each network's beats (a delivered freight contract vs a restructured debt vs a won petition vs a meibutsu reaching Edo) are deferred to the balance facet / PRD 4. What relative weight should each cross-network beat carry?

Do the upper-tier networks (T4/T5) get full sub-area maps, or abstract holdings/influence boards over a thinner location set? (PRD open question 'macro-tier spatiality' — recommend abstract above T3 to control scope.)

Which single product is the canonical meibutsu the Artisan Lineage builds toward (silk/sericulture vs sake vs textile vs lacquer/paper)? This pins the Artisan, Syndicate, and Porter networks' endgame payload.

How far up does the player's evidence reach in the Racket before the honoured ceiling stops them — does the osso petition touch the daikan (T4) and merely glimpse the Edo factor (T5), and whose neck is on the line (protagonist, ally, or a gimin-style martyr)? (Reconcile with PRD partial-justice open question.)

Should any cross-network beat be allowed to DENT Influence (a failed petition, a defaulted loan, a smuggling scandal touching the house) as scripted, recoverable tension — or do networks only ever add? (Ties to the 'decay vs stall' open question.)

For the side-faction multiplier budget ('weaving both side factions roughly halves time-to-tier'), how is that ~50% acceleration apportioned across SIX networks so they stack sensibly without trivializing the climb?

v1 scope: which network SEEDS appear in the T1-2 vertical slice (recommend: Artisan via Gonta, Syndicate via the rice-broker, Pilgrimage via Ryoa, Scholarly via Ranpo, Racket via Magobei) and which (Porter Guild) stay stubbed until T3 with the origin?
